name: getNodes()
description: ReactFlowInstance method returning the current array of nodes. Snapshot read (does not subscribe/re-render).
terminal_note: Pure data accessor over the node list; trivial in C as a getter over the node array.
evidence: flow_nodes(f) returns the node array, flow_node_count(f) its length — src/flow_model.h:47,561,563. Snapshot read; no React subscription needed (renderer reads the store each frame via flow_present).

name: setNodes(payload)
description: Sets the nodes array by overwriting with an array or by passing an updater function (nodes => nodes). In a controlled flow it triggers onNodesChange.
terminal_note: Replace/update the node collection then mark for redraw; updater-fn form maps to a callback over the slice.
evidence: No bulk array-replace setter; spec §1 deliberately collapses the controlled-state/applyNodeChanges contract into direct per-node mutators (flow_add_node/flow_move_node/flow_remove_node, src/flow_model.h:453,462,104). A whole-array overwrite + onNodesChange trigger is not built — grep 'flow_set_nodes' src/ → no hits.

name: addNodes(payload)
description: Adds one node or an array of nodes to the existing nodes. Triggers onNodesChange in controlled flows.
terminal_note: Append to node array; pure data mutation.
evidence: flow_add_node(f,type,pos,data) appends one node, assigns a stable int id, measures it — src/flow_model.h:453-458. Single-node form; multi-node is a loop. Records an undo command (the C analog of the change-descriptor).

name: getNode(id)
description: Returns a single node by id, or undefined if not found.
terminal_note: Hash/linear lookup by id; pure data op.
evidence: flow_node *flow_get_node(flow_t*,int id) — linear lookup, returns NULL if absent. src/flow_model.h:43,559.

name: getInternalNode(id)
description: Returns the internal representation of a node by id (InternalNode), which includes measured size and internals.positionAbsolute (absolute position resolved through parent groups).
terminal_note: Lookup returning a node plus computed absolute-position/measured fields; all integer cell math in a TUI.
evidence: flow_get_node returns the node (with cached w/h from measure, src/flow_model.h:448-450); flow_node_abs(f,n) resolves position absolute through the parent chain (src/flow_model.h:49,565-570) and flow_node_rect_abs returns the absolute rect (571). The InternalNode's measured-size + positionAbsolute fields are exactly these.

name: getEdges()
description: Returns the current array of edges. Snapshot read.
terminal_note: Pure data accessor over the edge list.
evidence: flow_edges(f) returns the edge array, flow_edge_count(f) its length — src/flow_model.h:48,562,564.

name: setEdges(payload)
description: Sets the edges array by overwriting or via an updater function. Triggers onEdgesChange in controlled flows.
terminal_note: Replace/update edge collection then redraw.
evidence: No bulk edge-array replace setter; spec §1 collapses the controlled applyEdgeChanges contract into direct mutators (flow_add_edge/flow_remove_edge, src/flow_model.h:543,105). grep 'flow_set_edges' src/ → no hits.

name: addEdges(payload)
description: Adds one edge or an array of edges to the existing edges. Triggers onEdgesChange in controlled flows.
terminal_note: Append to edge array; pure data mutation.
evidence: flow_add_edge(f,src,dst,sh,th) appends one edge with addEdge-style validation (rejects self-edge, missing endpoints, duplicate source/target/handles) — src/flow_model.h:543-557. Single-edge form; records undo.

name: getEdge(id)
description: Returns a single edge by id, or undefined.
terminal_note: Lookup by id; pure data op.
evidence: flow_edge *flow_get_edge(flow_t*,int id) — linear lookup, NULL if absent. src/flow_model.h:44,560.

name: updateNode(id, nodeUpdate, options?)
description: Updates a node by id. nodeUpdate is a partial Node object or an updater function (node => partial). By default the partial is merged into the node; passing { replace: true } replaces the node object entirely.
terminal_note: Merge-or-replace fields of a node struct; replace flag toggles shallow-merge vs overwrite.
evidence: Partial-field updates exist as targeted mutators: flow_move_node (pos, src/flow_model.h:462), flow_set_parent (parent, 488). flow_get_node returns a mutable flow_node* so callers can edit fields directly. There is no single merge/replace updateNode entry-point, but the per-field mutator path (the spec's deliberate design, §4) covers the durable fields; partial for the engine-managed fields.

name: updateNodeData(id, dataUpdate, options?)
description: Updates only a node's data object. dataUpdate is a partial data record or an updater function (node => partial). By default merged with existing data (partial updates); { replace: true } replaces the data object. Core primitive for computing-flows / data propagation.
terminal_note: Merge/replace a node's user-data blob; in C this is updating an opaque data pointer/struct per node.
evidence: node->data is an opaque void* set only at flow_add_node (src/flow_model.h:457); no merge/replace data-update function and no change notification. grep 'flow_update_node_data' src/ → 0 hits. App mutates n->data directly via flow_get_node, but the reactive merge/propagate primitive is unbuilt.

name: updateEdge(id, edgeUpdate, options?)
description: Updates an edge by id with a partial Edge or updater function; merges by default, { replace: true } overwrites.
terminal_note: Merge/replace edge fields; pure data op.
evidence: Targeted edge-field mutators exist: flow_reconnect_edge (repoint source/target endpoint + handle, src/flow_model.h:106,740) and flow_set_edge_label (107,767). flow_get_edge returns a mutable flow_edge* for direct field edits. No single merge/replace updateEdge entry-point, but the per-field path covers durable edge fields.

name: updateEdgeData(id, dataUpdate, options?)
description: Updates only an edge's data object; partial merge by default, { replace: true } overwrites the data object.
terminal_note: Merge/replace an edge's user-data blob.
evidence: flow_edge has an opaque void* data field but no setter/merge function and serialize explicitly does NOT persist edge data (workpackages §serialize note B). grep 'flow_update_edge_data' src/ → 0 hits. Unbuilt.

name: deleteElements({ nodes, edges })
description: Deletes the given nodes and/or edges (by id objects). Returns a Promise resolving to { deletedNodes, deletedEdges } (cascade-deletes connected edges and respects deletability). Async because deletion goes through the change pipeline.
terminal_note: Remove nodes/edges and cascade-remove connected edges; the Promise is just for ordering — a synchronous return in C works.
evidence: flow_remove_node cascades incident edges AND child nodes recursively and fires on_nodes_delete with the removed id set (src/flow_model.h:104,789-804); flow_remove_edge removes one edge (105); flow_delete_selection removes the whole selection (118, 835-842). Synchronous, no Promise — exactly the terminal_note's expectation. Per-id rather than a single batched-object call.

name: getIntersectingNodes(node, partially?, nodes?)
description: Returns nodes intersecting a given node, a node id object, or an arbitrary Rect. partially=true includes partial overlaps (default requires full containment); optional nodes array restricts the search set.
terminal_note: Axis-aligned rectangle overlap test in flow coordinates; works directly on cell-grid bounding boxes.
evidence: Only the primitive flow_rect_intersects exists (src/flow_geom.h:10,30) and flow_select_in_rect uses it to SELECT nodes in a rect (src/flow_model.h:100,702-716); there is no node-vs-node intersection QUERY returning the overlapping set. grep 'flow_intersecting' src/ → 0 hits. The AABB primitive is present but the query is unbuilt.

name: isNodeIntersecting(node, area, partially?)
description: Boolean test of whether a node/node-id/Rect intersects a given Rect area. partially=true allows partial overlap.
terminal_note: Single AABB overlap predicate; integer cell math.
evidence: flow_rect_intersects(a,b) is the AABB overlap predicate (edge-touch counts) — src/flow_geom.h:10,30. Combined with flow_node_rect_abs (src/flow_model.h:571) this is exactly isNodeIntersecting against a Rect; the boolean predicate over a node's absolute rect is directly available.

name: getNodesBounds(nodes)
description: Instance method (also exported as a standalone util) returning the Rect bounding box enclosing the given nodes (accepts node ids, Node objects, or InternalNodes). Supports a nodeOrigin param ([0,0] top-left vs [0.5,0.5] center) in the util form. Formerly getRectOfNodes. Pairs with getViewportForBounds for fit calculations.
terminal_note: Min/max over node rects → bounding box; pure integer geometry in a TUI.
evidence: flow_bounds(f) returns the min/max bounding rect over node absolute rects — src/flow_model.h:66,572. Delta vs xyflow: it covers ALL nodes only (no subset-array argument and no nodeOrigin top-left/center param); it's used by flow_fit_view (862-868). The core bounding-box computation is built; the subset/origin parameters are not.

name: getHandleConnections({ type, nodeId, id? })
description: Returns all connections (HandleConnection[]) attached to a specific handle of a node. type is 'source' or 'target'; id selects among multiple same-type handles.
terminal_note: Filter the edge list by node+handle; pure graph query (handles modeled as named ports).
evidence: Handles are modeled (flow_handle, source_handle[16]/target_handle[16] on flow_edge) and edges are iterable via flow_edges, but there is no query function filtering edges by node+handle. grep 'flow_handle_connections' src/ → 0 hits. Would require a new filter over the edge list.

name: getNodeConnections({ type?, handleId?, nodeId })
description: Returns all connections (NodeConnection[]) to/from a node, optionally filtered by handle type and handle id. More general than getHandleConnections.
terminal_note: Filter edges incident to a node, optionally by port; pure graph query.
evidence: No incident-edge query function; flow_remove_node internally walks edges to cascade (src/flow_model.h:789ff) but exposes no public NodeConnection[] query. grep 'flow_node_connections' src/ → 0 hits. Pure graph query, not yet built.

name: toObject()
description: Serializes the flow to a ReactFlowJsonObject { nodes, edges, viewport } — a JSON-compatible snapshot for saving to a database or file and loading later.
terminal_note: Serialize graph + viewport to a plain struct; JSON or any encoding in C.
evidence: flow_save(f,path) serializes nodes (id/type/pos/parent + vtable data), edges (source/target/handles/type/label), and the float viewport (ox/oy/zoom) to JSON — src/flow_json.h:40-277. Delta: it writes directly to a file path rather than returning an in-memory object/struct; the same {nodes,edges,viewport} content is produced. No detached object-return form.

name: ReactFlowJsonObject shape (nodes/edges/viewport)
description: The JSON-compatible representation of a flow: { nodes: NodeType[], edges: EdgeType[], viewport: Viewport }. Produced by toObject(); the canonical persistence format.
terminal_note: Plain data container of three fields; maps to a serializable C struct.
evidence: The on-disk JSON format is exactly {version, viewport:{ox,oy,zoom}, nodes:[...], edges:[...]} — written by flow_save (src/flow_json.h, format documented in workpackages §serialize lines 277-281). Snapshot-tested via tests/snapshots/json_basic.txt. The three-field shape is realized as the serialization format rather than an in-memory typed struct.

name: Save & restore pattern (no restore() method)
description: Restoration is manual: save with toObject()->JSON->storage; restore by JSON.parse then setNodes(flow.nodes), setEdges(flow.edges), setViewport(flow.viewport). React Flow 12 has NO instance restore() method — the app re-applies state itself. The save-and-restore example also re-generates node ids on add.
terminal_note: Deserialize struct and re-apply graph+viewport setters; straightforward load routine in C.
evidence: flow_load(f,path) resets the graph (flow__graph_reset) then rebuilds nodes/edges, restores parent + viewport float zoom, re-measures, and bumps nextid/nexteid past the loaded ids — src/flow_json.h:279ff, src/flow_model.h:414-418. Unlike React it IS a one-call restore (flow owns one store), which is strictly simpler than the manual re-apply pattern. Round-trip tested in tests/test_json.c.

name: getViewport()
description: Returns the current Viewport { x, y, zoom } — the pan offset and zoom of the flow's independent coordinate system.
terminal_note: Repo ships real float zoom (commit c721608); pan (ox,oy) + float zoom are both live, so this is a full data accessor, not a fixed-zoom stub.
evidence: flow_view_get(f) returns the flow_viewport {float ox,oy,zoom} — src/flow_model.h:71,643. Real float zoom shipped (src/flow_view.h, commit c721608), so x/y/zoom are all meaningful.

name: setViewport(viewport, options?)
description: Sets the current viewport { x, y, zoom }. Options support animated transition: duration, ease (t=>t), interpolate ('smooth'|'linear'). Returns Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: Pan via flow_pan and zoom via flow_set_zoom exist; the eased/animated transition is out of scope per spec §1 (no sub-cell tweens). A direct {ox,oy,zoom} assignment helper is the missing piece.
evidence: The viewport is fully settable via flow_pan (src/flow_model.h:640) for offset and flow_set_zoom (src/flow_view.h:17) for zoom; flow_load also writes f->view directly. There is no single setViewport({x,y,zoom}) convenience and no animated transition (spec §1 cuts eased tweens), but every viewport field is set through existing functions — the parts are built, the one-call wrapper + animation are not.

name: setCenter(x, y, options?)
description: Centers the viewport on a flow position (x,y). Options: zoom target and animation duration. Returns Promise<boolean>. Example focuses a node by centering on its measured center.
terminal_note: Compute pan offset so a flow point sits at viewport center; doable in cells with real zoom, animation aside.
evidence: No center-on-point function. grep 'flow_set_center' src/ → 0 hits. flow_fit_view centers all-node bounds (src/flow_model.h:862) but there is no center-on-arbitrary-(x,y); not in spec or pending packages, so a future candidate.

name: fitView(options?)
description: Pans/zooms so all nodes (or a subset) fit in the viewport. Options: padding, includeHiddenNodes, minZoom, maxZoom, duration, ease, interpolate, and nodes (subset to fit; defaults to all). Returns Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: Compute bounds then a zoom+pan to enclose nodes; real-scale zoom makes this a true fit, not just scroll-to-fit.
evidence: flow_fit_view(f,margin) computes flow_bounds then picks a zoom (clamped to [zmin,zmax]) + pan so all nodes fit with `margin` cells padding — src/flow_model.h:120,862-868; bound to key 'f' (904). Delta: padding=margin and min/max via zmin/zmax are supported, but no node-subset argument and no animation (spec §1).

name: fitBounds(bounds, options?)
description: Fits the viewport to a given Rect. Options include padding and animation duration. Returns Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: Zoom/pan to enclose an explicit rectangle; the fit math exists inside flow_fit_view but is not exposed for an arbitrary rect.
evidence: No fit-to-arbitrary-rect function; flow_fit_view fits flow_bounds (all nodes) only — src/flow_model.h:862. grep 'flow_fit_bounds' src/ → 0 hits. The getViewportForBounds math is inlined in flow_fit_view but not parameterized by an explicit Rect; not in spec or pending packages.

name: zoomIn(options?) / zoomOut(options?)
description: Zoom in (x1.2) or out (/1.2) by one step. Options: duration, ease, interpolate for animation. Each returns Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: Repo implements real-scale zoom steps (FLOW_ZOOM_STEP), pointer-centered; animation aside, this is a genuine zoom, not a no-op.
evidence: flow_zoom_in(f,center) multiplies zoom by FLOW_ZOOM_STEP, flow_zoom_out divides — both pointer-centered via flow_set_zoom — src/flow_view.h:3-4,30-31. Bound to +/=/-/_ in flow_feed. No animated tween (spec §1) but the step zoom is real.

name: zoomTo(zoomLevel, options?)
description: Sets an absolute zoom level. Options: duration, ease, interpolate. Returns Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: Absolute zoom set; real float zoom means this is a genuine absolute set, not a discrete stub.
evidence: flow_set_zoom(f, zoom, screen_center) sets an absolute float zoom level, clamped to [zmin,zmax], keeping the cell under screen_center fixed — src/flow_view.h:2,17. That IS zoomTo (pointer-anchored); the name differs and there is no animation, but absolute-zoom-set is built.

name: getZoom()
description: Returns the current zoom level as a number.
terminal_note: Reads the zoom scalar; the port models real float zoom so the value is meaningful, not a constant 1.
evidence: float flow_zoom(flow_t*) returns f->view.zoom — src/flow_view.h:5,10. Real float zoom (commit c721608), so the value varies.

name: screenToFlowPosition(clientPos, options?)
description: Converts a screen/pixel position to a flow-coordinate position, accounting for pan+zoom. Options: snapToGrid, snapGrid. Key for drag-and-drop from an external sidebar onto the canvas.
terminal_note: Maps terminal cell coords -> world coords via inverse of pan/zoom; integer-cell positions make snap automatic.
evidence: flow_to_world(f,screen) = flow_unproject(view,screen): (screen - (ox,oy)) / zoom rounded to a cell — src/flow_model.h:70,642, spec §3. Snap-to-grid is automatic because world positions are integer cells. This is screenToFlowPosition.

name: flowToScreenPosition(flowPos)
description: Converts a flow-coordinate position to a screen/pixel position (inverse of screenToFlowPosition).
terminal_note: Maps world coords -> terminal cell coords (apply pan + zoom); used for hit-testing/drawing.
evidence: flow_to_screen(f,world_abs) = flow_project(view, world): round(world*zoom + (ox,oy)) — src/flow_model.h:69,641, spec §3. Used throughout render/hit-test. This is flowToScreenPosition.

name: viewportInitialized flag
description: Boolean on the instance indicating the viewport is mounted and ready for zoom/pan operations; guard before calling viewport methods.
terminal_note: A readiness boolean; trivial state flag in C set once the render surface is sized.
evidence: flow_new takes cols/rows so the viewport is sized at construction and is always ready; there is a `running` flag (src/flow_model.h:207) but no viewport-readiness boolean and no need for one (no async mount). grep 'viewportInitialized' / 'view_ready' src/ → 0 hits. A no-op-equivalent flag could be added but isn't; not in spec.

name: useReactFlow() hook
description: Returns the ReactFlowInstance for programmatic access to all methods (update nodes/edges, manipulate viewport, query state). Does NOT cause re-render on state change (unlike useNodes/useEdges); query on demand. Must be inside a ReactFlowProvider or ReactFlow. Generic over <NodeType, EdgeType>.
terminal_note: In C the 'instance' is just the editor/context handle; this hook is the React access mechanism, replaced by passing the context pointer.
evidence: The flow_t* handle IS the instance — every public function takes it as the first argument (flow_new returns it, src/flow_model.h:32). The React access mechanism is replaced by passing the pointer directly; no provider/context indirection needed (spec §1).

name: onInit callback as instance source
description: Alternative to useReactFlow: the ReactFlow onInit event delivers the ReactFlowInstance once the viewport initializes (e.g. onInit={setRfInstance} or onInit={i => i.zoomTo(2)}). Renamed from onLoad in v11.
terminal_note: A one-shot ready callback handing back the editor handle; an init hook in C.
evidence: No on_init callback in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:180-190) and grep 'on_init' src/ → 0 hits. Unneeded in practice — the app holds flow_t* from flow_new directly — but the explicit one-shot ready hook isn't built and isn't in spec.

name: useNodes() hook
description: Returns the current nodes array and re-renders the component whenever ANY node changes (including selection/move). Generic over node type. Docs warn it is a common performance pitfall — prefer useStore with a narrow selector for specific derived values (e.g. node count).
terminal_note: Subscription that triggers redraw on any node change; maps to a dirty-on-change redraw, with the same over-redraw caveat.
evidence: flow_nodes(f)/flow_node_count(f) give the node array (src/flow_model.h:47,561,563). The React subscription-triggers-redraw is replaced by the run loop re-rendering the whole store each frame (flow_present → flow_render reads the store, src/flow_run.h:7, src/flow_render.h:163) — damage-diff makes this cheap, sidestepping the over-redraw pitfall entirely.

name: useEdges() hook
description: Returns the current edges array and re-renders on any edge change. Generic over edge type. Same performance caveat as useNodes — prefer useStore selectors for narrow needs.
terminal_note: Subscription -> redraw on edge change; maps to dirty-flag redraw.
evidence: flow_edges(f)/flow_edge_count(f) (src/flow_model.h:48,562,564); the renderer reads edges each frame in flow_render (src/flow_render.h:172) and damage-diffs. Subscription is replaced by per-frame read of the store.

name: useNodesState(initialNodes)
description: Prototyping helper returning [nodes, setNodes, onNodesChange]. Wraps React useState + applyNodeChanges so a controlled flow works with minimal code. Generic over custom node type. Docs note: fine for prototyping, but production may want a real state manager.
terminal_note: Bundles a node array + setter + change-applier; in C this is the built-in node store plus an apply-changes function.
evidence: This bundles the controlled-flow [state, setter, applyNodeChanges] triple. Spec §1 deliberately collapses that contract — flow owns one store and mutates directly, no applyNodeChanges. The store + mutators exist, but the change-applier triple this hook wraps is intentionally not built (grep 'applyNodeChanges'/'flow_apply_node_changes' src/ → 0 hits).

name: useEdgesState(initialEdges)
description: Prototyping helper returning [edges, setEdges, onEdgesChange]; wraps useState + applyEdgeChanges. Generic over custom edge type.
terminal_note: Edge array + setter + change-applier; built-in edge store equivalent in C.
evidence: Edge analog of useNodesState — the [edges, setEdges, applyEdgeChanges] triple. Spec §1 collapses the change-descriptor contract; flow owns the edge store and mutates via flow_add_edge/flow_remove_edge/flow_reconnect_edge directly. The applyEdgeChanges wrapper is not built.

name: useNodesData(nodeId | nodeId[])
description: Subscribes to changes of a specific node's (or several nodes') data object. Accepts a single id (returns {id,type,data}|null) or an array (returns array). Core building block for computing flows: read upstream node data reactively. Generic over node type.
terminal_note: Subscribe to one/many nodes' data and recompute on change; in C an observer/dirty-list over node data.
evidence: flow_get_node exposes a node's data (void*, src/flow_model.h:457,559) for on-demand read, but there is no reactive data-change subscription/observer (no node-data dirty list, no callback on data mutation). grep 'useNodesData'/'on_node_data' analog → none. The reactive computing-flows primitive is unbuilt.

name: useNodesInitialized(options?)
description: Returns true once all nodes have been measured (given width/height); false while a newly added node is unmeasured. Option includeHiddenNodes. Always false when the nodes array is empty. Used to delay auto-layout until dimensions exist.
terminal_note: The DOM-measurement gate collapses: sizes are computed synchronously by measure() at add-time, so nodes are always 'initialized' the instant they exist.
evidence: flow_add_node calls flow_measure_node synchronously (src/flow_model.h:457,448-450), so w/h are always set the instant a node exists — there is never an unmeasured state to gate on. The spec §5 explicitly notes 'useUpdateNodeInternals has no analog' for the same reason (no DOM measurement). The hook's entire purpose (wait for async measurement) does not exist in this model. Nearest analog: nodes are unconditionally measured at creation.

name: useInternalNode(id)
description: Returns the InternalNode for an id and re-renders whenever that node changes (selected/moved). Exposes internals.positionAbsolute (group-resolved absolute coords) plus measured dimensions. Returns undefined if absent. Generic over node type.
terminal_note: Reactive read of one node's computed/internal fields; absolute-position resolution is integer cell math in C.
evidence: flow_get_node + flow_node_abs (group-resolved absolute, src/flow_model.h:565-570) + flow_node_rect_abs (571) + cached w/h give the InternalNode's positionAbsolute and measured fields; returns NULL if absent. The reactive re-render is replaced by per-frame read. Same data as getInternalNode, read on demand.

name: useNodeId()
description: Returns the id of the node a component is rendered inside (from NodeIdContext), or null. Lets deep children of a custom node access its id without prop-drilling. Only valid inside a custom node subtree.
terminal_note: In C the custom-node renderer receives the node directly (const flow_node*), so its id is a field, not a context lookup.
evidence: The node-type render vtable passes the node directly: render(const flow_node*, flow_surface*, flow_render_ctx) — src/flow_model.h:19, spec §5. The renderer reads n->id from the struct (the flow_node has an id field, model.h:175ff) — no React context/prop-drilling needed; the id is right there. The need this hook solves is structurally absent.

name: useKeyPress(keyCode?, options?)
description: Reports whether given key(s) are currently pressed. keyCode is a string ('a'), a combo with '+' ('a+d' / 'Meta+s'), or an array of alternatives (['a','d+s']). Options: target element, actInsideInputWithModifier, preventDefault. Does NOT require a ReactFlowInstance — usable anywhere.
terminal_note: Terminals deliver key-press events (via SGR/stdin), not held-state with reliable keyup, so the engine binds key events; persistent held-state for arbitrary keys has no clean analog.
evidence: flow_bind_key(f,seq,fn,user) + flow_dispatch_key dispatch on key-event sequences from flow_feed (src/flow_model.h:115, dispatch 907ff). Delta vs xyflow: this covers key EVENTS (the terminal's native model), not continuous held-state polling — terminals lack reliable keyup. Space-pan does maintain a sticky held-state for one key (space_held, model.h:212) as a special case, but generic per-key held-state polling is not provided.

name: useOnSelectionChange({ onChange })
description: Registers a callback fired whenever node OR edge selection changes; receives { nodes, edges } (the selected sets). The handler MUST be memoized or it misbehaves. Only valid inside ReactFlowProvider/ReactFlow.
terminal_note: Selection-change observer delivering selected node/edge sets; a callback fired by the selection model in C.
evidence: flow_callbacks.on_selection_change(f,ids,n,user) is fired after the FLOW_SELECTED node set changes, in insertion order, only on actual change — src/flow_model.h:187, fired via flow__fire_selection_change 653-661 from every selection mutator (select/toggle/clear/marquee/delete). Delta: it delivers the selected NODE id set; edges are not bundled into the same event.

name: useStore(selector, equalityFn?)
description: Subscribes to internal Zustand store state via a selector returning a slice; re-renders only when the slice changes. equalityFn (e.g. Object.is or shallow) prevents unnecessary re-renders. Eager/subscription counterpart to useStoreApi's on-demand reads. Generic over ReactFlowState.
terminal_note: Selector-based subscription to internal state with equality gating; maps to observing specific fields of the editor state and redrawing on change.
evidence: Tests/embedders read internal state by selecting struct fields directly off flow_t (e.g. f->journal.n, f->view) — the struct is visible under FLOW_IMPLEMENTATION (src/flow_model.h:202ff); public accessors (flow_view_get, flow_zoom, flow_selected_nodes, flow_node_count) are the 'selectors'. There is no Zustand subscription/equality machinery, but the equivalent — reading state slices, redrawn each frame — is built; the selector subscription itself is replaced by the per-frame render model.

name: useStore store-action dispatch
description: Beyond read selectors, useStore can select internal ACTIONS (e.g. state.setMinZoom) and call them to drive internal behavior, since these are the same actions the library uses internally. Distinct capability from read selectors.
terminal_note: Invoking internal mutators directly; in C these are just internal API functions on the editor state.
evidence: The library's internal actions ARE the public C functions called directly on flow_t — e.g. setMinZoom maps to flow_set_zoom_limits(f,zmin,zmax) (src/flow_view.h:6,11) and flow_set_undo_limit (model.h:136); no store-action indirection needed. Calling the engine's own mutators is the native model.

name: useStoreApi()
description: Returns the raw store object { getState, setState, subscribe } for on-demand (non-subscribing) access to internal state and dispatching actions. Reads values in event handlers without forcing re-renders. Last-resort escape hatch; prefer dedicated hooks. Exposes nodeLookup etc. via getState().
terminal_note: Direct getState/setState/subscribe handle to internal state; maps to direct access to the editor state struct in C.
evidence: The flow_t* IS the raw store handle: getState = read f-> fields / accessors, setState = call mutators, and the lookup (nodeLookup) is flow_get_node (src/flow_model.h:559). Under FLOW_IMPLEMENTATION the full struct flow is accessible (model.h:202); the escape-hatch direct access is exactly how tests poke internals. subscribe is moot (per-frame render).

name: useNodeConnections({ handleType?, handleId?, id? })
description: Returns NodeConnection[] for the current (or specified) node, filterable by handle type ('source'/'target') and handle id. The reactive hook counterpart of getNodeConnections; central to computing-flows (finding upstream/downstream nodes). Reads node id from NodeIdContext if not provided.
terminal_note: Reactive filter of edges incident to a node/port; graph query + dirty-on-change in C.
evidence: No incident-edge query (the reactive counterpart of the unbuilt getNodeConnections). grep 'flow_node_connections' src/ → 0 hits; edges are iterable via flow_edges but no filter-by-node/port function exists. Computing-flows primitive is unbuilt.

name: useHandleConnections (deprecated)
description: Returns connections on a specific handle ({ type, id?, nodeId?, onConnect?, onDisconnect? }). DEPRECATED in favor of the more capable useNodeConnections; onConnect/onDisconnect fire on connect/remove.
terminal_note: Deprecated; superseded by useNodeConnections. Same per-handle edge-query semantics; implement the successor instead.
evidence: Deprecated upstream; per-handle edge-connection query not built (grep 'flow_handle_connections' src/ → 0 hits). on_connect fires on edge creation (src/flow_model.h:185,1016) but there is no per-handle connection list or onDisconnect. If built at all, the successor useNodeConnections should be — also a candidate.

name: useViewport()
description: Convenience hook to read the current Viewport { x, y, zoom } reactively in a component (re-renders on pan/zoom).
terminal_note: Reactive viewport read; pan + real float zoom both live, read each frame.
evidence: flow_view_get(f) returns the live {ox,oy,zoom} viewport (src/flow_model.h:71,643). The reactive re-render is replaced by the run loop reading and re-rendering each frame; pan and real float zoom are both present (src/flow_view.h).

name: useOnViewportChange({ onStart, onChange, onEnd })
description: Registers callbacks for the phases of a viewport change (pan/zoom): onStart, onChange, onEnd, each receiving a Viewport. Only valid inside ReactFlowProvider/ReactFlow.
terminal_note: Phase callbacks for pan/zoom; pan-scroll and real-zoom phases both map, but no such callback is registered.
evidence: flow_callbacks has no on_viewport_change / start/change/end hook — grep 'on_viewport' src/ → 0 hits; the struct (src/flow_model.h:180-190) covers node/pane/connect/selection/delete/overlay only. Viewport changes happen via flow_pan/flow_set_zoom but fire no phase callbacks. Not in spec; a future candidate.

name: useUpdateNodeInternals()
description: Returns a function updateNodeInternals(id) you must call after programmatically adding/removing handles or changing handle positions, so React Flow re-measures node dimensions and repositions handles/edges. Must be inside provider/ReactFlow.
terminal_note: Handle anchors are computed analytically every frame, so there is nothing to imperatively re-measure — the need is designed out.
evidence: Spec §5 states explicitly: 'Handle anchors are computed analytically — no DOM measure ... recomputed every frame, so useUpdateNodeInternals has no analog.' Verified: flow_handle_anchor computes anchors from node rect + handle pos every frame (src/flow_model.h:78,935ff); flow_measure_node recomputes w/h at add/load. There is no stale internal state to imperatively refresh. Nearest analog: anchors are always live, recomputed each render.

name: getIncomers(node, nodes, edges)
description: Graph util returning the nodes connected to the given node as the SOURCE of an edge (i.e. its predecessors / incoming neighbors). Accepts {id} or full node.
terminal_note: Predecessor lookup over the edge list; pure graph traversal in C.
evidence: No predecessor-traversal util. grep 'flow_incomers' src/ → 0 hits. Edges are iterable via flow_edges (src/flow_model.h:48) so it's trivially buildable, but the function does not exist and is not in spec/pending packages.

name: getOutgoers(node, nodes, edges)
description: Graph util returning the nodes connected to the given node as the TARGET of an edge (i.e. its successors / outgoing neighbors).
terminal_note: Successor lookup over the edge list; pure graph traversal.
evidence: No successor-traversal util. grep 'flow_outgoers' src/ → 0 hits. Buildable over flow_edges but not present and not in spec.

name: getConnectedEdges(nodes, edges)
description: Graph util filtering edges to those whose source OR target is among the given nodes. Useful for deleting/highlighting all edges touching a selection.
terminal_note: Filter edges incident to a node set; pure graph query.
evidence: No public connected-edges util. grep 'flow_connected_edges' src/ → 0 hits. flow_remove_node walks edges internally to cascade incident edges (src/flow_model.h:789ff) but exposes no reusable query. Not in spec; a future candidate.

name: isNode(element)
description: Type-guard util: tests whether an arbitrary object is usable as a Node (narrows type in TypeScript). Used to discriminate mixed element arrays.
terminal_note: Shape/tag check distinguishing node vs edge; a discriminant/enum check in C.
evidence: Nodes and edges are distinct C struct types (flow_node, flow_edge — src/flow_model.h) kept in separate typed vec arrays (f->nodes / f->edges); there is no mixed element[] array and no untyped union to discriminate. The TS type-guard exists only because JS arrays mix Node|Edge — that situation cannot arise in C's static typing. Nearest analog: the static type itself is the guard. grep 'flow_is_node' src/ → 0 hits.

name: isEdge(element)
description: Type-guard util: tests whether an object is usable as an Edge (narrows type in TypeScript).
terminal_note: Shape/tag check distinguishing edge vs node; discriminant check in C.
evidence: Same as isNode: flow_edge is a distinct static type in its own array (src/flow_model.h:48); no mixed Node|Edge array exists to discriminate at runtime. The C type system is the guard. grep 'flow_is_edge' src/ → 0 hits.

name: getViewportForBounds(bounds, width, height, minZoom, maxZoom, padding)
description: Low-level util computing the Viewport { x, y, zoom } that encloses a Rect within a given width/height with padding and zoom clamps — WITHOUT changing the viewport. Used to pre-calculate viewports server-side or for image export. Formerly getTransformForBounds. Higher-level alternatives: fitView/fitBounds.
terminal_note: Pure math returning pan+zoom for a fit; real float zoom makes the returned transform meaningful.
evidence: The getViewportForBounds math (pick zoom + pan to enclose a rect with padding, clamped to [zmin,zmax]) is implemented but INLINED inside flow_fit_view, which mutates f->view rather than returning a viewport — src/flow_model.h:862-868. There is no standalone pure util that returns the {ox,oy,zoom} without applying it (no bounds/width/height/minZoom params). The computation exists; the return-only util does not. grep 'viewport_for_bounds' src/ → 0 hits.

name: <ReactFlowProvider> context
description: Context provider exposing the flow's internal state (nodes, edges, viewport) to components OUTSIDE <ReactFlow>; required for most hooks (useReactFlow, useStore, useNodes, etc.). Typically near the app root.
terminal_note: A shared editor-state container/handle; in C this is just the editor context object passed to subsystems — no React context needed.
evidence: The flow_t* created by flow_new(cols,rows) is the shared editor-state container (src/flow_model.h:32,397); it is passed to every subsystem function directly. No React context/provider indirection is needed (spec §1: 'In C we own one store'). The provider's role — owning + sharing the state — is exactly flow_t.

name: ReactFlowProvider SSR/init props
description: Provider props for first-render/SSR control: initialNodes, initialEdges, defaultNodes, defaultEdges, initialWidth, initialHeight, fitView, initialFitViewOptions, initialMinZoom, initialMaxZoom, nodeOrigin, nodeExtent, zIndexMode, children. 'initial-' values seed only the first render; thereafter context nodes/edges win.
terminal_note: Init params (size, zoom limits, fit) map to constructor + setters; SSR initialWidth/initialHeight seeding has no terminal analog since size is known from the TTY.
evidence: The init-config subset that matters in C is built: flow_new(cols,rows) seeds size (src/flow_model.h:397), flow_set_zoom_limits is initialMinZoom/initialMaxZoom (src/flow_view.h:6), flow_fit_view is the initial fitView (model.h:862), and nodes/edges are seeded by add_node/add_edge after construction. The SSR-only initialWidth/initialHeight first-render dance is moot (terminal size is known) and nodeOrigin/zIndexMode are not separate knobs — those specific props are absent, hence partial.

name: Multiple flows on one page
description: Each independent flow needs its own <ReactFlowProvider>; instances and hooks are scoped per provider. Distinct testable behavior (state isolation between flows).
source: component `<ReactFlowProvider />` Notes ('multiple flows... separate provider for each') — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/react-flow-provider
terminal_note: Multiple isolated editor-state instances; in C, several independent editor structs each owning their own graph + viewport.
evidence: flow_new returns a fully self-contained, heap-allocated flow_t owning its own nodes/edges/viewport/types/callbacks (src/flow_model.h:32,202-231,397); nothing is global, so creating two flow_t* yields two isolated editors. State isolation is structural. (tests routinely create independent flow_t per test, e.g. tests/test_json.c builds A and B.)

name: Provider placement outside router
description: To persist a flow's state across client-side route changes, the <ReactFlowProvider> must be mounted OUTSIDE the router (otherwise state resets on navigation).
terminal_note: React/client-router mount-lifecycle concern; a single-process TUI has no router and the flow_t simply lives for the program's lifetime.
evidence: This is a React client-router remount concern. A TUI is a single process with no client-side routing; the flow_t* persists for the program lifetime regardless (allocated by flow_new, freed by flow_free, src/flow_model.h:32-33). There is no navigation event that could reset it. Nearest analog: none — the failure mode the advice avoids cannot occur.

name: Controlled vs uncontrolled flow
description: Controlled: app owns nodes/edges, passes them in plus onNodesChange/onEdgesChange and re-applies changes. Uncontrolled: pass defaultNodes/defaultEdges and let ReactFlow manage internal state, then mutate via the instance (addNodes etc.). Determines who is the source of truth.
terminal_note: Source-of-truth choice; a C port can own the store internally (uncontrolled) or expose change callbacks for an external owner (controlled).
evidence: flow takes the uncontrolled side decisively: the library owns one store and the app mutates via the instance (flow_add_node/flow_move_node etc.) — spec §1 ('In C we own one store and mutate it directly'). The controlled half (external source-of-truth + applyChanges) is deliberately NOT offered. The source-of-truth model is resolved and built (uncontrolled), so the feature 'which model applies' is realized — as the uncontrolled one only.

name: External state manager integration (Zustand/Redux/etc.)
description: Pattern for wiring an external store as source of truth: keep nodes/edges in the store with onNodesChange/onEdgesChange/onConnect actions implemented via applyNodeChanges/applyEdgeChanges/addEdge; expose custom actions (e.g. updateNodeColor) read by custom nodes via the store hook instead of prop-drilling functions through node.data. Zustand recommended (RF uses it internally) but Redux/Recoil/Jotai work too.
terminal_note: Centralized store + actions feeding the editor and read by node renderers; the engine IS the centralized store with mutator actions.
evidence: flow_t IS the centralized store with mutator 'actions' (the flow_* functions); custom node renderers read app data via node->data (passed into render(), src/flow_model.h:19,457) and on_overlay/callbacks (180-190) rather than prop-drilling — exactly the pattern this learn-doc advocates. The external-store contract built on applyNodeChanges is unneeded because flow itself is that store. The capability (centralized store + actions read by renderers) is realized natively.

name: applyNodeChanges(changes, nodes) / applyEdgeChanges(changes, edges)
description: Drop-in utils that apply an array of NodeChange/EdgeChange (position, dimensions, selection, removal, add, etc.) to a nodes/edges array, returning the new array. The core of controlled-flow and external-store integration; useNodesState/useEdgesState wrap them.
terminal_note: Reducer applying a change list to the graph; the change vocabulary instead returns as the undo/redo command layer (spec §1, §11).
evidence: Spec §1 explicitly: the React controlled change-descriptor contract 'exists only because React state is immutable ... In C we own one store and mutate it directly' and 'The change vocabulary returns, deliberately, as the undo/redo command layer (§11).' So applyNodeChanges/applyEdgeChanges as a reducer over an external array is intentionally NOT built — grep 'flow_apply_node_changes' src/ → 0 hits. The closest landed thing is the undo command journal (src/flow_undo.h), which is a different mechanism. The named util is a candidate.

name: Computing flows (data propagation through the graph)
description: Pattern for computing data inside the flow: store inputs in node.data; in a downstream custom node use useNodeConnections to find connected sources, useNodesData to read their data reactively, derive a result, and write it back with updateNodeData (often in a useEffect keyed on upstream data). Builds dataflow/visual-programming graphs entirely client-side. By default updateNodeData merges; { replace: true } overwrites.
terminal_note: Topological/observer-driven data propagation over the graph; a C dataflow engine could propagate on upstream-data change.
evidence: This pattern needs three unbuilt primitives in concert: useNodeConnections (no incident-edge query), useNodesData (no reactive data subscription), and updateNodeData (no data-merge fn) — all three are candidates (grep returned 0 hits for each). node->data exists for storage but there is no propagation/observer engine. Not in spec; a future candidate composed of currently-missing parts.

name: Generic typing of instance/hooks (NodeType/EdgeType)
description: useReactFlow, useNodes, useEdges, useNodesState/useEdgesState, useNodesData, useInternalNode, useStore, useStoreApi, useOnSelectionChange all accept generic <NodeType, EdgeType> (often union types like ColorNode | BuiltInNode) so methods/returns are correctly typed. Exposes typed Node/Edge/FitViewOptions/OnConnect/OnNodesChange etc.
terminal_note: TS generics have no runtime cost; in C the analog is a node 'type' tag + per-type data struct (tagged union) selected by node.type.
evidence: The C analog is realized: flow_node carries a type[32] string tag and an opaque void* data payload (src/flow_model.h, spec §4), and the node/edge type vtable registries (flow_register_node_type, flow_node_type_for — model.h:35-38) dispatch per-type renderers, exactly as TS NodeType unions select components. demos/topo.c defines a 'device' typed node with its own data struct. TS compile-time generics map to this runtime type-tag + per-type struct; the capability (per-type typed nodes) is built.

name: SSR/SSG static rendering of flows
description: Server rendering (since v12): supply node width/height (static) or initialWidth/initialHeight (dynamic, first-render only) since nodes can't be measured server-side; supply node.handles (type/position/x/y) so edges can be drawn; pass container width/height (and to ReactFlowProvider as initialWidth/initialHeight) to use fitView on the server; render with React's renderToStaticMarkup to produce static HTML for docs/non-JS/OG images.
terminal_note: The motivation — render without live measurement — is exactly the TUI's situation: sizes/handle anchors are supplied/computed, not DOM-measured. Rendering to a static cell buffer without an interaction loop is the direct analog.
evidence: flow_render(f, out, cols, rows) composes the entire scene into a caller-provided cell buffer with NO TTY and no interaction loop — src/flow_render.h:2,163; spec §6/§13 ('flow_render lets tests compose a known scene into memory'). Sizes are computed by measure() not DOM-measured and handle anchors are analytic (spec §5) — precisely the no-live-measurement situation SSR addresses. This is used by every snapshot test to produce static output. The 'render to static buffer without a live loop' capability is fully built; output is a cell grid, not HTML.

name: Client-side image export (downloadImage + html-to-image)
description: Export pattern: compute getNodesBounds(getNodes()) then getViewportForBounds to fit all nodes, call toPng() (html-to-image) on the .react-flow__viewport element overriding its CSS transform, then trigger a browser download via an <a download> with the data URL. Supports backgroundColor/width/height. (html-to-image pinned to 1.11.11.)
terminal_note: Rasterizes DOM to PNG via html-to-image/canvas + browser download — no terminal analog; the closest is dumping the cell buffer to text/ANSI, a different mechanism.
evidence: Rasterizing DOM to a PNG via html-to-image/canvas and a browser <a download> has no terminal counterpart (no DOM, no canvas, no browser). grep for any image/png export → none. The nearest analog — stringifying the flow_render cell buffer to text/ANSI (as snapshot tests do via tests/flowtest.h) — is a fundamentally different mechanism, not image export. Correctly terminal_fit:no.

name: Server-side image creation (Puppeteer, Pro)
description: Pro example: combine v12 static rendering with a backend (Express + Puppeteer) that screenshots the rendered flow to produce server-generated images (e.g. OG embeds), with Dockerfile for deployment. Edit nodes on the client; image is generated/served from the server.
terminal_note: Headless-browser screenshot pipeline (Puppeteer/canvas) — inherently DOM/browser-bound with no terminal counterpart.
evidence: A headless-browser (Puppeteer) screenshot pipeline serving images over HTTP is entirely DOM/browser/server infrastructure with no terminal equivalent — no browser to drive, no canvas to capture. grep → none. The flow library is a single-header C TUI engine; this Pro web-deployment example has no analog. Correctly terminal_fit:no.

name: Node.id
description: Unique id of a node (string). Required. Used everywhere to reference the node and resolve edges/parents.
terminal_note: flow uses a monotonic int id, not a string — a deliberate spec divergence (§4) confirmed correct.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `flow_node{ int id; ... }`; flow_add_node mints ids, flow_get_node(f,id) resolves; spec §4 'int ids (not strings)'.

name: Node.position
description: XYPosition {x,y} giving the node's position on the pane. For child nodes this is parent-relative. Required.
terminal_note: Integer cell pos (flow_pt); parent-relative for children, absolute via flow_node_abs. No quantize needed — already cells.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `flow_pt pos` (relative to parent else world); flow_node_pos (relative) + flow_node_abs (src/flow_model.h:49,51); groups commit 438ea0d.

name: Node.data
description: Arbitrary user data passed to the node (NodeData). Read by the node renderer; updated immutably via setNodes/updateNodeData.
terminal_note: void* opaque payload per node; renderer reads it (e.g. default type treats data as a C-string label). No immutability semantics in C.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `void *data`; flow__default_render reads (const char*)n->data (src/flow_types.h:9); flow_add_node(...,void*data).

name: Node.type (+ built-in default/input/output/group)
description: Type string keyed into nodeTypes to pick the renderer. Built-in values: "default", "input", "output", "group". If type is unset React Flow falls back to "default". Built-ins remain available even when nodeTypes is set.
source: Node type field `type` + 'Default node types' section — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/node
terminal_note: type[32] dispatches to a vtable. flow ships only `default` (both handles) + `group`; xyflow's source-only `input` and target-only `output` are NOT built in (and not spec'd — §5 commits to default+group only).
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `char type[32]`; flow_node_type_for dispatch; flow_register_defaults registers ONLY default + group (src/flow_types.h:47-52). input/output absent — spec §5 lists only 'a default node ... and group'.

name: Node.sourcePosition
description: Position enum controlling where the source handle sits. Only relevant for default/source/target node types.
terminal_note: flow attaches a handle's side via the per-type flow_handle.pos (a flow_pos side), declared on the node TYPE rather than per-node sourcePosition; the default type fixes 'out' to RIGHT.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:10 `flow_handle{ flow_pos pos; }`; flow_default_handles `out` is FLOW_RIGHT (src/flow_types.h:25). Per-type, not a per-node sourcePosition field — handle side IS the source position.

name: Node.targetPosition
description: Position enum controlling where the target handle sits. Only relevant for default/source/target node types.
terminal_note: Same mechanics as source: handle side via flow_handle.pos on the type; default 'in' is LEFT.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:10 flow_handle.pos; flow_default_handles `in` is FLOW_LEFT FLOW_HANDLE_TARGET (src/flow_types.h:24). Per-type handle side, not a per-node targetPosition field.

name: Node.hidden
description: boolean — whether or not the node should be visible on the canvas. Hidden nodes are removed from rendering; cascade to edges.
terminal_note: Would be a trivial visibility flag skipped in draw+hit-test, cascading to incident edges. Not built and not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'hidden' src/ → 0 files; flow_node flags are only SELECTED|DRAGGING|HOVERED (src/flow_model.h:2); no visibility flag in struct (src/flow_model.h:12). Not in spec §4 or workpackages.

name: Node.selected
description: boolean — current selection state of the node, managed by React Flow / settable by user.
terminal_note: FLOW_SELECTED flag bit; selected nodes draw bold and last within their sibling layer.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:2 `FLOW_SELECTED=1u`; flow_select_node/toggle/clear (src/flow_model.h:92-94); render passes n->flags to ctx and bolds selected (src/flow_types.h:15).

name: Node.dragging
description: boolean — whether the node is currently being dragged. Set by React Flow during a drag interaction.
terminal_note: Spec names a FLOW_DRAGGING flag (§4), but the live drag tracks f->drag_node instead; the flag is never set during a drag — only cleared on undo-restore. The named-but-unmaintained transient flag.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:2 enum defines FLOW_DRAGGING=2u; grep 'FLOW_DRAGGING' src/ → only the enum def + two CLEARS in flow_undo.h:25,34. Drag uses f->drag_node (src/flow_input.h:173,190); flag never OR'd in. Named in spec §4 'flags ... DRAGGING'.

name: Node.draggable
description: boolean — whether the node is able to be dragged. Per-node override of the flow-level nodesDraggable.
terminal_note: Would gate the drag interaction per node; a boolean. All nodes are currently draggable unconditionally. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'draggable' src/ → 0 files; drag arms for any down_node with no per-node gate (src/flow_input.h:170-178). No such field in flow_node (src/flow_model.h:12); absent from spec.

name: Node.selectable
description: boolean — whether the node can be selected. Per-node override of flow-level elementsSelectable.
terminal_note: Would gate selection per node; a boolean. All nodes selectable unconditionally today. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'selectable' src/ → 0 files; flow_select_node has no per-node gate (src/flow_model.h:92). No field in flow_node; absent from spec.

name: Node.connectable
description: boolean — whether connections can be started/ended at this node's handles. Per-node override; surfaces as NodeProps.isConnectable.
terminal_note: Connectability is per-TYPE today (handle_count==0 => not connectable, as for group); no per-node connectable boolean. Not spec'd as a node field.
evidence: grep 'connectable' src/ → only a comment in flow_types.h:43 ('group has NO handles ... not connectable'); that is type-level (handle_count) not a per-node field. No node.connectable in struct (src/flow_model.h:12); absent from spec.

name: Node.deletable
description: boolean — whether the node can be deleted (e.g. via Backspace/Delete or the delete API).
terminal_note: Would gate deletion per node; a boolean. flow_delete_selection / flow_remove_node delete unconditionally. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'deletable' src/ → 0 files; flow_remove_node (src/flow_model.h:104) and flow_delete_selection (src/flow_model.h:118) have no per-node gate. No field in flow_node; absent from spec.

name: Node.focusable
description: boolean — whether the node element can receive focus (keyboard tab focus / a11y).
terminal_note: A TUI analog would be a focused-node index for arrow/tab nav gated by this flag; flow has no keyboard node-focus traversal at all, so there is nothing to gate. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'focusable' src/ → 0 files; no keyboard focus/tab order — flow_hovered_node tracks pointer hover only (src/flow_model.h:83). No field; absent from spec.

name: Node.dragHandle
description: A CSS class string; only matching sub-elements act as drag handles, so the node drags only by that region.
terminal_note: No CSS in a TUI; the analog is designating a sub-region (e.g. a title row) of the node's cell rect as the only drag-initiating zone. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'drag_handle\|dragHandle' src/ → 0 hits; drag arms on any node-body press (src/flow_input.h:170). No region-restriction; absent from spec.

name: Node.width / Node.height (read-only, measured)
description: number — the node's render size, calculated internally from the measured DOM; not set directly.
terminal_note: flow inverts xyflow: w/h are CACHED from the type's measure() callback in character cells, never DOM-measured — the spec's deliberate collapse of the whole measured/width/height machinery.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `int w,h /* CACHED from vtable->measure */`; flow_measure_node (src/flow_model.h:39) calls type->measure; spec §4 'sizes computed by measure() rather than DOM-measured'.

name: Node.initialWidth / Node.initialHeight
description: number — initial width/height used before the node is measured, important for SSR/first render.
terminal_note: flow measures synchronously at add time (flow_measure_node runs inside flow_add_node), so there is no pre-measure window to seed — the SSR-jump concern does not exist. Intentionally collapsed by the measure() model.
evidence: Sizes set immediately by flow_measure_node within add (src/flow_model.h:39); spec §4 collapses xyflow's measured/initial* machinery as a 'browser-only artifact'. grep 'initial' src/ → none relevant.

name: Node.measured
description: { width?; height? } — the actual measured rendered dimensions, distinct from width/height/initial* and style.
terminal_note: Collapsed into the single cached w/h computed by measure(); flow has exactly one computed-size field, which is the spec's stated simplification.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 single `int w,h` cached from measure(); spec §4 'collapses xyflow's entire measured/width/height/NodeDimensionChange machinery'. The w/h IS the measured size.

name: Node.parentId (sub-flows)
description: string — parent node id for sub-flows. Child positions become relative to the parent; rendering/z-order nests under the parent.
terminal_note: int parent pointer; child pos relative, absolute via parent-chain walk; depth-aware z-order. Full scene-graph nesting.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 `int parent` (-1 if none); flow_node_abs walks chain; flow__node_depth + flow__node_order depth-aware draw (src/flow_model.h:475,614); groups commit 438ea0d; tests/test_groups.c.

name: Node.extent (CoordinateExtent | "parent" | null)
description: Boundary a node can be moved within: explicit [[minX,minY],[maxX,maxY]], the string "parent" (clamp inside parent), or null (unbounded).
terminal_note: Pure-arithmetic position clamp during move; 'parent' clamps to the parent rect. Spec §9 names it ('Optional coordinate extent clamps a child within its parent') but the groups package did not build it.
evidence: Spec §9 'Optional coordinate extent clamps a child within its parent.' No clamp on move — flow_move_node (src/flow_input.h:214,220) applies delta with no bound; grep 'extent' src/ → only an unrelated comment (flow_input.h:56); tests/test_groups.c has no extent test.

name: Node.expandParent
description: boolean — when true, the parent auto-expands its bounds if this child is dragged to the parent's edge.
terminal_note: On child move, grow the parent rect to contain the child; arithmetic. Not built and not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'expand' src/ → 0 files; flow_group sizes the container once from the bbox at creation, never re-grows on child drag (src/flow_model.h flow_group). Absent from spec §9 and workpackages.

name: Node.origin (NodeOrigin)
description: NodeOrigin [number,number] anchor of the node relative to its own coords: [0,0]=top-left ... [1,1]=bottom-right; affects how position maps to rendered top-left.
terminal_note: renderTopLeft = pos - origin*size, pure cell arithmetic. flow treats pos as the top-left implicitly (origin fixed at [0,0]); no configurable origin. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'origin' src/ → only undo/insertion-order comments (flow_undo.h, flow_model.h:349), no NodeOrigin. flow_node_rect_abs uses pos as top-left directly; no origin field in struct (src/flow_model.h:12). Absent from spec.

name: Node.zIndex
description: number — stacking order of the node relative to others, combined with automatic z management and selection/sub-flow rules.
terminal_note: Painter-order draw is implemented, but driven by depth + selection, not a user-set zIndex field. No per-node manual z. Not spec'd as a user field.
evidence: grep 'zIndex\|z_index' src/ → 0 files. Draw order is computed by flow__node_order from depth+selected (src/flow_model.h:614), with no user zIndex input; no zIndex field in flow_node (src/flow_model.h:12). Absent from spec.

name: Node.ariaLabel
description: string — accessibility label for the node element.
terminal_note: No screen-reader DOM; nearest analog would be a status-line announcement of the focused node. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'ariaLabel\|aria' src/ → 0 files; no a11y/announce path. No field in flow_node; absent from spec. (Closer to na-terminal, but a status-line analog is feasible, so candidate.)

name: Node.ariaRole
description: AriaRole — the ARIA role attribute for the node's DOM element. Default "group".
terminal_note: ARIA roles are a DOM/assistive-tech concept with no terminal analog; there is no DOM and no AT layer to consume a role. No nearest analog.
evidence: grep 'ariaRole\|role' src/ → 0 files; flow emits ANSI cells only (spec §6 compositor), no DOM/AT surface. Drop per terminal_fit 'no'.

name: Node.handles (NodeHandle[])
description: Array of NodeHandle descriptors attached to the node (used especially for SSR where DOM can't be measured).
terminal_note: flow always declares handles explicitly (no DOM to measure) — but on the node TYPE's handle array, not per node instance, with anchors computed analytically each frame.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:10 flow_handle{id,kind,pos,along}; flow_node_type.handles+handle_count (src/flow_model.h:20); flow_default_handles[2] (src/flow_types.h:23); anchors via flow_handle_anchor (src/flow_model.h:78). Per-type, not per-node-instance.

name: Node.style
description: CSSProperties — inline CSS on the node container; the documented way to size a node and set background/border.
terminal_note: No CSS; the renderer chooses cell attrs (fg/bg/bold/box style) directly in its draw fn. There is no per-node style field handed to a generic container — styling lives inside the type's render(). Not spec'd as a node field.
evidence: grep 'style' src/ → no node style field; flow_node has no style member (src/flow_model.h:12). Cell attrs are hard-chosen inside flow__default_render (src/flow_types.h:13-22). Absent from spec as a node field.

name: Node.className
description: string — CSS class names on the node container; examples switch appearance via it.
terminal_note: Analog would be a named style/theme tag resolved to terminal attrs; flow has no per-node theme tag — appearance is fixed in the type's render(). Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'className\|class_name' src/ → 0 hits; no per-node style/theme key in flow_node (src/flow_model.h:12). Absent from spec.

name: Node.resizing
description: boolean — whether the node is currently being resized (during a NodeResizer interaction).
terminal_note: A transient resize-gesture flag; but flow has no resize interaction at all (size is content-driven via measure()), so there is nothing to flag. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'resizing\|resize' src/ → 0 files (flow_resize is the terminal-size handler, unrelated). No NodeResizer; size from measure() (src/flow_model.h:39). Absent from spec.

name: Node.domAttributes
description: Escape hatch for custom HTML attributes on the node's DOM element.
terminal_note: Arbitrary HTML attributes have no terminal meaning; no DOM element exists. No nearest analog.
evidence: grep 'domAttributes\|dom_attr' src/ → 0 hits; flow renders to a cell buffer, no DOM (spec §6). Drop per terminal_fit 'no'.

name: Node width/height are read-only (size via style/className)
description: Documented constraint: you must not set width/height directly — they are computed internally; size via style/className CSS.
terminal_note: flow mirrors the rule by INVERTING the mechanism: w/h are derived from the type's measure() and never user-set, so they are effectively read-only/computed exactly as the constraint intends.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:12 'w,h CACHED from vtable->measure — never DOM-measured'; flow_measure_node is the sole writer (src/flow_model.h:39); spec §4. The computed-not-user-set rule holds.

name: NodeProps: full custom-node prop set
description: The exact props a wrapped custom node receives: id, data, width, height, sourcePosition, targetPosition, dragHandle, parentId, type, dragging, zIndex, selectable, deletable, selected, draggable, isConnectable, positionAbsoluteX/Y.
terminal_note: flow's render-callback signature is render(const flow_node*, flow_surface*, flow_render_ctx{zoom,flags,lod}). The node struct supplies id/type/data/w/h/parent; flags carries selected; absolute origin is handled by the surface placement. But many listed props (zIndex, selectable, deletable, draggable, dragHandle, isConnectable, split positionAbsoluteX/Y) are NOT passed — partial prop set.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:17-20 flow_node_type.render(const flow_node*, flow_surface*, flow_render_ctx); ctx={zoom,flags,lod} (src/flow_model.h:16). Renderer draws at surface-local (0,0) with absolute placement done by engine (src/flow_render.h:207). Delta: ctx omits zIndex/selectable/deletable/draggable/dragHandle/isConnectable/positionAbsoluteX-Y.

name: NodeProps.isConnectable
description: boolean passed to custom nodes indicating whether the node is connectable; used to enable/disable handle interaction inside the renderer.
terminal_note: The render ctx does not carry isConnectable; connectability is decided by the type's handle_count, not exposed to render(). A renderer cannot read it. Not spec'd.
evidence: flow_render_ctx is {float zoom; unsigned flags; int lod;} (src/flow_model.h:16) — no isConnectable. Connectability = handle_count>0 at the type level (src/flow_types.h:43). Not surfaced to renderer; absent from spec.

name: NodeProps.positionAbsoluteX / positionAbsoluteY
description: The node's absolute (flow-space) x and y, passed individually to custom nodes (parent transforms applied).
terminal_note: Absolute position is computed by flow_node_abs and used by the engine to place the node's surface at the right screen cell; the renderer draws at local (0,0) and never receives raw absolute X/Y — flow handles the transform for it (the same outcome xyflow's split props enable).
evidence: flow_node_abs walks the parent chain to absolute world (src/flow_model.h:49); flow_render projects it and hands a surface whose (0,0) is the node top-left (src/flow_render.h:202-207). Absolute coords are applied, just not passed as named props (spec §5: renderers 'never deal with world/screen/zoom').

name: Custom node: typed via Node<TData, TType> generic
description: Custom nodes can be strongly typed: NodeProps<CounterNode> tying a node type string to its data shape.
terminal_note: In C the analog is the per-type data convention: each type's render/measure/save/load cast n->data to their own struct (e.g. topo's `device`). There is no compile-time type-to-data binding (the TS generic is an ergonomic), but the runtime pattern is exactly this.
evidence: demos/topo.c:27 casts (const device*)n->data inside the DEVICE type's callbacks; DEVICE = {"device", dev_measure, dev_render, ..., dev_save, dev_load} (demos/topo.c:67). Per-type data-struct convention is the C analog; no generic type-checking.

name: NodeTypes mapping (type string -> component)
description: NodeTypes = { [key]: ComponentType }. Each node's type selects the renderer. Built-ins default/input/output/group always available unless overridden.
terminal_note: A registry of flow_node_type vtables keyed by type string; flow_node_type_for resolves type->vtable. Built-ins: default + group only (no input/output).
evidence: flow_register_node_type + flow_node_type_for(f, type) (src/flow_model.h:35,37); registry array on struct flow; flow_register_defaults registers default + group (src/flow_types.h:47-52).

name: nodeTypes referential stability requirement
description: nodeTypes must be defined outside render or memoized; a new object each render re-instantiates all node components and tanks performance.
terminal_note: Pure React reconciliation pitfall. In C the vtable table is registered once into the engine and read each frame; no re-instantiation hazard exists, so the requirement is meaningless here.
evidence: Types registered once via flow_register_node_type into a persistent registry on struct flow (src/flow_model.h:35); render reads it with no allocation (flow_node_type_for). No reconciliation; hazard absent. Drop per terminal_fit 'no'.

name: Custom node implementation (auto-wrapped container)
description: Any React component can be a node; React Flow wraps it in an interactive container injecting id/position/data and handling selection+dragging. Registered in nodeTypes by type.
terminal_note: The engine supplies the interactive shell (hit-test, select, drag, surface placement, clipping); the type provides only measure/render. The topo `device` type proves a real custom node consuming this.
evidence: flow_register_node_type registers a vtable (src/flow_model.h:35); engine does hit-test/select/drag/clip (src/flow_input.h, src/flow_render.h:202-209) and hands the type a positioned surface; demos/topo.c DEVICE type. Spec §5.

name: Adding Handles inside a custom node
description: Custom nodes place <Handle> elements (type source/target, Position side) to expose connection points; isConnectable gates them.
terminal_note: A custom type declares its handle array (id/kind/side/along) on the vtable; the engine marks those edge cells as anchors and reveals ◉ markers on hover/select. Declarative, not drawn by the renderer.
evidence: flow_node_type.handles + handle_count (src/flow_model.h:20); topo's DEVICE reuses flow_default_handles (demos/topo.c:67); engine draws ◉ markers + hit-tests them (src/flow_render.h:216-226, flow_hit_handle src/flow_model.h:81).

name: Hook: useNodeId
description: Hook returning the id of the node a custom-node component is rendered inside (no prop drilling).
terminal_note: The render/measure/save/load callbacks all receive the flow_node* directly, so node->id is in scope with zero plumbing — the hook's purpose is satisfied by the signature.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:18-22 every node-type callback takes (const flow_node *n ...); n->id available (src/flow_model.h:12). e.g. dev_render reads n (demos/topo.c).

name: Hook: useNodesData
description: Hook to read the data of one or more other nodes by id from inside a node, enabling data flow/derived display between nodes.
terminal_note: flow_get_node(f,id)->data is exactly this lookup — but the render() callback gets no flow_t*, only flow_node*/flow_surface/ctx, so a renderer cannot reach other nodes' data mid-draw. The lookup exists; in-renderer access does not. Not spec'd.
evidence: flow_get_node(f,id) returns the node incl. data (src/flow_model.h:43), but render signature has no flow_t* (src/flow_model.h:19) so it is unreachable from inside a node. No in-node data-flow API; absent from spec.

name: Hook: useNodeConnections
description: Hook to query the connections (incoming/outgoing edges/handles) attached to a node from within its renderer.
terminal_note: Would be an adjacency query over edges where source/target==node id. No such accessor exists, and the renderer has no flow_t* to call one. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: No connections/adjacency query API in src/flow_model.h accessor list (lines 43-51, 79-83); edges are a flat array iterated by index. render() has no flow_t* (src/flow_model.h:19). Absent from spec.

name: Hook: useUpdateNodeInternals
description: Hook returning a function to force re-measure of a node's internals (handle bounds, dimensions) after shape/handle/rotation change.
terminal_note: flow recomputes handle anchors and node size analytically every frame, so there is no cached internals state to invalidate — the spec explicitly states this hook has no analog.
evidence: Spec §5: 'Handle anchors are computed analytically ... recomputed every frame, so useUpdateNodeInternals has no analog.' flow_handle_anchor + flow_measure_node run each pass (src/flow_model.h:78,39); no internals cache to invalidate.

name: Hook: useReactFlow (instance access from a node)
description: Hook giving a custom node access to the React Flow instance for programmatic control (setNodes, getIntersectingNodes, viewport ops).
terminal_note: flow's instance handle is flow_t*, but the render() callback is NOT passed it (only flow_node*/surface/ctx), so a node renderer cannot reach the engine to mutate or query mid-draw. App-level callbacks (on_*) do get flow_t*. Not exposed in-renderer; not spec'd.
evidence: render signature lacks flow_t* (src/flow_model.h:19); only event callbacks receive flow_t* (src/flow_model.h:181-188). No in-renderer instance access; absent from spec.

name: NodeHandle descriptor (SSR handle geometry)
description: NodeHandle lets you declare handle geometry explicitly when DOM measurement isn't available: id, position, type, x, y, width, height.
terminal_note: flow ALWAYS declares handles explicitly (no DOM): flow_handle{id, kind, pos, along}. It stores the side+offset and computes the cell x/y analytically rather than storing x/y/w/h, but that is the same explicit-geometry model.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:10 flow_handle{char id[16]; flow_handle_kind kind; flow_pos pos; int along;}; anchor cell computed by flow_handle_anchor (src/flow_model.h:78). Explicit declaration, anchor derived rather than stored x/y/w/h.

name: Position enum (Top/Right/Bottom/Left)
description: Position = top|right|bottom|left; the anchor side on a node's perimeter where a handle/edge attaches.
terminal_note: Direct enum port: flow_pos {TOP,RIGHT,BOTTOM,LEFT}; used by handles and routers to pick the cell on the corresponding box edge.
evidence: src/flow_geom.h:4 `typedef enum { FLOW_TOP, FLOW_RIGHT, FLOW_BOTTOM, FLOW_LEFT } flow_pos;`; used by flow_handle.pos and route(s,sp,t,tp) (src/flow_model.h:10,25).

name: CoordinateExtent geometry & unbounded default
description: CoordinateExtent = [[minX,minY],[maxX,maxY]] rectangular boundary in flow coords constraining movement; defaults unbounded.
terminal_note: A min/max rect clamp on position — pure arithmetic. Spec §9 names the 'coordinate extent' for child clamping, but no extent type or clamp is implemented.
evidence: Spec §9 'Optional coordinate extent clamps a child within its parent.' No extent struct or move-clamp in code: flow_move_node stores pos verbatim (src/flow_input.h:214,220); grep 'extent' src/ → only unrelated comment (flow_input.h:56).

name: ZIndexMode (auto/basic/manual)
description: ZIndexMode = auto|basic|manual. auto = manage z for selections AND sub-flows; basic = selections only; manual = no automatic z.
terminal_note: flow's draw order is effectively a fixed 'auto'-like policy (depth for sub-flows + selected-last among siblings) but it is not configurable and there is no ZIndexMode enum or manual zIndex honoring. Not spec'd.
evidence: flow__node_order sorts by (depth, selected, index) with no mode switch (src/flow_model.h:614-627); no ZIndexMode type, no manual-z path. Absent from spec.

name: InternalNode.internals (engine-private node state)
description: InternalNode extends Node with internals: positionAbsolute, z, rootParentIndex, userNode, handleBounds, bounds, measured.
terminal_note: flow keeps the same engine-private derivations but RECOMPUTES them each frame rather than caching them in a per-node internals struct: absolute pos, effective z (order), handle anchors, bounds. Same information, no cache struct.
evidence: flow_node_abs (positionAbsolute), flow__node_order (effective z), flow_handle_anchor (handleBounds), flow_node_rect_abs (bounds) — all in src/flow_model.h:49,614,78,50. Delta: computed per frame, not stored in an internals struct.

name: InternalNode.internals.positionAbsolute
description: Cached absolute (flow-space) position after applying all parent transforms and origin.
terminal_note: flow_node_abs computes it by walking the parent chain each call (not cached); origin is fixed at top-left. Same value, recomputed.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:49 `flow_pt flow_node_abs(flow_t*, const flow_node*)` walks the parent chain (src/flow_model.h:216 ref). Used by render/hit-test. Delta: not cached.

name: InternalNode.internals.z (effective z-index)
description: Resolved/effective z used for stacking, derived from zIndex + selection + sub-flow rules, distinct from user zIndex.
terminal_note: flow computes the effective draw order from depth (sub-flow) + selected-last, which IS the resolved z — even though there is no user zIndex to combine. The effective-order key is implemented; the user zIndex input is not.
evidence: flow__node_order builds the effective order key (depth asc, unselected-before-selected, index) per pass (src/flow_model.h:614-627; cmp at flow__order_cmp_render); render iterates it (src/flow_render.h:199-201). No user zIndex term.

name: InternalNode.internals.handleBounds
description: Cached NodeHandleBounds — measured positions/sizes of the node's source/target handles, for connection hit-testing and routing.
terminal_note: flow computes each handle's anchor cell analytically (flow_handle_anchor) for both hit-test and routing, recomputed each frame instead of cached.
evidence: flow_handle_anchor returns the world-abs anchor cell per handle (src/flow_model.h:78); used by flow_hit_handle (src/flow_model.h:81) and edge end resolution (src/flow_render.h flow__edge_screen_ends). Delta: recomputed, not cached.

name: InternalNode.internals.bounds & rootParentIndex
description: bounds: cached bounding rect; rootParentIndex: index of the root parent in a nested hierarchy, for ordering sub-flows.
terminal_note: bounds = flow_node_rect_abs (computed). rootParentIndex's purpose — ordering nested sub-flows — is met by the depth key in flow__node_order rather than a stored root index.
evidence: flow_node_rect_abs gives the abs bounding rect (src/flow_model.h:50); nested ordering via flow__node_depth feeding flow__node_order (src/flow_model.h:475,614). Delta: depth key instead of a cached rootParentIndex.

name: InternalNode.internals.userNode
description: Reference back to the original user Node object that this InternalNode wraps, keeping engine internals separate from user data.
terminal_note: flow has no internal/user split — there is one flow_node struct holding both durable fields and engine flags, since it never needs an immutable user copy. The separation pattern is moot, not implemented as a back-pointer.
evidence: Single flow_node struct mixes durable (id/type/pos/parent/data) and engine (flags) fields (src/flow_model.h:12); no wrapper struct, no userNode pointer. The split exists only to mirror React immutability; flow mutates in place (spec §1 'we own one store and mutate it directly'). Not in spec as a structure.

name: Utility class: nodrag
description: CSS class on an element inside a node that prevents it from initiating a node drag, so inputs/buttons stay interactive.
terminal_note: Analog would be marking a sub-region of the node's cell rect as drag-exempt (region-based input routing). flow has no sub-region drag exemption; any node-body press starts a drag. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'nodrag' src/ → 0 hits; drag arms on any down_node press with no sub-region gate (src/flow_input.h:170-178). Absent from spec.

name: Utility class: nopan
