description: CSS class that stops viewport panning when interacting with the element; for fixed content inside the canvas.
terminal_note: Analog would flag a sub-region so a press/drag there does not pan the pane. flow has no such region routing. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'nopan' src/ → 0 hits; pan arms on any empty-pane press (src/flow_input.h:179-181) with no region exemption. Absent from spec.

name: Utility class: nowheel
description: CSS class that disables the canvas's wheel pan/zoom over the element so its own content can scroll.
terminal_note: Analog would route wheel/scroll (or PgUp/PgDn) to an inner scrollable node region instead of pan/zoom when the cursor is over it. flow has no scrollable node regions or wheel-region routing. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'nowheel' src/ → 0 hits; wheel events drive pan/zoom globally (flow_feed scroll handling) with no per-region override. Absent from spec.

name: Utility class names are configurable
description: The nodrag/nopan/nowheel class names can be customized via React Flow's style/props configuration.
terminal_note: Customizing CSS class strings is DOM-specific; flow has no CSS classes at all. The underlying region-exempt behavior (itself unbuilt) is what would port, never a class name.
evidence: No CSS class concept anywhere in src/ (grep nodrag/nopan/nowheel → 0). Cells carry only fg/bg/attr (src/flow_cell.h). Drop per terminal_fit 'no'.

name: Example: interactive custom node (color selector)
description: Custom node with a color picker that updates node data immutably and drives the flow background; sits between input/output with animated edges.
terminal_note: flow custom node types exist and the topo demo has an interactive (right-click details) node, but there is no in-node focusable widget that emits a data-change event back into the graph; no animated edges. The interactive-inner-control example is not reproduced.
evidence: Custom types work (demos/topo.c DEVICE), but no in-node widget/data-change-emit mechanism (render has no event return path; src/flow_model.h:19). No animated edges (spec §1 non-goal). Example not in spec/workpackages.

name: Example: drag handle restriction
description: dragHandle so only a designated sub-element (e.g. header bar) drags the node; nodrag complements it for elements within the handle.
terminal_note: Analog is designating one row/region of the node box as the only drag-initiating zone (region hit-test). flow has no sub-region drag restriction. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: Same as Node.dragHandle: drag arms on any node-body press (src/flow_input.h:170); grep 'dragHandle\|drag_handle' src/ → 0. Absent from spec.

name: Example: hidden nodes toggle (collapsible diagram)
description: Uses the hidden boolean on nodes AND edges, toggled by a checkbox, to show/hide the whole graph reactively — basis for expand/collapse.
terminal_note: Would be a per-node/edge visibility flag skipped in draw+hit-test with edge cascade — fully portable, but flow has no hidden flag, so the example cannot be reproduced. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: Depends on Node.hidden which is absent (grep 'hidden' src/ → 0; flags only SELECTED|DRAGGING|HOVERED, src/flow_model.h:2). No visibility toggle. Absent from spec.

name: Node intersection detection (getIntersectingNodes)
description: getIntersectingNodes(node) returns all nodes spatially overlapping the given node; example highlights them during onNodeDrag.
terminal_note: Trivial AABB overlap over node bounding rects (flow_rect_intersects + flow_node_rect_abs already exist), but no getIntersectingNodes API is wired and no onNodeDrag-highlight. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: flow_rect_intersects (src/flow_geom.h:30) + flow_node_rect_abs (src/flow_model.h:50) are the primitives, but grep 'getIntersect\|intersecting' src/ → 0; no node-vs-node query API. Absent from spec.

name: isNodeIntersecting helper
description: Companion helper testing whether a node intersects a given rect/area (vs getIntersectingNodes returning all overlapping nodes).
source: example reference to instance intersection helpers — https://reactflow.dev/examples/nodes/intersections
terminal_note: The AABB primitive flow_rect_intersects exists and is used for marquee, but there is no public node-vs-rect intersection helper exposed for app use. Not built as such, not spec'd.
evidence: flow_rect_intersects exists and is used by flow_select_in_rect/marquee (src/flow_geom.h:30, src/flow_model.h:716), but no isNodeIntersecting public API named for node-vs-rect. Absent from spec.

name: Example: shape nodes via centralized SVG Shape component (Pro)
description: One node type renders flow-chart shapes (circle/diamond/hexagon/rectangle) by switching SVG path on a shape-kind data field; sidebar + minimap nodes + toolbar.
terminal_note: A single shape-node type keyed on a shape-kind field ports; rectangles are native box-drawing and circle/diamond/hexagon would be ASCII/Unicode approximations. flow ships no shape node type and no shape-kind field. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: Only `default` (titled box) and `group` (frame) types exist (src/flow_types.h); no shape type, no shape-kind data convention, no SVG analog. Absent from spec/workpackages.

name: Example: update node data/properties dynamically
description: Updating label/style/visibility via setNodes with an immutable map (match id, return new object); reference-equality requirement.
terminal_note: In C the update-by-id pattern is direct in-place mutation: flow_get_node(f,id) then write fields, or flow_set_edge_label / flow_move_node; re-measure on size-affecting changes. The React immutability requirement vanishes.
evidence: flow_get_node(f,id) returns a mutable pointer (src/flow_model.h:43); mutators update by id (flow_move_node src/flow_model.h:41, flow_set_edge_label src/flow_model.h:107); flow_measure_node re-derives size (src/flow_model.h:39). In-place, no immutability dance. (visibility/hidden not supported — see Node.hidden.)

name: Example: rotatable node (CSS transform rotation)
description: Custom node rotated via CSS transform: rotate(); a rotate handle changes angle and useUpdateNodeInternals realigns connectors.
terminal_note: Arbitrary-angle rotation of a character-cell box is infeasible (cells are axis-aligned); only a few orientations could be faked. Nearest analog is the already-frame-recomputed handle anchors, but free rotation itself does not port.
evidence: Cells are axis-aligned, glyph = one cell (spec §1 non-goals, §16); no transform/rotation in the compositor (src/flow_render.h). grep 'rotat' src/ → 0. Drop free rotation per terminal_fit 'no'; workpackages list nothing for it.

name: Example: node position animation (Pro)
description: Animates node positions between layouts via lerp + d3-timer in a useAnimatedNodes hook; setNodes per frame, tween between layouts.
terminal_note: Lerping positions over a frame loop would quantize to cells (steppy) but is feasible; flow has no animation loop or tweening, and spec §1/§16 cut 'animation smoothness beyond the cell grid'. Not built, and animation tweening is explicitly a non-goal.
evidence: Spec §1 non-goals 'Animation smoothness beyond the cell grid ... is out of scope'; §16 confirms. No per-frame position tween in code (flow_move_node is instant; src/flow_input.h). grep 'lerp\|animate' src/ → 0.

name: Example: delete-middle-node graph repair
description: onNodesDelete repairs a chain: getConnectedEdges/getIncomers/getOutgoers, then bridge each incomer to each outgoer (A->B->C, delete B => A->C).
terminal_note: on_nodes_delete fires with the removed id set, so an app could do the bridge surgery via the public edge mutators; but flow ships no getIncomers/getOutgoers/getConnectedEdges helpers, so the app must scan the edge array itself. The callback exists; the graph-traversal helpers do not. Not spec'd.
evidence: on_nodes_delete callback exists (src/flow_model.h:188, events commit 142fcde) and flow_add_edge/flow_remove_edge are public, but no getIncomers/getOutgoers/getConnectedEdges accessors in src/flow_model.h. The repair helpers are absent from spec/workpackages.

name: UI: BaseNode (shadcn styled node wrapper)
description: A node wrapper with shared styling and sub-components header/content/footer for consistent design across nodes.
terminal_note: Maps to a reusable box renderer with header/content/footer cell regions and a shared border theme. flow's default/group types draw bordered boxes but there is no shared header/content/footer panel helper. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: flow_box draws a frame and flow__default_render makes a titled box (src/flow_cell.h flow_box, src/flow_types.h:13-22), but no reusable header/content/footer region component. Absent from spec/workpackages (UI shadcn set not ported).

name: UI: NodeTooltip (hover tooltip on node)
description: Wrapper (built on NodeToolbar) showing a tooltip on hover; sub-components trigger/content; position prop sets placement.
terminal_note: Analog is a popup shown on focus/selection placed on a chosen side of the node rect (flow does track FLOW_HOVERED, so a hover-tooltip is feasible). But no tooltip/toolbar component exists. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'tooltip\|toolbar' src/ → 0 hits; FLOW_HOVERED exists (src/flow_model.h:82) but only reveals handle markers (src/flow_render.h:216-218), no tooltip popup. Absent from spec.

name: UI: NodeStatusIndicator (status wrapper)
description: Wraps a node to show state initial/loading/success/error with a border ring or overlay spinner variant.
terminal_note: A colored status border + cycled spinner glyph is feasible, but needs a frame loop for the spinner; flow has no status wrapper, no animation loop, and animation smoothness is a §1 non-goal. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: No status/spinner component in src/ (grep 'status_indicator\|spinner' → 0; flow_set_statusbar is the help line, unrelated, src/flow_model.h:121). Spinner needs a frame loop (animation cut, spec §1/§16). Absent from spec.

name: UI: PlaceholderNode (click-to-create)
description: A dashed/ghost node clicked to create a new node; accepts children like a '+'; registered as placeholderNode type.
terminal_note: Fully portable as a custom type drawn with a dashed/dim box and a '+' glyph whose click spawns a real node — but flow ships no placeholder type and flow_box has no dashed style. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: grep 'placeholder' src/ → 0 hits; no dashed-border box style in flow_box (src/flow_cell.h); no click-to-create node type. Absent from spec/workpackages.

name: UI: DatabaseSchemaNode (per-row column handles)
description: A node visualizing a DB table: header + rows, each row with a LEFT target and RIGHT source handle for column-level connections.
terminal_note: Per-row left/right handles are expressible via the handle.along offset (multiple handles per side), so a schema node is buildable as a custom type — but flow ships no such type. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: flow_handle.along supports multiple handles per side (src/flow_model.h:10), so per-row handles are feasible, but no DatabaseSchema node type exists (only default+group, src/flow_types.h). Absent from spec/workpackages.

name: UI: NodeAppendix (absolutely-positioned attachment)
description: Wrapper appending content to a node in an absolutely-positioned container anchored to a node edge (badges, labels, status panels).
terminal_note: An extra cell strip adjacent to a chosen side of the node rect is portable, but flow has no appendix/attachment component; surfaces are clipped to the node rect, so an overflow strip would need engine support. Not built, not spec'd.
evidence: No appendix/attachment helper in src/ (grep 'appendix' → 0); node surfaces are clipped to footprint (src/flow_render.h:142-162), with no adjacent-strip region. Absent from spec/workpackages.

name: Self-connecting (loop-back) edge for source === target
description: A custom edge component that handles the degenerate case where an edge's source and target are the same node (a self-loop), for which the default bezier/smoothstep path algorithms collapse. The component is a conditional wrapper: when props.source !== props.target it simply renders the built-in <BezierEdge {...props} /> unchanged; only when source === target does it build a custom path. The custom path is a single SVG elliptical-arc ('A') command that loops out and back to the same node: edgePath = `M ${sourceX - 5} ${sourceY} A ${radiusX} ${radiusY} 0 1 0 ${targetX + 2} ${targetY}` with radiusX = (sourceX - targetX) * 0.6 and radiusY = 50, large-arc-flag = 1 and sweep-flag = 0, and the endpoints nudged slightly (sourceX - 5, targetX + 2) so the arc clears the node. It is rendered via <BaseEdge path={edgePath} markerEnd={markerEnd} />. The self-loop edge in the example also carries markerEnd: { type: MarkerType.Arrow }. Registered as edge type 'selfconnecting'.
terminal_note: Concept maps: detect source===target and draw a loop back to the same node instead of letting the straight/orthogonal router degenerate to a zero-length path. But the smooth elliptical SVG arc has no faithful terminal analog; in a character grid it must degrade to a small fixed box-drawing or braille loop glyph anchored beside the node (e.g. a ╭─╮/╰─╯ stub or a single 'self-loop' badge), with arrowhead approximated by a directional char; sub-cell radii (radiusX*0.6, radiusY=50px) collapse to a couple of cells.
evidence: NOT implemented, and the precondition is actively forbidden: flow rejects self-edges everywhere. flow_add_edge: src/flow_model.h:747 `if (nsrc == ntgt) return;` (validation port noted at spec §4 line 214 'reject self-edges'); flow_reconnect_edge revalidates and rejects (tests/test_edge.c:85-88); flow_end_connection rejects (tests/test_connect.c:176-178 'self-connection rejected'). So the store CANNOT hold a source==target edge. No self-loop router/glyph exists — the only two registered routers are flow_route_orthogonal and flow_route_straight (src/flow_route.h:9-10). Repo-wide grep across src/ demos/ tests/ docs/ for self-loop/selfconnect/loopback → no hits beyond the rejection logic above. Not in the spec, not in the inc-3 workpackages, and NOT listed under 'Deferred beyond increment 3' (workpackages lines 148-156 cut only bezier/smoothstep/wide-glyphs/zoom-tween, not self-loops) — so it is a genuine future-increment candidate, not a deliberate cut. Spec §1 line 31 cuts bezier/smoothstep ('collapse to box-drawing corners') but says nothing about a degenerate self-loop case.

name: Bidirectional offset edge (reciprocal edges on separate, non-overlapping tracks)
description: A custom edge component that draws two reciprocal edges between the same pair of nodes (A->B and B->A) on offset paths so they do not overlap. It detects the bidirectional case at render time with useStore: isBiDirectionEdge is true when the store's edges array contains an edge whose source/target are the reverse of this edge (s.edges.some(e => (e.source === target && e.target === source) || (e.target === source && e.source === target))). When bidirectional, it calls getSpecialPath(params, offset) which builds a quadratic-bezier ('Q') path with the control point offset in Y at the midpoint: `M ${sourceX} ${sourceY} Q ${centerX} ${centerY + offset} ${targetX} ${targetY}`, where centerX/centerY are the midpoint of the endpoints; the offset is +25 or -25 chosen by direction (sourceX < targetX ? 25 : -25) so the two reciprocal edges bow to opposite sides. When NOT bidirectional it falls back to the standard [path] = getBezierPath(edgePathParams). Rendered via <BaseEdge path={path} markerEnd={markerEnd} />, registered as edge type 'bidirectional'. The setup also relies on a paired custom node (BiDirectionalNode) exposing two source handles, id='left' (Position.Left) and id='right' (Position.Right), plus ConnectionMode.Loose; the two edges use mirrored sourceHandle/targetHandle ('right'/'left' vs 'left'/'right') and each carries markerEnd: { type: MarkerType.ArrowClosed }.
source: example (BiDirectionalEdge.tsx + BiDirectionalNode.tsx, pattern: bidirectional offset edge) — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/custom-edges
terminal_note: Concept maps: detect a reverse-direction edge already exists between the same pair and route the two reciprocal edges on separate tracks so they don't draw on top of each other (offset by one cell row/column in the terminal grid), each with its own directional arrowhead. But the quadratic-bezier midpoint Y-offset of +/-25px has no smooth terminal analog; in a cell grid it degrades to two parallel orthogonal/straight paths separated by a fixed cell offset, and the dual-handle node (two source handles left/right + loose connection mode) maps only roughly since terminal handles are coarse anchor cells.
evidence: NOT implemented, and architecturally blocked by the router contract: the edge-type route vtable signature is `void (*route)(flow_pt s, flow_pos sp, flow_pt t, flow_pos tp, flow_route *out)` (src/flow_model.h:25) — it receives ONLY the single edge's two endpoints, with no flow_t* and no edge list, so unlike xyflow's useStore-based detection it physically cannot see whether a reverse edge exists. The render edge loop iterates one edge at a time and calls et->route per edge (src/flow_render.h:123 + the et=flow_edge_type_for/route resolution), with no reciprocal-pair detection or track offsetting. Repo-wide grep across src/ demos/ tests/ docs/ for bidirect/reciprocal/offset-track → no hits (the 'offset' matches are unrelated: handle 'along' offset, viewport offset, drag grab-offset). Both built-in routers (flow_route_orthogonal, flow_route_straight, src/flow_route.h) draw a single direct/stepped path with no awareness of other edges. Not in the spec, not in the inc-3 workpackages, and not in 'Deferred beyond increment 3' — a genuine future-increment candidate (would require an API/router-contract change to give the router store access, not merely a new router).

name: No built-in layouting
description: React Flow ships no layout algorithm; node positions are always explicit x/y. Layouting is delegated to external libraries the docs survey: dagre, d3-hierarchy, d3-force, elkjs, d3-flextree, entitree-flex, Cola.js.
terminal_note: A C port likewise needs its own positioning; explicit integer cell coordinates per node map directly to a TUI grid.
evidence: Spec §10 deliberately mirrors xyflow: 'xyflow does NOT bundle layout … a faithful port likewise treats layout as a consumer convenience that operates through the public mutators (flow_move_node)'. WP#8 builds two opt-in algorithms; positions are explicit integer cells (flow_node.pos, src/flow_model.h:175-183). Layout itself not yet landed (no src/flow_layout.h).

name: Dagre tree layout integration
description: Drop-in directed-graph tree layout. getLayoutedElements resets a dagre graph, sets rankdir direction (TB/BT/LR/RL), feeds each node's width/height, runs dagre.layout, then maps dagre center-anchored positions back to React Flow top-left anchors. Configurable spacing/alignment. Supports dynamic node sizes and (buggy) sub-flow layouting; no edge routing.
terminal_note: Layered tree algorithm ports to C (compute ranks, order, coordinates). Node cell-dimensions replace pixel measurement.
evidence: Spec §10 layered layout (FLOW_LAYOUT_LAYERED = Sugiyama longest-path rank + barycenter ordering, dir FLOW_LR/FLOW_TB, gap_x/gap_y) is the dagre analog. WP#8 lines 1179-1186. NOT landed: grep 'flow_layout' src/ → no hits; no src/flow_layout.h.

name: Dagre direction switching (vertical/horizontal)
description: onLayout('TB') vs onLayout('LR') re-runs layout with a direction arg; node sourcePosition/targetPosition flip (top/bottom vs left/right) to match flow direction. Buttons in a Panel trigger relayout.
terminal_note: Direction enum and re-layout on a keypress are pure logic; trivial in a TUI.
evidence: WP#8 API additions (workpackages lines 1124-1145) define `flow_layered_dir { FLOW_LR, FLOW_TB }` and `flow_layout_layered(f, dir, gap_x, gap_y)`. Spec §10. Direction switching is literally the dir enum. NOT landed (no src/flow_layout.h).

name: Static vs dynamic layout distinction
description: The dagre example is static: layout runs once and does not recompute when nodes/edges change. Dynamic layouting requires re-invoking the layout function on graph changes (see auto-layout pro example).
terminal_note: When to recompute (on mutation vs on demand) is a caller decision; identical in a TUI.
evidence: Spec §10 makes flow_layout a consumer-invoked transform through mutators (caller decides when to call); WP#9 flowchart-demo wires it to startup + an 'l' key (on-demand re-layout). NOT landed: flow_layout absent (grep → no hits).

name: d3-hierarchy tree layouts
description: For single-root trees: tree, tree maps, partition layouts, enclosure (pack) diagrams. Requires exactly one root; assigns identical width/height to all nodes during layout. No dynamic node sizes.
terminal_note: Tidy-tree (Reingold-Tilford) ports well; treemap/pack are area-based and awkward at coarse cell resolution.
evidence: Distinct algorithm not in flow's two-algorithm scope (spec §10 commits to force + layered only). grep 'hierarchy\|treemap\|partition\|pack' src/ → no hits. Genuine future candidate.

name: d3-force physics layout
description: Iterative force-directed layout via a useLayoutedElements hook. Uses getNodes/getEdges; gated by store initialized selector. Multiple forces combined; a tick advances the sim and writes positions back. Includes drag-during-simulation handling and an on/off toggle since recomputing every frame is expensive.
terminal_note: Force sim (charge/link/center forces, velocity integration) is pure numeric and ports; continuous per-frame animation is heavy for a TUI, so run to convergence then snap.
evidence: Spec §10 FLOW_LAYOUT_FORCE = Fruchterman-Reingold, runs to convergence then rounds to cells (WP#8 lines 1171-1177 'compute in float, round to cells on commit'). This is the force-directed analog. NOT landed (no src/flow_layout.h).

name: Rectangular collision force (custom)
description: d3-force's built-in collide force assumes circular nodes; the docs ship a custom collision.js force for rectangular nodes, preventing rectangular node overlap.
terminal_note: Rectangular AABB overlap resolution is integer-friendly and maps perfectly to character-cell node boxes.
evidence: Not in spec §10 (force + layered only; no overlap-resolution force). flow_geom has flow_rect_intersects but no collision-force/separation pass: grep 'collision\|collide\|overlap\|resolveCollisions' src/ → no hits. Genuine future candidate.

name: elkjs advanced layout
description: Eclipse Layout Kernel ported to JS. Async layout via a hook. Highly configurable: elk.algorithm, layer spacing, elk.direction. Supports dynamic node sizes, sub-flow layouting, AND edge routing. Returns x/y on children.
terminal_note: ELK's layered algorithm is reimplementable but large; orthogonal edge routing is the valuable part for a TUI.
evidence: ELK as a distinct engine is not in flow's scope; spec §10 ships only Fruchterman-Reingold + Sugiyama, not the ELK kernel. grep 'elk' src/ → no hits. The layered analog is covered separately by FLOW_LAYOUT_LAYERED; ELK specifically is a candidate.

name: elkjs multiple handles / ports (FIXED_ORDER)
description: Configures elkjs to route edges to specific handles ('ports'). Requires unique handle/port ids, ports declared per node with a side property, and portConstraints:FIXED_ORDER. Reduces edge crossings.
terminal_note: Port-constrained routing (assign each edge an ordered entry/exit slot per side) is valuable for clean box-char edges; multiple handles map to multiple connection cells on a box edge.
evidence: flow has per-node handles (flow_handle, src/flow_model.h with id/kind/pos/along), but no port-constrained layout/routing or ELK. Not in spec §10. grep 'elk\|portConstraint\|FIXED_ORDER' src/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: entitree-flex tree layout
description: Tree layout supporting sibling nodes and nodes with differing dimensions (flextree-style), suited to family trees. Uses layoutElements(tree, rootId, direction TB/LR), a custom node type, and direction buttons.
terminal_note: Variable-size tidy-tree (flextree) ports to C and suits a TUI where node widths differ by label length.
evidence: Distinct algorithm; spec §10 commits to force + layered only. grep 'flextree\|entitree' src/ → no hits. Candidate. (flow's layered uses real node w/h per WP#8, but flextree tidy-tree specifically is a separate algorithm.)

name: d3-flextree / Cola.js (honourable mentions)
description: d3-flextree: tidy trees with variable node dimensions. Cola.js (WebCola): constraint-based layouts. Listed as alternatives not given dedicated examples.
terminal_note: Both portable to C; constraint-based solvers (Cola) are heavier. Reference candidates for a TUI layout engine.
evidence: Neither in spec §10's two-algorithm scope. grep 'flextree\|cola' src/ → no hits. Genuine future candidates for a richer layout engine.

name: Edge routing libraries
description: Routing edges is separate from node layout. Suggested: react-flow-smart-edge, orthogonal connector routing, elkjs routing. The editable-edge pro example can be a base for custom routed edges.
terminal_note: Orthogonal edge routing is the natural fit for box-drawing-character edges; a grid router over cells is the analog.
evidence: flow ships orthogonal (flow_route_orthogonal, src/flow_route.h:34) AND straight (flow_route_straight, src/flow_route.h:70) built-in routers, both registered (flow_register_defaults, src/flow_types.h:51). Edge type vtable lets consumers add custom routers (flow_register_edge_type, spec §5). Smart/A* grid routing past these two is not built, but per-edge routing exists.

name: Auto-layout hook with switchable engines (pro)
description: Pro example: a reusable useAutoLayout hook re-arranging nodes after additions, switchable at runtime between dagre, d3-hierarchy, and elk. Controls expose algorithm, direction, spacing x/y.
terminal_note: A pluggable layout dispatcher (engine + direction + spacing) is clean for a TUI; runtime switching is trivial.
evidence: WP#8 defines exactly this dispatcher: flow_layout(f, flow_layout_opts) with a mode enum (FLOW_LAYOUT_FORCE/FLOW_LAYOUT_LAYERED), direction (FLOW_LR/FLOW_TB), and gap_x/gap_y spacing (workpackages lines 1124-1145). Two engines, not dagre/hierarchy/elk, but the same switchable-dispatcher pattern. NOT landed (no src/flow_layout.h).

name: Dynamic layouting with placeholder nodes (pro)
description: Pro example: self-organizing vertical tree. Users add nodes by clicking placeholder elements, convert placeholders to nodes, or insert between nodes via a + button on edges. A d3-hierarchy auto-layout hook recalculates positions on change.
terminal_note: Placeholder-driven construction + tree recompute port fully; position animation degrades to instant snap.
evidence: Placeholder-node UX pattern is not in spec/WP. grep 'placeholder' src/ → no hits; relies on a d3-hierarchy engine flow doesn't have. Candidate (the recompute-on-change half overlaps the specd flow_layout, but the placeholder interaction is unscoped).

name: Expand/collapse tree with useExpandCollapse (pro)
description: Pro example: hierarchical tree where nodes expand/collapse on click, revealing/hiding descendants. A hook keeps the full graph in memory but renders only visible nodes; dagre re-layouts on change. Tracks expanded state in node.data.
terminal_note: Expand/collapse + hidden-subtree culling is ideal for a TUI (collapse triangles like a file tree); pure data/logic.
evidence: No hidden/collapse field on flow_node (src/flow_model.h:175-183 has id/type/pos/parent/w/h/data/flags; flags are SELECTED/DRAGGING/HOVERED only). grep 'collapse\|hidden\|expand' src/flow_model.h → no hits (LOD 'collapse' at low zoom is unrelated). Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Force layout: non-overlapping node insertion (pro)
description: Pro example integrating d3-force so newly added nodes never overlap existing ones. Interactive strength and distance sliders tune force strength and inter-node distance.
terminal_note: Collision-avoiding placement of new nodes ports as an AABB push-apart pass; one-shot resolve on insert is the practical TUI analog.
evidence: The force-simulation core is spec §10 FLOW_LAYOUT_FORCE (Fruchterman-Reingold with repulsion keeping nodes apart, WP#8 lines 1171-1177). NOT landed (no src/flow_layout.h). The on-insert/slider variant is a specific application of that specd engine.

name: Node collision auto-resolution
description: resolveCollisions(nodes, {maxIterations, overlapThreshold, margin}) detects overlapping/too-close nodes and moves them apart. Run on onNodeDragStop. margin e.g. 15px.
terminal_note: Iterative AABB separation with a cell margin is a strong TUI fit; trigger on drag-end.
evidence: No collision-resolution pass on drag-end; flow_handle_mouse release branch (src/flow_input.h:179+) reparents but does not separate overlaps. grep 'resolveCollision\|overlapThreshold\|separate' src/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate (distinct from the FR repulsion inside the specd layout).

name: Horizontal / mixed flow direction via handle positions
description: Diagram direction controlled per node by sourcePosition and targetPosition (left/right/top/bottom). source=right/target=left yields left-to-right flow; mixed per-node allowed. Edges use smoothstep; positions still manually authored.
terminal_note: Handle-side selection (which cell of a node box an edge attaches to) is core; left/right/top/bottom map to node-box edge cells.
evidence: flow_handle carries `flow_pos pos` (FLOW_TOP/RIGHT/BOTTOM/LEFT) + `along` offset (src/flow_model.h handle struct); anchors computed analytically per side (spec §5: 'TOP → (wx+w/2, wy), RIGHT → (wx+w, wy+h/2)'). Default node type declares N/E/S/W handles (flow_default_handles, flow_types.h). Per-node handle-side selection is live.

name: Sub-flow / parentId mechanics
description: A node becomes a child via parentId. Child position becomes relative to parent ({0,0}=parent top-left). Moving the parent moves children; children are positioned relatively, not DOM children. Parent must appear before children in the array.
terminal_note: Parent-relative coords and parent-before-child ordering map directly to a TUI scene graph.
evidence: flow_node has `int parent` (src/flow_model.h:179); flow_set_parent (flow_model.h:488) converts abs→relative preserving world position; flow_node_abs walks the parent chain (flow_model.h:565); dragging a parent moves children via relative coords; render z-order draws parent-before-child (groups commit 438ea0d, depth-aware ordering).

name: extent: 'parent' child clamping
description: Setting extent:'parent' on a child prevents dragging/positioning it outside its parent's bounds. Without it, children can move outside the parent.
terminal_note: Clamping a child's cell position to the parent's bounding box is trivial integer math.
evidence: Spec §9 lists 'Optional coordinate extent clamps a child within its parent' but groups landed (438ea0d) WITHOUT it: grep 'extent' src/flow_model.h src/flow_input.h → only an unrelated auto-pan 'extent' comment (flow_input.h:56); no parent-bounds clamp in flow_set_parent or the drag path. Specd, not yet built.

name: group node type
description: type:'group' is a convenience node type with no handles, used as a parent container styled with width/height. Any node type can be a parent — group is just handle-less. Default types can also be parents.
terminal_note: A handle-less container box is straightforward in a TUI (bordered region with a title; children render inside).
evidence: flow_group_node_type = {"group", flow__group_measure, flow__group_render, NULL, 0, ...} with handle_count 0 (src/flow_types.h:46); registered by flow_register_defaults (flow_types.h:49). flow__group_render draws a box frame; any node can be a parent via the generic `parent` field.

name: Nested sub-flows (multi-level groups)
description: Groups nest arbitrarily (Group B contains Group B.A which contains nodes); each level uses parentId. Edges can connect within a group and cross from a sub-flow to outer nodes.
terminal_note: Recursive containment with relative offsets is a standard scene tree; renders with nested bordered regions plus clipping.
evidence: flow_node_abs recursively walks the full parent chain (src/flow_model.h:565); flow__node_depth/depth-aware render order handle arbitrary nesting (groups commit 438ea0d). test_groups.c asserts a→b→c 3-level abs == sum of three relative positions. Edges reference node ids regardless of nesting.

name: draggable:false on child nodes
description: Children can be made non-draggable (draggable:false) so they stay fixed within a default-type parent while the parent moves.
terminal_note: Per-node draggable flag is a boolean gate on drag input; trivial in a TUI.
evidence: No per-node draggable flag: grep 'draggable' src/ → no hits. flow_node.flags are only SELECTED/DRAGGING/HOVERED (src/flow_model.h enum). Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Edge z-index over parented nodes
description: Edges render below nodes by default, but edges connected to a node with a parent render ABOVE nodes; as of 12.8.0 edges within a subflow render above nodes. Customizable via the edge zIndex option.
terminal_note: TUI z-order is paint order over the cell buffer; edge-vs-node layering is a draw-sequence decision but box-char edges over node content reduce legibility.
evidence: flow's compose z-order is fixed: background → group containers → edges → nodes → handles → preview/marquee → minimap → overlay (spec §6, confirmed src/flow_render.h order). Edges always draw under nodes; no per-edge zIndex or parented-edge-above rule. grep 'zIndex\|z_index\|zindex' src/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: Dynamic parent-child attach/detach by drag (pro)
description: Pro example: drag a node over a group to attach it as a child; a node-toolbar detach button removes it. Handles absolute<->relative coordinate conversion when reparenting.
terminal_note: Drop-into-group hit testing and absolute<->relative transforms are pure integer math; reparent on drop ports cleanly.
evidence: flow_handle_mouse release branch implements drag-to-reparent: on single-node drop, hit-test for a group under the node (skipping self/descendants) and flow_set_parent preserving abs; drop on empty pane detaches (src/flow_input.h:179-218, groups commit 438ea0d). flow_set_parent does the abs<->relative conversion (flow_model.h:488-499).

name: Selection-box grouping / ungrouping (pro)
description: Pro example: select multiple nodes, then a Group Nodes button wraps them in a group; selecting a group shows Ungroup. Group nodes are resizable and auto-size to fit children.
terminal_note: Compute bbox of selected nodes, create container, reparent — all integer math. Auto-resize to children fits TUI box layout.
evidence: flow_group(f, ids, n) creates a group container sized from the member world bbox + padding and reparents each (src/flow_model.h:506-524); flow_ungroup dissolves the container, reparenting children out and surviving them (flow_model.h:529-538). test_groups.c verifies group/ungroup. The Group/Ungroup buttons are app UI; the engine mutators exist. (Live container auto-resize on child move is not continuous; sized at group time.)

name: Labeled Group Node UI component
description: shadcn-registry GroupNode component: a group container with an optional label prop, built on BaseNode. Used as a nodeType with width/height and children via parentId + extent:'parent'.
terminal_note: A titled bordered container is a native TUI widget; the label renders in the top border.
evidence: flow_group_node_type renders a box frame plus an optional label read from node->data (flow__group_render, src/flow_types.h; WP#3 design notes: 'draws just a box frame (+ optional label from node->data)'). WP#9 flowchart-demo specs a 'labeled group container'. The container + label render path is built; extent:'parent' clamping is the only sub-part still specd.

name: Prevent child nodes of different parents overlapping
description: As of 12.9.0, child nodes belonging to different parents no longer overlap each other.
terminal_note: Cross-parent overlap avoidance is collision logic on absolute cell positions.
evidence: No cross-parent overlap-avoidance logic; flow has no overlap-resolution pass at all (grep 'overlap\|collision' src/ → no hits). Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Whiteboard freehand draw (pro)
description: Pro example: draw smooth curves on the pane, adjustable brush/color, paths converted into selectable/resizable custom nodes. Uses perfect-freehand.
terminal_note: Smooth freehand vector curves are inherently sub-cell/SVG; a TUI can only crudely approximate with line-draw characters. Nearest analog: a polyline of box-drawing glyphs.
evidence: Spec §1 non-goal: 'Sub-cell rendering — a glyph cannot be smaller than one cell … curved edges collapse to box-drawing corners.' perfect-freehand polygon hulls are inherently sub-cell. grep 'freehand\|brush\|stroke' src/ → no hits. No terminal fit beyond a coarse glyph polyline.

name: Lasso (freeform) selection
description: <Lasso partial/> overlaid on ReactFlow. Click-drag draws a freeform selection area (canvas, pointer events); selects all enclosed items. partial toggles partially- vs fully-enclosed. Toggle between Lasso and standard Selection.
terminal_note: Point-in-polygon hit testing ports; drawing a freeform lasso path in a TUI is coarse. The full-vs-partial enclosure selection logic is the portable, valuable part — and the rectangular marquee already implements both modes.
evidence: flow has a rectangular marquee with FULL/PARTIAL modes (flow_select_in_rect, src/flow_model.h:702; flow_rect_contains vs flow_rect_intersects at flow_model.h:716), but no freeform polygon lasso: grep 'lasso\|polygon\|point_in' src/ → no hits. Freeform lasso specifically is a candidate (rect marquee is the shipped neighbor).

name: Eraser tool
description: <Eraser/> erases nodes/edges by wiping over them. Collision between eraser trail and elements; edges sampled along the SVG path via getPointAtLength into a polyline. Custom ErasableNode/Edge react to a toBeDeleted flag. Toggle Eraser/Selection.
terminal_note: Trail-vs-node AABB collision ports; edge-path sampling becomes sampling the box-char edge's cell line. The trail is a cursor sweep over cells.
evidence: No eraser tool / trail-collision / toBeDeleted mechanic: grep 'eraser\|toBeDelete\|trail' src/ → no hits. Deletion is via Delete/x on selection (flow_delete_selection) only. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Rectangle / shape drawing tool
description: <RectangleTool/> + a rectangle nodeType. Click-and-drag creates a rectangle node with width/height and a color; for backgrounds/containers/highlighting. Toggle Rectangle/Selection.
terminal_note: Drag-to-draw a bordered rectangle region snapped to cells is a natural TUI primitive; color maps to ANSI color.
evidence: No drag-to-draw rectangle tool / tool-mode. A consumer could register a custom rectangle node type, but the interactive draw-to-create tool is unbuilt: grep 'RectangleTool\|draw_rect\|rect_tool' src/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Tool-mode toggling pattern
description: All whiteboard tools follow the same pattern: a boolean isXActive state conditionally renders the tool, with a Panel button-group toggling between tool mode and Selection Mode. One tool active at a time.
terminal_note: A modal tool state (select/draw/erase) toggled by keys or a status-bar toggle is the standard TUI editor pattern.
evidence: flow has no modal tool-mode state; interaction states (IDLE/PANNING/DRAGGING/CONNECTING/RECONNECTING/MARQUEE, spec §8) are transient gesture states, not user-selectable tool modes. grep 'tool_mode\|tool_active' src/ → no hits. Candidate (the keybinding infra flow_bind_key could host it).

name: Whiteboard library guidance (tldraw/Excalidraw)
description: Docs note React Flow is not a whiteboard tool; for full whiteboard needs they recommend tldraw or Excalidraw.
terminal_note: Reference-only guidance, not an implementable feature.
evidence: Documentation guidance, not a library feature; nothing to port. flow is explicitly a node-graph engine (spec §1 Overview), not a whiteboard, so the recommendation 'use another tool' carries no implementable surface.

name: Undo/redo snapshot history (pro)
description: Pro example: useUndoRedo hook with a snapshot-based past/future stack. Tracks moving, adding, deleting, connecting nodes/edges. Navigate via buttons or keyboard (Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z).
terminal_note: Inverse-op (or snapshot) undo/redo of the model is pure state; keybindings map to terminal key events.
evidence: flow_undo/flow_redo/flow_can_undo/flow_can_redo/flow_set_undo_limit (src/flow_undo.h:124-148, decls flow_model.h:132-136); inverse-op journal with FLOW_CMD_ADD/REMOVE_NODE/EDGE, MOVE, RECONNECT, SET_LABEL, REPARENT (flow_model.h:147-150); gesture coalescing via flow__undo_begin/end; bound to 'u'/Ctrl-r in flow_dispatch_key. 154-assert test_undo.c. Commit 74b6045.

name: Copy / cut / paste of nodes and edges (pro)
description: Pro example: select nodes/edges, then cut/copy/paste them within the graph, duplicating the selected sub-flow.
terminal_note: Serialize selection, offset positions, re-id, reinsert — all data ops. Clipboard is in-process state; keybindings for copy/cut/paste.
evidence: No clipboard / copy / cut / paste of a selection: grep 'clipboard\|flow_copy\|flow_paste\|flow_cut\|duplicate' src/ → no hits (the 'copy' hits are label_copy memory helpers in flow_undo.h). Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Alignment helper lines with snapping (pro)
description: Pro example: as nodes are dragged, horizontal & vertical guide lines appear when sides nearly align, plus snapping for precise positioning. Works correctly with viewport transform/scale.
terminal_note: Edge-alignment detection and snap-to-guide are integer comparisons of node bounds; guides render as full-row/column rule characters. Strong fit.
evidence: No alignment-guide detection or snap-to-guide during drag: grep 'helper\|guide\|align\|snap' src/flow_input.h → no hits (positions are auto-cell-snapped because they are integers, spec §3, but no neighbor-alignment guides). Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Collaborative multiplayer with Yjs (pro)
description: Pro example: realtime multi-user graph via React Flow + Yjs (CRDT) + y-websocket. Synchronized changes and cursor tracking; rooms via URL/flow id; right-click adds nodes.
terminal_note: The graph-sync model ports (a CRDT or server protocol over the C model), but networking + CRDT is a large subsystem far from core TUI rendering. Cursors render as colored cell markers.
evidence: No networking / CRDT / multi-user sync anywhere: grep 'yjs\|crdt\|websocket\|collab\|sync' src/ → no hits. flow is single-header, no-deps, single-user (spec §1). Not in scope. Candidate (large subsystem).

name: Multiplayer architecture guidance
description: Guidance: realtime collaboration needs a sync/conflict-resolution engine. Categories: CRDT/local-first (Yjs, Jazz, Automerge, Loro) vs server-authoritative (Liveblocks, Supabase, Convex, Velt).
terminal_note: Architectural guidance, not code. A C TUI could integrate a CRDT lib but it is a major undertaking; relevant only if collaboration is a goal.
evidence: Pure architectural guidance with no flow surface; no sync engine exists (grep 'crdt\|collab' src/ → no hits). If collaboration ever became a goal it would be a future increment. Candidate.

name: Ephemeral vs durable state sync rules
description: Guidance on which fields to sync. Durable: node id/type/data/position/width/height; edge id/type/data/source/target/handles. Ephemeral (droppable): dragging, in-progress connections, cursors. Never synced: selected, measured.
source: learn page Ephemeral vs Durable tables — https://reactflow.dev/learn/advanced-use/multiplayer
terminal_note: The durable/ephemeral/per-user partition is a clean model-design principle applicable to any C state struct.
evidence: flow's persistence boundary is exactly this partition (spec §4 'Persistence boundary'). flow_save writes durable fields only (id/type/pos/parent/data; edge source/target/handles/type/label; viewport) and never flags/drag/marquee/zmin; test_json.c:369-373 asserts the JSON contains no 'flags'/'selected'/'zmin'/'marquee'. w/h are recomputed via measure, not persisted. Commit f3f87a0.

name: Cursor presence / sharing
description: Other users' cursors (and viewports) shared as purely ephemeral state; can be debounced/smoothed. Losing them doesn't break correctness.
terminal_note: Remote cursors map to colored character markers at cell positions; needs the networking layer to exist first.
evidence: No remote-cursor rendering or presence; requires the absent networking layer. grep 'cursor\|presence' src/ → no hits (terminal cursor is hidden in raw mode). Candidate, gated on multiplayer.

name: Drag-and-drop from sidebar (HTML DnD API)
description: Create nodes by dragging from a sidebar palette onto the pane via the native HTML Drag and Drop API: dataTransfer type, screenToFlowPosition on drop, append a new node. Not well supported on touch.
terminal_note: Concept ports: a palette pane + drop-to-create with screen->canvas mapping. HTML DnD is browser-bound; in a TUI it's a custom mouse-drag-from-sidebar or pick-then-place via keyboard.
evidence: No sidebar palette / drag-to-create-from-palette interaction. Nodes are created via flow_add_node / flow_add_node_center / 'n' key (flow_model.h:378), not a palette drop. grep 'palette\|sidebar\|drop' src/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate. (flow_to_world, the screen->canvas mapping it needs, does exist.)

name: Drag-and-drop via Pointer Events / Neodrag
description: Alternative DnD implementations for cross-device consistency: Pointer Events API or the Neodrag library, since HTML DnD fails on touch.
terminal_note: In a TUI all dragging is synthesized from terminal mouse events, so the browser DnD-vs-pointer distinction collapses to one drag handler.
evidence: Browser-specific input-API choice (HTML DnD vs Pointer Events vs Neodrag) with no terminal analog — flow decodes a single SGR-1006 mouse stream (flow_term.h, src/flow_input.h flow_handle_mouse). The distinction does not exist in a TTY; the nearest analog is flow's single mouse-drag handler, already present.

name: screenToFlowPosition coordinate conversion
description: screenToFlowPosition({x,y}) (formerly project) converts screen/client pixel coords into flow/canvas coords accounting for pan and zoom; central to drop placement. As of 12.10.2 accepts a snapGrid option.
terminal_note: Inverse viewport transform (subtract pan, divide by zoom) is core to any pannable/zoomable TUI canvas; snapGrid rounding is integer-friendly.
evidence: flow_to_world(f, screen) = round((screen - offset)/zoom) and flow_to_screen are the inverse/forward transforms (decls src/flow_model.h:69-70; spec §3). Used throughout flow_handle_mouse for drop placement / hit-testing. Integer-cell positions make snapGrid implicit (spec §3 'snap-to-grid is automatic').

name: Performance: memoize node/edge components
description: Custom node/edge components should be wrapped in React.memo or declared outside the parent so React doesn't trigger unnecessary re-renders.
terminal_note: React memoization doesn't apply, but the principle — only redraw cells whose state changed — maps to dirty-region/diff rendering.
evidence: flow's analog is the damage-diff renderer: flow_present composes the back buffer then emits ANSI only for cells differing from the front buffer (spec §6 step 3 'Damage diff'; src/flow_cell.h diff emitter + render double-buffer). Only changed cells repaint — the structural equivalent of avoiding needless re-renders.

name: Performance: memoize callbacks/objects
description: Functions passed to ReactFlow should use useCallback; arrays/objects like defaultEdgeOptions or snapGrid should use useMemo to avoid new references causing re-renders.
terminal_note: Pure React render-stability concern; a C program holds stable references/structs by default.
evidence: React-reference-stability concern with no terminal analog — flow callbacks are plain function pointers in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188) set once via flow_set_callbacks; there is no render-on-new-reference behavior to defend against. Nearest analog: none needed (C structs are stable by default).

name: Performance: don't read full nodes/edges in components
description: Avoid subscribing to the whole nodes/edges array (changes on every drag/pan/zoom). Store derived data (e.g. selectedNodeIds) separately so components re-render only when that slice changes. Use targeted store selectors.
source: learn page Avoid accessing nodes in components — https://reactflow.dev/learn/advanced-use/performance
terminal_note: Maintain derived/indexed state separately from the master arrays so a panel redraw doesn't depend on the full graph — good architectural guidance for a C store.
evidence: flow keeps the master node/edge vecs but does not maintain a separate derived-selection index; selection lives in node->flags and is gathered on demand (flow_selected_nodes). on_selection_change fires only on actual change (flow_model.h:646-653), which partially realizes the principle, but the 'separate derived state slice' architecture is not built out. Candidate/architectural.

name: Performance: collapse large node trees via hidden
description: For deeply nested graphs, render only a limited subset and toggle node.hidden dynamically to expand/collapse rather than rendering all nodes.
source: learn page Collapse large node trees — https://reactflow.dev/learn/advanced-use/performance
terminal_note: node.hidden culling + on-demand expand is a direct fit; hidden nodes simply aren't painted and skip layout.
evidence: No node.hidden field or culling: grep 'hidden' src/flow_model.h → no hits; flow_node.flags are SELECTED/DRAGGING/HOVERED only. (LOD collapses node detail at low zoom, flow__lod_for at flow_model.h:584, but does not hide nodes by data flag.) Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Performance: simplify node/edge styles
description: Complex CSS (animations, shadows, gradients) on many nodes hurts performance; reduce style complexity for large graphs.
source: learn page Simplify node and edge styles — https://reactflow.dev/learn/advanced-use/performance
terminal_note: CSS-specific; a TUI has no shadows/gradients. The analog (limit per-cell styling cost) is marginal.
evidence: CSS-cost guidance with no terminal analog — flow cells carry only fg/bg (256-color) + attr bitflags bold/reverse/dim/underline (flow_cell, spec §6); there are no shadows/gradients/animations to simplify. Nearest analog: per-cell SGR cost, already minimal and coalesced by the diff emitter.

name: Stress test (large graph rendering)
description: Example renders many nodes/edges via initialElements(rows, cols) (e.g. 15x30) with MiniMap, Controls, Background, and a change-pos button; minZoom=0 to zoom far out.
terminal_note: A scalability benchmark is valuable for a TUI; needs viewport culling. minZoom=0 implies sub-cell node rendering — TUI must clamp min zoom.
evidence: No large-graph stress benchmark/demo: tests are correctness-focused (test_render etc.); no generator of N×M nodes. minZoom is clamped (FLOW_ZOOM_MIN 0.25, flow_model.h:5) so the sub-cell case is already handled, but a stress-test demo/harness is unbuilt. Candidate.

name: Testing: Cypress / Playwright
description: Recommended E2E test tools; no extra setup since they render real DOM (React Flow measures nodes from the DOM to render edges).
terminal_note: A TUI tests via a headless pty harness (snapshot the cell buffer) rather than a browser; the need for end-to-end interaction tests carries over.
evidence: flow's headless analog is built and exceeds it: flow_render composes to an in-memory cell buffer for golden snapshot diffing (spec §6/§13; tests/snapshots/*.txt), and flow_feed injects synthetic SGR sequences for interaction tests (test_input/test_mouse/test_marquee/test_connect/test_groups). No TTY required — the explicit testing strategy of spec §13.

name: Testing: Jest mockReactFlow shim
description: Jest needs DOM measurement mocked: a mockReactFlow() shim overriding ResizeObserver, DOMMatrixReadOnly, offsetWidth/Height, SVGElement.getBBox. Edge tests waitFor measurement; disable d3-drag for mouse tests.
terminal_note: Entirely about faking browser DOM measurement. A C TUI measures nodes from string widths directly — no measurement mocking needed.
evidence: DOM-measurement mocking has no analog: flow_measure_node computes w/h from the node type's measure() (src/flow_model.h:448-450), never from a DOM, so ResizeObserver/getBBox/DOMMatrix shims are unnecessary. Nearest analog: none — the measure() vtable hook makes sizes available synchronously.

name: DOM measurement requirement (measured field)
description: React Flow must render node DOM to measure width/height before rendering edges; measured dimensions are added to each node after measurement (computed, not synced).
terminal_note: In a TUI, node dimensions are known from content/explicit w/h without a measurement round-trip — simpler than the DOM model. measured becomes a pure compute.
evidence: flow replaces DOM measurement with flow_measure_node calling the node type's measure() hook (src/flow_model.h:448-450); w/h are cached on flow_node and recomputed, never DOM-measured (spec §4: 'sizes computed by measure() … collapses xyflow's entire measured/width/height machinery, a browser-only artifact'). No measurement round-trip; edges route off the analytic anchors.

name: DevTools: NodeInspector
description: Copy-paste <NodeInspector/> reads all nodes and renders each node's id, type, and measured w/h via a <NodeInfo/> overlay positioned in the viewport (so it pans/zooms with the flow).
terminal_note: An overlay showing per-node id/type/dimensions tracking the viewport transform is straightforward (draw labels at node cell positions).
evidence: No node-inspector overlay shipped. The hook exists to build one (on_overlay callback gets a flow_surface, flow_model.h:181; accessors flow_get_node/flow_node_abs/flow_to_screen), but flow ships no NodeInspector. grep 'inspector' src/ demos/ → no hits. Candidate (easy via on_overlay).

name: DevTools: ChangeLogger
description: <ChangeLogger/> wraps onNodesChange to intercept and log each NodeChange/EdgeChange as dispatched, using the store. Helps understand the change-event stream.
terminal_note: Logging the change/mutation stream is trivially portable — a debug log panel of mutations. Mirrors this repo's mutator journal.
evidence: flow has the underlying mutation record (the undo journal records every add/remove/move/reconnect/label/reparent, src/flow_undo.h), but no debug logger panel exposes/streams it. grep 'changelog\|ChangeLogger\|log_change' src/ demos/ → no hits. Candidate (the journal is the data source).

name: DevTools: ViewportLogger
description: <ViewportLogger/> reads the viewport transform from the store (transform key) and renders x, y, and zoom into a Panel.
terminal_note: Display pan x/y + zoom in a status panel — trivial and directly useful for debugging viewport math.
evidence: No viewport-logger panel. The data is accessible (f->view ox/oy/zoom, flow_zoom; overlay via on_overlay), but no shipped widget renders it. grep 'ViewportLogger\|viewport_log' src/ demos/ → no hits. Candidate (trivial via on_overlay).

name: DevTools UI component (bundled)
description: Installable <DevTools/> shadcn component bundling ViewportLogger + NodeInspector + ChangeLogger; takes a position prop and a toggle-group to switch tools.
terminal_note: A composite debug HUD toggled by position with sub-tool switches is a clean TUI debug overlay; ports as one component.
evidence: No bundled devtools HUD; its three constituent loggers are all candidates (above). flow ships only the on_overlay hook to draw such a HUD, not the HUD itself. grep 'devtools' src/ demos/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: Node Search UI component
description: A search-bar (shadcn Command/cmdk) to find nodes. Default: case-insensitive substring match on node label, then selects the node and fitView()s to it. Overridable via onSearch/onSelectNode.
terminal_note: A fuzzy/substring node finder with select+center-on-result is an excellent command-palette-style TUI feature; pure logic.
evidence: No node-search/command-palette. The primitives exist (flow_select_node, flow_fit_view to frame, flow_get_node iteration), but no search UI is built. grep 'search\|find_node\|palette' src/ demos/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: colorMode prop (dark/light/system)
description: colorMode accepts 'dark' | 'light' | 'system'; adds a class to .react-flow so you can style by mode. 'system' follows OS preference.
terminal_note: Dark/light maps to two ANSI palettes; 'system' could read terminal background. The mechanism (a mode flag selecting a palette) ports.
evidence: No theme/palette mode switch; colors are hardcoded per renderer via fg/bg args to flow_put/flow_box (flow_cell.h). No flow_set_theme / colorMode: grep 'colorMode\|theme\|palette\|dark' src/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: CSS variable theme tokens
description: ~25 --xy-* CSS variables set defaults for edges, connection line, minimap bg, background pattern colors, node colors/border/bg/hover/selected, handle colors, selection, controls, resize. Overridable per-element.
terminal_note: A centralized theme-token table maps well to a TUI theme struct (edge color, node fg/bg/border, selection color). Box-shadows/hover have no analog; colors and border styles do.
evidence: No centralized theme-token struct; each renderer passes literal fg/bg/attr to flow_put/flow_box. No flow theme table: grep 'theme\|token\|--xy' src/ → no hits. Not in spec (selection styling is hardcoded in flow_render.h). Candidate.

name: Overridable CSS classes
description: Large set of stable .react-flow__* classes for styling containers, edges (.selected/.animated/.updating), nodes (.selected, default/input/output), handles (connecting/valid states), background, minimap, controls.
terminal_note: Classes are CSS hooks with no TUI equivalent, but the enumerated set of element STATES (edge selected/animated, handle connecting/valid, node selected) is a useful checklist a TUI renderer must distinguish via color/attributes.
evidence: CSS class selectors have no terminal analog — flow has no stylesheet layer; visual state is decided in C render code. The nearest analog (distinguishing states via SGR attr) is partially realized: selected nodes/edges draw last with distinct styling (spec §6 step 2 'Selected items draw last'; FLOW_SELECTED flag), but there is no class/override mechanism.

name: Inline style props
description: Many components accept a style prop for inline CSS (ReactFlow bg/width/height; node style for width/height/backgroundColor). Easiest entry point to customization.
terminal_note: Per-element style overrides map to per-node/per-edge attribute structs (color, dimensions). CSS-specific properties drop; geometry+color survive.
evidence: No per-node/per-edge style override struct; appearance is fully owned by the type's render() function drawing with literal colors (spec §5). A node carries no style/color field (flow_node: id/type/pos/parent/w/h/data/flags). The geometry half (w/h) is computed by measure(), but a consumer-set per-element color/style override is unbuilt. Candidate.

name: Default style vs base style
description: Two stylesheets: dist/style.css (full default theme) and dist/base.css (minimal, MANDATORY). Third-party styling must still import base.css.
terminal_note: The split between 'structural/required' rendering and 'cosmetic/optional' theming is a useful separation (core box/edge drawing vs themable colors). CSS files don't port.
evidence: CSS-file packaging concept with no terminal analog — flow has no stylesheets; rendering is C code in flow_render.h / type render() hooks. The conceptual structural-vs-cosmetic split exists implicitly (compositor draws structure; colors are renderer args) but there is no base.css/style.css artifact to port, and no themable cosmetic layer (see CSS variable tokens, candidate).

name: TailwindCSS styling
description: Custom nodes are React components stylable with Tailwind utility classes; import Tailwind after base styles. Tailwind v4 uses CSS-variable-only config.
terminal_note: Utility-class CSS framework is browser-only; no TUI analog. Relevant only as evidence nodes are fully user-styled components.
evidence: Browser CSS framework with no terminal analog. flow's parallel point — nodes are fully consumer-controlled — IS realized via the render() vtable (spec §5 'analog of a custom node component'), but Tailwind itself does not port. Nearest analog: the node-type render() hook draws whatever the consumer wants in cells.

name: Turbo Flow effect (animated gradient borders)
description: Nodes with glowing animated gradient borders via custom TurboNode/TurboEdge, an SVG linearGradient, and a custom circle marker. defaultEdgeOptions sets type:'turbo' + the marker.
terminal_note: Animated SVG gradients and glow are sub-cell/GPU effects with no TUI equivalent; at most a TUI could color-cycle a border, a crude approximation.
evidence: Spec §1 non-goal (sub-cell rendering) + §16; animated SVG gradients/glow are inherently sub-cell. grep 'gradient\|glow\|turbo' src/ → no hits. Nearest analog: blinking/color-cycling a border, explicitly out of scope (spec §1 'Animation smoothness beyond the cell grid is out of scope').

name: Custom SVG markers / markerEnd
description: Edges reference custom SVG markers by id via markerEnd (e.g. a circle). Open arrow markers fixed in 12.8.4; markers fall back to edge stroke color when null; marker types accept strings/enums.
terminal_note: Edge end-decorations map to a terminal arrowhead glyph at the edge terminus; arbitrary SVG markers don't, but an enum of cap styles does.
evidence: Edge routers place a directional arrowhead glyph at the target terminus (spec §5 built-ins; flow_route_orthogonal and flow_route_straight both emit arrowheads, src/flow_route.h:34/70; WP#2 'arrowhead glyph at the target end … by approach direction'). This is the cap/markerEnd analog. Arbitrary SVG markers don't port, but the directional arrow cap is built and per-edge via the router.

name: EdgeToolbar component
description: New in 12.9.0: an <EdgeToolbar> (analogous to NodeToolbar) for attaching action UI to edges.
terminal_note: A small action menu anchored to an edge maps to a popup near the edge's midpoint cell; positioning along a box-char edge is approximate.
evidence: No edge toolbar/popup. The router does expose out->label_anchor (edge midpoint, used for labels — flow_set_edge_label, flow_route_straight sets it), which is the anchor a toolbar would use, but no toolbar widget exists. grep 'toolbar' src/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: zIndexMode prop
description: New in 12.10.0: zIndexMode controls how z-index is calculated for nodes and edges.
terminal_note: Paint/stacking order policy for nodes vs edges is a TUI concern (which overlaps win on a shared cell); a mode enum governing it ports as a draw-order rule.
evidence: flow's z-order is a single fixed compose sequence (spec §6 step 2; src/flow_render.h) with depth-aware node ordering (groups) and selected-last within a layer — but there is no configurable zIndexMode enum. grep 'zIndexMode\|zindex' src/ → no hits. Candidate (the draw order exists; the policy knob does not).

name: autoPanOnSelection prop
description: New (2026-06-01, PR #5677): autoPanOnSelection auto-pans the viewport when a user drags a selection box close to the viewport edge.
terminal_note: Auto-pan near viewport edge during a selection-drag is the same edge-proximity scroll logic as object-drag auto-pan (already in this repo); extend it to the selection box.
evidence: flow's auto-pan fires for OBJECT drags only and explicitly excludes marquee: src/flow_input.h:55 'pane-pan and marquee drags never auto-pan'. flow__autopan (flow_input.h:58) is called from node/connect/reconnect motion, not the marquee branch. Extending it to the selection box is unbuilt → candidate.

name: Start a selection above a node
description: New in 12.9.0 (PR #5551): a selection box can be started even when the pointer is over a node (previously starting on a node would drag the node).
terminal_note: Disambiguating start-selection-vs-drag-node when pressing over a node (via a modifier or tool mode) is pure input routing.
evidence: flow's marquee arms only on a Shift-drag over the EMPTY pane (src/flow_input.h:145 'shift-drag empty pane: arm marquee'); pressing over a node always arms node-drag/select (flow_input.h press branch). Starting a marquee over a node is not supported. grep confirms marquee anchor is pane-only. Candidate.

name: connectionDragThreshold prop
description: Added in 12.8.0 (PR #5344): connectionDragThreshold sets the drag distance before a connection starts.
terminal_note: A movement threshold (in cells) before a connection-drag begins disambiguates click vs drag; trivial integer threshold.
evidence: flow has a 1-cell movement threshold before any gesture engages: f->moved is set only when scr != down_pos (src/flow_input.h:151/161/166-167), gating drag/marquee/pan/connect. This is the connect/drag threshold the prop configures (fixed at 1 cell rather than a tunable distance). connectOnClick is also offered as an alternative (spec §8). Threshold behavior present; the tunable knob is not exposed.

name: ariaRole prop on nodes/edges
description: Added in 12.7.0 (PR #5299): ariaRole prop on nodes and edges to set their ARIA role.
terminal_note: ARIA roles are web-accessibility-tree metadata with no TUI equivalent; terminal a11y is handled by the terminal/screen reader, not per-element roles.
evidence: ARIA accessibility-tree metadata has no terminal analog — there is no DOM/a11y tree in a TTY; a screen reader consumes the terminal's text output, not per-node roles. grep 'aria' src/ → no hits. No nearest analog at the element level.

name: ariaLabelConfig prop
description: Added in 12.7.0 (PR #5277): ariaLabelConfig customizes UI text such as aria labels and the keyboard-interaction hint strings shown for nodes/edges.
terminal_note: Centralized customizable UI/help strings (the 'press enter to select' hints) DO port as a localizable string table for status-line help.
evidence: flow shows a static statusbar help line (' n:add  x:del  f:fit  ?:help', render_statusbar.txt golden) but the strings are hardcoded, not a configurable label table. grep 'aria\|label_config\|help_strings' src/ → no hits (the statusbar text is a literal). The customizable-string-table analog is unbuilt → candidate.

name: domAttributes option on nodes/edges
description: Added in 12.7.0 (PR #5317): domAttributes passes arbitrary DOM attributes to node/edge elements. Later dangerouslySetInnerHTML removed for safety.
terminal_note: Passing raw DOM attributes is browser-only; no TUI analog.
evidence: Raw DOM-attribute passthrough has no terminal analog — flow nodes render into a cell buffer via render(), with no DOM element to attach attributes to. grep 'domAttribute' src/ → no hits. No nearest analog (node->data is the opaque consumer payload, but it is not DOM attributes).

name: autoPanOnNodeFocus + focus offscreen nodes on Tab
description: Added in 12.7.0 (PR #5308): tabbing focuses nodes even when outside the viewport, with autoPanOnNodeFocus to auto-pan to the focused node.
terminal_note: Tab-to-cycle-focus through nodes and auto-pan to bring the focused node into view is an excellent keyboard-first TUI navigation pattern.
evidence: No keyboard Tab-focus cycling through nodes; flow has no focused-node concept (flags are SELECTED/DRAGGING/HOVERED, no FOCUSED) and no Tab binding (flow_dispatch_key built-ins: x/n/f/?/Space/u/Ctrl-r/Delete). grep 'focus\|\\t' relevant code → no Tab-focus handling. Object-drag auto-pan exists but not focus-driven. Candidate.

name: Viewport ease / interpolate options
description: Added in 12.7.0 (PR #5276): all viewport-altering functions (setViewport, fitView, zoomTo) gained ease and interpolate options to control transition animation.
terminal_note: Eased viewport transitions can be approximated with a few interpolated redraw frames; an ease/interpolate option ports as a tween config.
evidence: Spec §1 non-goal: 'Animation smoothness beyond the cell grid (e.g. eased zoom tweens) is out of scope', reiterated in WP 'Deferred beyond increment 3' (no smooth/eased zoom). flow_fit_view/flow_set_zoom snap instantly (src/flow_view.h). Nearest analog: a few interpolated redraw frames, explicitly cut.

name: NodeResizer zoom-stable controls
description: 12.7.0+: NodeResizer control handles no longer shrink when zooming out; props can change during a resize; resizing beyond absolute extents fixed.
terminal_note: Resize handles are corner/edge cells of a node box; keeping them usable regardless of zoom = always render at least one cell. Resize-extent clamping is integer math.
evidence: No interactive node resizer at all — node w/h are computed by measure() (flow_measure_node, flow_model.h:448), not user-draggable; flow_resize is the TERMINAL resize, not a node resizer (flow_model.h:428). grep 'resizer\|NodeResizer\|resize_handle' src/ → no hits. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Detached handle content as connection start
description: 12.8.x (PR #5428): handle child content positioned outside the handle itself can still start a connection (larger clickable connection targets).
terminal_note: Decoupling the connection hit-area from the handle's visual cell is useful where handles are 1 cell; an expanded hit region around a handle ports as a logic change.
evidence: flow's handle hit-area equals the handle's analytic anchor cell (flow_hit_handle off flow_handle_anchor); there is no expanded/detached hit region around a handle. grep 'hit_handle' src/ shows single-anchor testing. Not in spec. Candidate.

name: Snap selection (group) to grid
description: 12.8.x (PR #5453): with snapGrid enabled, the whole selection snaps together rather than each node independently. 12.10.2 added snapGrid to screenToFlowPosition.
terminal_note: Snapping a multi-node selection as a rigid group to a cell grid (vs per-node) is exactly right since everything is already cell-aligned.
evidence: Positions are integer cells so snap-to-grid is automatic (spec §3 'Integer positions mean snap-to-grid is automatic'). Multi-drag applies ONE shared world delta to selection roots (src/flow_input.h multi-drag branch; groups fix moves roots so children follow), so the selection translates rigidly — no independent per-node snapping. The rigid-group snap behavior is the default.

name: experimental_useOnNodesChangeMiddleware hook
description: 12.10.0 (PR #5484): experimental hook to intercept/transform the onNodesChange stream via middleware before changes apply.
terminal_note: A middleware/interceptor over the node-change pipeline (validate, transform, veto changes) is a clean architectural hook for a C mutator pipeline.
evidence: No middleware/interceptor over the mutator pipeline; mutators apply directly (spec §1 'we own one store and mutate it directly'). flow_add_edge does internal validation (reject self/dup, spec §4) but exposes no consumer veto/transform hook. grep 'middleware' src/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: Render edges above nodes within a subflow
description: 12.8.0 (PR #5361): edges within a subflow render above nodes (refining the earlier parented-edge z behavior).
terminal_note: Subflow edge layering is a paint-order rule; doable but box-char edges over node content reduce legibility.
evidence: flow's compose order draws ALL edges below ALL nodes uniformly (spec §6 step 2 'edges → nodes'; src/flow_render.h), with no subflow-specific edge-above-node rule. grep 'zindex\|edge.*above' src/ → no hits. Same gap as 'Edge z-index over parented nodes'. Candidate.

name: MiniMap custom nodes / MiniMapNode export
description: 12.8.x (PR #5444): MiniMapNode exported for custom minimap node shapes; interactive minimap zoom step default set to 1.
terminal_note: A minimap (downscaled overview) ports as a small block/braille-character density map in a corner; custom per-node minimap rendering is the styling layer.
evidence: flow ships a minimap: flow_set_minimap(enabled, corner, w, h) (src/flow_model.h:195) and flow__minimap downsamples the graph into a corner panel reusing the LOD path (src/flow_render.h:28-48, spec §7 'the minimap reuses the same downsample path'). Custom per-node minimap glyph shapes are not exposed (the styling-layer extension), but the minimap widget itself is built.
status_corrected: candidate — corrected against source: minimap config is {enabled,w,h,corner} only (flow_model.h:221), no per-node renderer/color callback

name: llms.txt AI documentation
description: 2026-03-19: React Flow publishes /llms.txt, /llms-medium.txt, /llms-full.txt to help LLMs consume docs.
terminal_note: Documentation-tooling meta-feature, not a library feature; no port relevance beyond being a bulk source.
evidence: Documentation-publishing meta-feature with no library surface and no terminal analog — it concerns reactflow.dev's own docs site, nothing in a node-graph engine. Not applicable to flow. No nearest analog.

name: React Flow Pro examples catalog
description: Pro subscription unlocks downloadable apps + guides for core-team examples: Helper Lines, Auto Layout, Collaborative, Copy/Paste, Dynamic Layouting, Editable Edge, Expand/Collapse, Force Layout, Freehand Draw, Node Position Animation, Parent Child Relation, Selection Grouping, Server Side Image, Shapes, Undo/Redo.
terminal_note: Catalog of app-level patterns; each itemized separately here. Most port as logic; image-export/freehand/turbo are weakest fits.
evidence: A catalog meta-entry, not a single feature — its constituents are itemized separately in this inventory (auto-layout=specd, parent-child/selection-grouping/undo-redo=implemented, freehand/server-image=na-terminal, force/dynamic-layout=specd/candidate). As an aggregate it has no single implementable surface. No nearest analog beyond its parts.

name: Editable edge with draggable control points (pro)
description: Pro example: custom bezier/linear edges with draggable control points; users adjust edge paths by dragging and keyboard. Suggested base for custom edge routing.
terminal_note: User-routable edges via waypoints map to draggable bend-points on box-char edges (orthogonal segments); bezier becomes polyline.
evidence: Edges are auto-routed by the type's route() function with no user-draggable waypoints/control points (spec §5; flow_route_orthogonal/straight compute the path). flow_reconnect_edge moves an ENDPOINT to a new handle (flow_model.h reconnect) but not intermediate bend-points. grep 'waypoint\|control_point\|bend' src/ → no hits. Candidate.

name: Node position animation hook (pro)
description: Pro example: a hook that animates a node between previous and new positions (smooth transitions on relayout).
source: pro examples index (Node Position Animation) — https://reactflow.dev/pro/examples
terminal_note: Tweening node positions across cells over a few frames is possible but coarse; often better to snap.
evidence: Spec §1 non-goal: 'Animation smoothness beyond the cell grid … is out of scope'; WP 'Deferred beyond increment 3' cuts animation. Position changes snap instantly via flow_move_node (no tween). Nearest analog: a few interpolation frames, explicitly cut. (Layout commits positions directly per spec §10 'round to cells on commit'.)

name: Server-side image generation of a flow (pro)
description: Pro example: generate an image of a flow with a server plus a frontend to adjust/display it.
source: pro examples index (Server Side Image Creation) — https://reactflow.dev/pro/examples
terminal_note: Rasterizing the flow to a bitmap is inherently pixel/SVG-based. A TUI analog would be exporting the cell buffer as text/ANSI, not a raster image.
evidence: Raster/SVG image export is inherently pixel-based with no in-grid analog. flow renders to a cell buffer; the nearest analog (dump the cell buffer as text/ANSI) is effectively what flow_render produces for snapshot tests, but server-side raster image creation is out of scope. grep 'png\|svg\|image\|raster' src/ → no hits.

name: Flowchart shape nodes (pro)
description: Pro example: custom node types as common flowchart shapes (diamonds, circles, cylinders) using SVG, for technical diagrams.
terminal_note: A few shapes (rectangle, rounded box) render in box-chars; diamonds/circles approximate poorly at cell resolution but are doable with slashes/Unicode.
evidence: flow ships only the default titled-box node and the group container (spec §5; flow_default_node_type, flow_group_node_type). No shape-node set (diamond/circle/cylinder). A consumer COULD register custom shape types via the render() vtable, but none ships. grep 'diamond\|cylinder\|shape' src/ → no hits. Candidate. (WP#9 flowchart-demo uses default/lightly-customized nodes, not a shape library.)

name: Animated edges
description: Edges support animated:true (default style adds a marching-ants dash animation); the .react-flow__edge.animated class targets it.
terminal_note: Marching-ants ports as a cycling dash pattern of box-chars over time frames; a per-edge flag toggles it. Cheap and legible.
evidence: No per-edge animated flag or marching-ants cycling. A static dashed pattern exists only for the in-flight connection PREVIEW (src/flow_render.h:243 'dashed: every other cell'), not for committed edges and not time-animated. flow_edge has no animated field (flags = SELECTED only). grep 'animated\|marching' src/ → no hits. Not in spec (animation is a §1 non-goal generally). Candidate for the cheap static-dash variant.

name: ConnectionLineType / edge type enums
description: Edges and the live connection line can be typed: ConnectionLineType.SmoothStep, plus edge types 'smoothstep', bezier, straight, step. Set per-edge (type) or globally.
terminal_note: Edge routing styles map to TUI line styles: straight (diagonal approx), step/smoothstep (orthogonal box-char) — orthogonal is the natural default.
evidence: flow has a per-edge type[16] selecting a router vtable (spec §5): 'default' orthogonal (step analog, flow_route_orthogonal, flow_route.h:34) and 'straight' (flow_route_straight, flow_route.h:70), both registered (flow_types.h:51). flow_edge_type_for resolves by type string; render edge loop picks it up automatically. bezier/smoothstep are the explicitly-cut curved non-goals (spec §1, WP Deferred) — collapsed to box-drawing corners, which the orthogonal type provides.

name: Panel component (positioned overlay)
description: <Panel position='top-left'|...> renders fixed UI over the flow without scrolling with the canvas. Used for layout buttons, mode toggles, devtools. Pointer events removed while a selection drags.
terminal_note: Screen-anchored overlay panels (corners/edges) that don't pan with the canvas are a core TUI layout primitive (status bars, toolbars, HUDs).
evidence: flow exposes the overlay hook on_overlay(flow_t*, flow_surface *screen, user) drawn LAST over the composited frame (src/flow_model.h:181; spec §6 z-order ends with 'overlay (panels)', §8). topo.c uses it for a right-click details panel. The built-in statusbar (render_statusbar.txt) is a screen-anchored panel that doesn't pan. The positioned-overlay primitive is built (corner enum exists for the minimap; arbitrary positioning is the consumer's via the surface).

name: fitView / fitView on layout
