description: fitView frames all nodes in the viewport; called after layout/selection/search to recenter. Pairs with minZoom (e.g. 0) to frame very large graphs.
terminal_note: Compute graph bbox, set pan+zoom to fit the terminal viewport — pure transform math; clamp zoom to legible cell sizes.
evidence: flow_fit_view(f, margin) computes flow_bounds and picks zoom+pan to fit with margin padding, zoom clamped to [zmin,zmax] (src/flow_view.h / decl flow_model.h:120; spec §7 getViewportForBounds). Bound to the 'f' key (flow_dispatch_key). WP#8 fit_after wires it post-layout; test_zoom.c covers fit-view math. minZoom clamp (FLOW_ZOOM_MIN 0.25) keeps nodes legible.

name: Edge.id
description: Unique id of an edge (string). Required identity used for selection, deletion, reconnection, and z-order.
terminal_note: Int key in flow's port (deliberate divergence: int ids, not strings) — used for select/delete/reconnect.
evidence: flow_edge { int id, source, target; ... } at src/flow_model.h:13-14; flow_get_edge(f,id) at src/flow_model.h:44; ids assigned via nexteid.

name: Edge.source / Edge.target
description: `source` is the id of the source node, `target` is the id of the target node (both strings). Define the two endpoints of the edge.
terminal_note: Two node-id int fields; core graph model.
evidence: flow_edge { int id, source, target; ... } src/flow_model.h:13; flow_add_edge(f,src,dst,...) src/flow_model.h:42; persisted JSON src/flow_json.h:66-70,364-365.

name: Edge.sourceHandle / Edge.targetHandle
description: `sourceHandle`/`targetHandle` (string | null): id of the source/target handle, only needed when a node has multiple handles. Disambiguates which port the edge attaches to.
terminal_note: Optional handle-id strings; the node type declares named handle anchors and the edge stores the chosen id.
evidence: flow_edge { char source_handle[16], target_handle[16]; } src/flow_model.h:13; resolved by name in flow__edge_handle src/flow_render.h:127 (named match wins); persisted src/flow_json.h:66-70,366-367.

name: Edge.type
description: Type of edge defined in `edgeTypes` (string). Selects the renderer/path algorithm. Built-ins: default(bezier), straight, step, smoothstep, simplebezier. Falls back to 'default' if unset.
source: Edge type field `type` + Default edge types — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge
terminal_note: Type tag is portable and resolves a router vtable; renderers are char-cell line generators (orthogonal/straight), not SVG paths.
evidence: flow_edge.type[16] src/flow_model.h:14; resolved each frame via flow_edge_type_for(f, type[0]?type:"default") src/flow_render.h:176; empty => default. Delta: flow's default router is orthogonal box-drawing, not xyflow's bezier.

name: Edge.animated
description: Boolean. Enables the built-in animated dashed-line effect for the default edge type. Adds `.react-flow__edge.animated` CSS class.
terminal_note: Flag is portable; the visual needs a marching-ants/blink analog using alternating box-drawing glyphs on a timer.
evidence: No animated field on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14); grep 'animated' src/ flow.h → no hits. Not addressed by spec; §1's animation cut is for smooth/sub-cell tweens, not in-grid marching glyphs.

name: Edge.hidden
description: Boolean. When true the edge is not rendered (but remains in state).
terminal_note: Skip drawing the edge when true; trivial — would gate the render edge loop.
evidence: No hidden field on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14); the render edge loop draws every edge unconditionally (src/flow_render.h:172-189). grep 'hidden' src/ → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.deletable
description: Boolean. Controls whether the edge can be removed (e.g. by delete key / onNodesDelete-style flows).
terminal_note: Boolean gate checked before deleting; portable.
evidence: flow_remove_edge / flow_delete_selection delete unconditionally (src/flow_model.h:105,118); no deletable field on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14). grep 'deletable' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.selectable
description: Boolean. Controls whether the edge can be selected by clicking.
terminal_note: Boolean gate on click/keyboard selection hit-testing; portable.
evidence: flow_select_edge / edge-body click-select are ungated (src/flow_model.h:97; src/flow_input.h:266-268); no selectable field on flow_edge. grep 'selectable' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.selected
description: Boolean. Current selection state of the edge; drives `.react-flow__edge.selected` styling and is passed to custom edges.
terminal_note: Selection-state flag; renders edge bold/highlighted when set.
evidence: FLOW_SELECTED bit on flow_edge.flags (src/flow_model.h:14, enum :2); set via flow_select_edge (mutually exclusive with node sel) src/flow_model.h:97; render draws selected edge bold: attr = (e->flags & FLOW_SELECTED) ? FLOW_BOLD : 0, src/flow_render.h:180.

name: Edge.focusable
description: Boolean. Controls whether the edge can receive keyboard focus (a11y/tab order).
terminal_note: Include/exclude edge from a keyboard focus ring; portable but there is no edge focus-ring traversal in flow.
evidence: No focusable field and no keyboard edge-focus traversal in flow (selection is via mouse hit; src/flow_input.h:266-268). grep 'focusable' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.data
description: Arbitrary data passed to an edge (generic EdgeData). Carries app-specific payload consumed by custom edge renderers (e.g. control points, label key, animation config).
terminal_note: Opaque user payload as a void* on the edge struct.
evidence: flow_edge { ... void *data; ... } src/flow_model.h:14. Field exists and is carried; note it is intentionally NOT persisted (edge data has no save/load hook — serialize design notes B) and no built-in router reads it.

name: Edge.label
description: ReactNode. The label or custom element rendered along the edge — commonly a text label or custom controls.
terminal_note: Text labels port directly (string at edge midpoint); arbitrary React nodes do not — flow restricts to a heap string.
evidence: flow_edge.label (heap char*) src/flow_model.h:14; set via flow_set_edge_label (strdup, frees prior) src/flow_model.h:107; drawn UTF-8 at rt.label_anchor src/flow_render.h:183-186; persisted src/flow_json.h:375. Delta: string only, no rich/custom element.

name: Edge.labelStyle
description: CSSProperties. Custom styles applied to the edge label element.
terminal_note: Would map to terminal text attributes (fg/bg/bold) instead of CSS; subset only.
evidence: Edge label is drawn with fixed FLOW_FG/FLOW_BG and the path's attr (src/flow_render.h:186); no per-edge label style field. grep 'labelStyle' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.labelShowBg
description: Boolean. Whether a background rectangle is drawn behind the edge label.
terminal_note: Toggle a filled background cell-run behind the label; portable but unbuilt.
evidence: Label render writes glyphs with no background run (src/flow_render.h:183-186); no labelShowBg field. grep 'labelShowBg'/'showbg' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.labelBgStyle
description: CSSProperties. Styles for the label background rectangle (fill etc).
terminal_note: Would map to a background color attribute for label cells; CSS subset only.
evidence: No label background is drawn at all (src/flow_render.h:183-186); no field. grep 'labelBg' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.labelBgPadding
description: [number, number]. Horizontal/vertical padding between the label text and its background.
terminal_note: Would express as integer cell padding around label text; depends on a label background that flow does not draw.
evidence: No label background / padding logic (src/flow_render.h:183-186). grep 'labelBgPadding'/'Padding' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.labelBgBorderRadius
description: Number. Corner radius of the label background rectangle.
terminal_note: Rounded corners are sub-cell; a char grid cannot render radius. Nearest analog: rounded box-drawing corner glyphs for a whole box, not a true radius.
evidence: Sub-cell corner rounding has no char-cell representation; spec §16 / non-goals state glyphs are one cell and curves collapse to box-drawing corners. No label background exists (src/flow_render.h:183-186).

name: Edge.markerStart / Edge.markerEnd
description: EdgeMarkerType (string id of an SVG marker, or an EdgeMarker config object). Sets the marker at the beginning/end of the edge (e.g. arrowheads).
terminal_note: Arrowheads map to glyphs (▶◀▲▼) at endpoints; a target arrowhead is always drawn but is not a per-edge configurable field, and markerStart is never drawn.
evidence: Routers auto-draw a directional target arrowhead (flow__glyph→0x25B6/0x25C0/0x25BC/0x25B2, src/flow_route.h:60 and :103) but there is no markerStart/markerEnd field on flow_edge and no start-marker. grep 'marker' src/flow_model.h → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.style
description: CSSProperties. Inline styles for the edge SVG path (stroke color, stroke width, dasharray, etc).
terminal_note: Would map stroke color → fg, dash → glyph pattern; stroke-width has no cell analog (single-cell lines).
evidence: Edge path is drawn with fixed FLOW_FG/FLOW_BG, only selection toggles FLOW_BOLD (src/flow_render.h:180-182); no per-edge style field on flow_edge. grep 'style' src/flow_model.h → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.className
description: String. CSS class applied to the edge element for custom styling.
terminal_note: CSS classnames are DOM-bound; a TUI has no stylesheet cascade. Nearest analog: a style-token/enum, which flow does not use for edges.
evidence: No stylesheet/DOM in a char-cell renderer; flow draws edges directly into a flow_cellbuf (src/flow_render.h:180-189). grep 'className' → no hits.

name: Edge.interactionWidth
description: Number. ReactFlow renders an invisible path around each edge to make it easier to click/tap; this sets the width of that invisible hit path (BaseEdge default 20; the edge-intersection example raises it to 75).
terminal_note: Click tolerance maps to a Chebyshev hit-test distance in cells; flow implements the tolerance but as a call-time argument, not a per-edge field.
evidence: flow_hit_edge(f, screen, tol) tests Chebyshev distance <= tol to the routed path (src/flow_render.h:122,135). Delta: tol is a parameter at call sites (input.h:118,266 use tol=1), not a per-edge interactionWidth field.

name: Edge.zIndex
description: Number. Stacking order of the edge relative to other edges/nodes.
terminal_note: Integer painter's draw order; flow uses fixed layer order (all edges under all nodes) and array order within the edge layer, with no per-edge zIndex.
evidence: Edge draw order is array order in a fixed layer below nodes (src/flow_render.h:172, edges before nodes :191); no zIndex field on flow_edge. grep 'zIndex'/'z_index' → no hits; not in spec (nodes get depth z-order via groups, edges do not).

name: Edge.ariaLabel
description: String. Accessibility label announced for the edge.
terminal_note: No ARIA in a TUI; could feed a status-line/screen-reader bridge. Not stored by flow.
evidence: No ariaLabel field on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14). grep 'aria' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Edge.ariaRole
description: AriaRole (default 'group'). ARIA role attribute for the edge element, used for accessibility.
terminal_note: ARIA role is a DOM accessibility-tree concept with no terminal equivalent.
evidence: No DOM accessibility tree in a char-cell renderer; grep 'ariaRole'/'role' → no hits. Nearest analog: none in a TUI.

name: Edge.reconnectable
description: boolean | HandleType ('source'|'target'). Determines whether the edge can be updated by dragging its source or target to a new node/handle; overrides the `edgesReconnectable` ReactFlow prop. 'source' = only the source end is draggable, 'target' = only target, true = both.
source: Edge type field `reconnectable` — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge ; example values in reconnect-edge example — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/reconnect-edge
terminal_note: The reconnect interaction works for both ends; only the per-edge gate field that restricts WHICH end is draggable is missing.
evidence: Both endpoints are draggable (flow_reconnect_edge which=0/1, src/flow_model.h:106; arm/drop in src/flow_input.h:115-130,235-242) but with no per-edge reconnectable gate field. grep 'reconnectable' → no hits; the field/gate is not in spec.

name: Edge.domAttributes
description: Escape hatch for custom attributes on the edge's DOM <g> element: Omit<SVGAttributes<SVGGElement>, 'id'|'style'|'className'|'role'|'aria-label'|'dangerouslySetInnerHTML'>.
terminal_note: Raw SVG/DOM attribute passthrough; no analog in a char-cell renderer.
evidence: No DOM element backs an edge in flow (edges are routed cell runs, src/flow_render.h:180-189). grep 'domAttributes' → no hits. Nearest analog: none.

name: Edge union variants (DefaultEdge | SmoothStepEdge | BezierEdge)
description: The Edge<T> type is a union of DefaultEdge, SmoothStepEdge, and BezierEdge. Each variant narrows `type` and adds variant-specific `pathOptions`.
source: Edge type `export type Edge<T> = DefaultEdge<T> | SmoothStepEdge<T> | BezierEdge<T>` — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge
terminal_note: Modeled as a single flat edge struct keyed by a `type` string + a registered router vtable, rather than a discriminated union with per-variant pathOptions.
evidence: flow uses one flat flow_edge with a type[16] tag resolved to a flow_edge_type vtable (src/flow_model.h:14,24-25; src/flow_render.h:176). The smoothstep/bezier variants are spec'd-but-cut (spec §1 non-goal 'curved edges collapse to box-drawing'; workpackages 'Deferred beyond increment 3' ~line 151). No pathOptions union exists.

name: SmoothStepEdge.pathOptions (offset, borderRadius)
description: For type 'smoothstep': pathOptions = { offset?: number; borderRadius?: number }. offset controls distance the path extends from the node before bending; borderRadius rounds the step corners.
terminal_note: offset/corner style could route to box-drawing orthogonal routing; borderRadius reduces to a corner-glyph choice (sharp └ vs rounded ╰).
evidence: No smoothstep router or pathOptions exists (only orthogonal+straight registered, src/flow_types.h:50-51). Spec §1 cuts curved/smoothstep edges ('collapse to box-drawing corners'); workpackages 'Deferred beyond increment 3' ~line 151 explicitly defer bezier/smoothstep.

name: BezierEdge.pathOptions (curvature)
description: For type 'default': pathOptions = { curvature?: number } controlling the intensity of the bezier curve (same as getBezierPath curvature, default 0.25).
terminal_note: Curvature is a smooth-curve parameter; a TUI approximates curves with stepped glyph runs, so curvature would be a routing hint at best.
evidence: No bezier router/curvature option (src/flow_route.h has only orthogonal+straight). Spec §1 non-goal: 'curved edges (bezier/smoothstep) collapse to box-drawing corners'; workpackages ~line 151 defers bezier explicitly.

name: Built-in edge types (default/straight/step/smoothstep/simplebezier)
description: Setting Edge.type to one of 'default'(bezier), 'straight', 'step', 'smoothstep', 'simplebezier' selects a built-in renderer. These remain available even when a custom edgeTypes map is provided, unless a key is overridden. Default fallback is 'default'.
source: Default edge types section — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge ; edge-types example — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/edge-types
terminal_note: straight/step port cleanly to char-cell lines; bezier/smoothstep/simplebezier curves are cut to stepped approximations.
evidence: Two of five ship registered by flow_register_defaults: 'default'=flow_route_orthogonal and 'straight'=flow_route_straight (src/flow_types.h:50-51; src/flow_route.h:9-10). 'step'≈the orthogonal box-drawing path. Delta: flow's 'default' is orthogonal (not bezier); smoothstep/simplebezier/bezier cut per spec §1 / workpackages ~line 151.

name: EdgeData generic / typed edges
description: Edge takes a generic data type; custom edges can declare e.g. Edge<{node:string},'animatedNode'> to type the data payload and the type tag.
terminal_note: Generics are a TS concern; in C the typed payload is a per-type data struct behind void*.
evidence: flow_edge.data is an opaque void* (src/flow_model.h:14); the capability (per-edge typed payload) exists. Delta: no compile-time typing (C has no generics); edge data is not persisted.

name: EdgeProps.sourceX / sourceY / targetX / targetY
description: Numbers passed to custom edge components: the absolute screen/flow coordinates of the source and target endpoints, already resolved from node+handle positions, so the edge only needs to draw between them.
terminal_note: Pre-computed endpoint cell (col,row) pairs handed to the router.
evidence: flow__edge_screen_ends resolves screen endpoint cells ss/ts from node+handle (src/flow_render.h:104-115), passed as flow_pt s, flow_pt t to et->route(...) (src/flow_render.h:179). The router signature takes s and t (src/flow_model.h:25).

name: EdgeProps.sourcePosition / targetPosition
description: Position enum (Top/Right/Bottom/Left) for each endpoint, telling the path generator which side of the node the edge leaves/enters.
terminal_note: Four-way flow_pos enum (TOP/RIGHT/BOTTOM/LEFT) passed to the router as sp/tp facings.
evidence: flow_pos enum {TOP,RIGHT,BOTTOM,LEFT} src/flow_model.h (flow_geom); flow__edge_screen_ends sets facings lsp/ltp from the resolved handle side and outputs sp/tp (src/flow_render.h:108-114); router signature route(s, sp, t, tp, out) src/flow_model.h:25.

name: EdgeProps.sourceHandleId / targetHandleId
description: string | null passed to custom edges: the resolved handle ids for the connected endpoints.
terminal_note: Resolved handle-id strings; flow resolves the handle internally but the router signature receives the side (sp/tp), not the id string.
evidence: Handle ids are stored (flow_edge.source_handle/target_handle, src/flow_model.h:13) and resolved by name in flow__edge_handle (src/flow_render.h:127). Delta: the router vtable receives the resolved facing (sp/tp), not the handle id strings directly (src/flow_model.h:25).

name: EdgeProps.markerStart / markerEnd (resolved url strings)
description: In EdgeProps these are plain `string` values (the resolved 'url(#markerId)' references), distinct from the Edge config object form. Custom edges pass them straight to BaseEdge.
terminal_note: In a TUI the resolved marker is just a glyph id/enum at the endpoint; the url() indirection is SVG-specific.
evidence: The router auto-draws a target arrowhead glyph but there is no marker value threaded through the route() vtable, and no markerStart (src/flow_route.h:60,103; src/flow_model.h:25). grep 'marker' src/ → no hits; not in spec.

name: EdgeProps.pathOptions (passthrough)
description: `any` — the edge's pathOptions are passed through to the custom edge component so it can forward them to the matching path utility.
terminal_note: An opaque options bag forwarded to the router; flow's route() vtable takes no options bag.
evidence: flow_edge_type.route(flow_pt s, flow_pos sp, flow_pt t, flow_pos tp, flow_route *out) has no per-edge options parameter (src/flow_model.h:25); flow_edge has no pathOptions field. grep 'pathOptions' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Custom edge component contract (receives EdgeProps)
description: A custom edge is a component registered in edgeTypes that receives EdgeProps (id, type, source/target, sourceX/Y/targetX/Y, source/targetPosition, source/targetHandleId, selected, animated, data, style, selectable, deletable, label+label* props, markerStart/End, interactionWidth, pathOptions) and renders a path (typically via BaseEdge).
terminal_note: Maps to a route() callback in a vtable: given resolved endpoints + facings, emit a cell polyline. The extension point ports; the prop set passed is narrower (no animated/style/selectable/deletable/markers).
evidence: flow_edge_type { const char *type; void (*route)(flow_pt s, flow_pos sp, flow_pt t, flow_pos tp, flow_route *out); } src/flow_model.h:24-25; registered via flow_register_edge_type and invoked per edge (src/flow_render.h:179). Delta: the route hook receives geometry+facing, not the full EdgeProps bag (no selected/data/markers passthrough).

name: edgeTypes registry / registration
description: EdgeTypes is an object map { [key:string]: ComponentType<EdgeProps> }. You define an edgeTypes object mapping type names to custom edge components and pass it to <ReactFlow edgeTypes={...}>, then set an edge's `type` to the key. Built-in types stay available unless overridden.
terminal_note: A name→router registry of function pointers keyed by edge.type; clean C analog.
evidence: flow_register_edge_type(f, const flow_edge_type*) + flow_edge_type_for(f, type) (src/flow_model.h:36,38) back a type-string→router registry; built-ins 'default'+'straight' registered by flow_register_defaults (src/flow_types.h:50-51); edge.type resolves through it each frame (src/flow_render.h:176).

name: DefaultEdgeOptions (defaultEdgeOptions prop)
description: Object applied as defaults to every edge unless overridden per-edge. Documented fields: type, animated, hidden, deletable, selectable, focusable, data, style, className, markerStart, markerEnd, zIndex, ariaLabel, ariaRole, interactionWidth, label, labelStyle, labelShowBg, labelBgStyle, labelBgPadding, labelBgBorderRadius, reconnectable. Used e.g. to set interactionWidth:75 globally in the edge-intersection example.
source: DefaultEdgeOptions type — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/default-edge-options ; usage in edge-intersection example — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/edge-intersection
terminal_note: A defaults struct merged into each edge at create time; straightforward but unbuilt — flow has no per-engine edge-defaults object.
evidence: No defaults-merge for edges; flow_add_edge sets fields to fixed defaults (type "", no label) src/flow_model.h:42. grep 'defaultEdgeOptions'/'edge_defaults' → no hits; not in spec. (Most of the underlying fields it would default — animated/hidden/deletable/etc. — also don't exist.)

name: EdgeMarker config object
description: Object form of a marker: type (MarkerType|'arrow'|'arrowclosed'), color (string|null), width (number), height (number), markerUnits (string, e.g. 'userSpaceOnUse'/'strokeWidth'), orient (string, e.g. 'auto-start-reverse'), strokeWidth (number). Lets you size/color/orient built-in arrowheads.
source: EdgeMarker type fields — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge-marker ; usage in markers example — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/markers
terminal_note: type/color/orient could map to glyph + fg + direction; width/height/strokeWidth/markerUnits are sub-cell SVG sizing with no char-grid analog.
evidence: flow auto-draws a directional arrowhead glyph (orient analog) but has no marker config object, color, or sizing (src/flow_route.h:60,103). grep 'EdgeMarker'/'markerUnits' → no hits; not in spec.

name: EdgeMarkerType union (string id | EdgeMarker)
description: markerStart/markerEnd accept either a string (id of a self-defined SVG marker in <defs>) or an EdgeMarker config object for built-in arrow/arrowclosed markers.
source: EdgeMarkerType usage — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/edge-marker ; markers example `markerEnd:'logo'` vs object — https://reactflow.dev/examples/edges/markers
terminal_note: Built-in arrowheads → glyphs; the 'string id of a custom SVG marker' branch has no TUI analog beyond a custom glyph table.
evidence: No marker field accepting either form on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14); only a hardcoded auto arrowhead glyph (src/flow_route.h:60,103). grep 'markerEnd'/'EdgeMarkerType' → no hits; not in spec.

name: MarkerType enum (Arrow, ArrowClosed)
description: enum MarkerType { Arrow='arrow', ArrowClosed='arrowclosed' }. Two built-in arrowhead styles: open arrow and filled/closed arrow. Docs note future shapes (circles, diamonds) may be added.
terminal_note: Open vs closed arrow collapse to a single directional glyph (▶◀▲▼); the open/closed distinction is mostly lost in one cell.
evidence: flow draws one fixed filled-triangle glyph per direction (0x25B6/0x25C0/0x25BC/0x25B2, src/flow_route.h:60,103) with no MarkerType enum or open/closed choice. grep 'MarkerType'/'arrowclosed' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Custom SVG markers via <defs>
description: You can define your own <marker> in an SVG <defs> (id, viewBox, markerWidth/Height, refX/refY, orient, markerUnits) and reference it by id from markerStart/markerEnd; the markers example defines a multi-path 'logo' marker and a per-edge 'selected-marker' defined inside a custom edge that swaps on selection.
terminal_note: Arbitrary vector marker artwork can't render in a char cell. Nearest analog: a user-supplied single glyph per marker id.
evidence: No SVG <defs>/vector artwork in a char-cell renderer; flow's endpoint marker is a single fixed glyph (src/flow_route.h:60,103). grep 'defs'/'marker' → no relevant hits.

name: getBezierPath()
description: Util returning [path, labelX, labelY, offsetX, offsetY] for a bezier edge. Params: sourceX, sourceY, sourcePosition(default Position.Bottom), targetX, targetY, targetPosition(default Position.Top), curvature(default 0.25). labelX/Y are the path center; offsetX/Y are abs difference between source and path middle.
terminal_note: Return contract ports as a router returning a cell polyline + midpoint; cubic-bezier geometry would have to be rasterized to cells.
evidence: No bezier router; flow ships orthogonal+straight only (src/flow_route.h:34,70). Spec §1 non-goal cuts bezier ('collapse to box-drawing corners'); workpackages 'Deferred beyond increment 3' ~line 151 explicitly says do NOT add a curved router.

name: getSimpleBezierPath()
description: Util returning [path, labelX, labelY, offsetX, offsetY] for a simpler bezier curve. Params: sourceX, sourceY, sourcePosition(default Bottom), targetX, targetY, targetPosition(default Top). No curvature parameter — a fixed, simpler curve than getBezierPath.
terminal_note: Portable signature/return, but the smooth curve needs a stepped cell approximation.
evidence: No simplebezier router (src/flow_route.h only orthogonal+straight). Spec §1 cuts curved edges; workpackages ~line 151 defers bezier/smoothstep family ('do NOT add a curved router').

name: getSmoothStepPath()
description: Util returning [path, labelX, labelY, offsetX, offsetY] for an orthogonal stepped path with rounded corners. Params: sourceX, sourceY, sourcePosition(Bottom), targetX, targetY, targetPosition(Top), borderRadius(default 5), centerX, centerY, offset(default 20), stepPosition(default 0.5; 0=at source,1=at target,0.5=midpoint controls where the bend occurs). Setting borderRadius:0 yields a step edge.
terminal_note: Orthogonal routing with offset/stepPosition maps well to box-drawing routing; only borderRadius (rounding) lacks a cell analog. flow's orthogonal router is the borderRadius:0 (step) case, with the bend fixed at midpoint and no offset/stepPosition options.
evidence: flow_route_orthogonal produces an H-V-H / V-H-V stepped path with sharp box-drawing corners (┌┐└┘) and label_anchor at midpoint (src/flow_route.h:34-65). Delta: this is the borderRadius:0 'step' variant only; no rounded corners, no configurable offset/centerX/Y/stepPosition (bend hardcoded to mid column/row, :40,:45).

name: getStraightPath()
description: Util returning [path, labelX, labelY, offsetX, offsetY] for a direct line between source and target. Params: sourceX, sourceY, targetX, targetY (no position/curvature). labelX/Y = midpoint.
terminal_note: Straight line + midpoint label is the most TUI-friendly path: drawn with ─│ and diagonal glyphs (DDA stepping).
evidence: flow_route_straight: integer-DDA direct line s→t with ─/│/╲/╱ glyphs, target arrowhead, and label_anchor = midpoint (src/flow_route.h:70-107); registered as the 'straight' edge type (src/flow_types.h:51). Delta: returns a cell polyline + label center, not offsetX/Y.

name: Path utility tuple return convention
description: All four path utils return a fixed-size tuple [path:string, labelX:number, labelY:number, offsetX:number, offsetY:number] so multiple edge paths can be destructured easily; labelX/Y are the path center for placing labels, offsetX/Y are abs differences between source and path middle.
source: Returns sections of get-bezier-path / get-smooth-step-path etc. — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/utils/get-bezier-path
terminal_note: A small struct {polyline, label center}; the convention ports, minus offsetX/Y.
evidence: flow_route is an out-struct carrying the polyline (cells/count) plus label_anchor (the path center used to place labels), filled by every router and consumed by render at src/flow_render.h:178-186 / src/flow_route.h:62,106. Delta: no offsetX/offsetY fields.

name: BaseEdge component
description: Internal edge renderer reusable in custom edges. Renders the visible SVG <path>, the invisible interaction (helper) path, and the edge label. Props: path(string), markerStart(string url id), markerEnd(string url id), label, labelStyle, labelShowBg, labelBgStyle, labelBgPadding, labelBgBorderRadius, labelX, labelY, interactionWidth(default 20), plus passthrough SVGPathElement attributes (excluding d/path/markerStart/markerEnd).
terminal_note: Maps to the engine's edge loop: draw the routed cell polyline, draw the label at label_anchor, register a hit region. The SVG-path/marker-url props need TUI substitutes.
evidence: The render edge loop draws the routed polyline + arrowhead, draws the label at rt.label_anchor, and flow_hit_edge provides the helper hit path (src/flow_render.h:178-189, :122). Delta: it is the engine loop, not a reusable component callable from a custom edge; no markerStart/labelBg* props.

name: BaseEdge invisible interaction path
description: BaseEdge draws an invisible widened path (interactionWidth, default 20) around the visible edge to enlarge the clickable/hoverable hit area.
terminal_note: Implemented as a distance-based hit test (Chebyshev tol cells from the routed line) rather than an invisible shape — exactly the cell analog, valuable for picking thin lines.
evidence: flow_hit_edge re-routes each edge and returns the topmost whose path passes within Chebyshev `tol` of the screen cell (src/flow_render.h:122-138). Delta: tol is a call-time argument (1 at call sites src/flow_input.h:118,266), not a per-edge interactionWidth.

name: EdgeText component
description: SVG helper to render edge label text. Props: x, y (render position), label(ReactNode), labelStyle, labelShowBg, labelBgStyle, labelBgPadding, labelBgBorderRadius, plus standard SVGElement/HTML attrs (onClick, className) excluding x/y. Type exported as EdgeTextProps.
terminal_note: Text at a cell position ports well; the background/border-radius/CSS props only partially map (no bg drawn in flow).
evidence: Edge label text is rendered glyph-by-glyph at the label_anchor cell in the render edge loop (src/flow_render.h:183-186). Delta: inline in the loop (not a separate EdgeText component), string only, no label background, no styling props.

name: EdgeLabelRenderer component
description: A portal that renders edge labels as HTML <div>s into one shared container above the SVG edge layer, escaping the SVG world so labels can be arbitrary HTML/components. Only prop is children. Labels must be positioned manually (position:absolute + transform: translate(-50%,-50%) translate(labelXpx,labelYpx)), need pointerEvents:'all' to be interactive, and the 'nodrag nopan' classes to stop pan/drag.
terminal_note: The 'rich labels in a separate overlay layer at the edge midpoint' idea could port as a top overlay buffer; the HTML/portal/transform/pointer-events mechanics are DOM-specific. flow draws labels inline in the edge layer, not via a separate interactive overlay.
evidence: flow draws plain-string labels inline in the edge layer (src/flow_render.h:183-186); there is a generic on_overlay HUD hook (src/flow_model.h:181) but no edge-label-specific interactive overlay layer. Not in spec; the rich/interactive-label portal concept is unbuilt.

name: Manual edge-label positioning via labelX/labelY
description: Path utils return labelX/labelY (path midpoint); custom edges translate their EdgeLabelRenderer label to that point using a CSS transform so the label sits centered on the edge.
terminal_note: Place the label at the router-returned midpoint cell; trivial coordinate math.
evidence: Routers set out->label_anchor (orthogonal: p[n/2], src/flow_route.h:62; straight: midpoint, :106) and the render loop places the label at that cell (src/flow_render.h:184). Delta: label is left-anchored at label_anchor (gx++ from anchor), not centered with a -50% transform.

name: Interactive edge label (delete button) pattern
description: Custom edge renders a button in EdgeLabelRenderer that deletes the edge on click, via either useReactFlow().deleteElements({edges:[{id}]}) or setEdges(es => es.filter(e=>e.id!==id)). Demonstrates per-edge interactive controls.
terminal_note: A clickable/keyboard hotspot at the edge midpoint that deletes the edge; the deletion primitive (flow_remove_edge) exists but the midpoint hotspot control does not.
evidence: flow has flow_remove_edge and edge-body click-select + delete (src/flow_model.h:105; src/flow_input.h:266-268, delete via flow_delete_selection :118) but no per-edge midpoint button/control. The interactive-label control pattern is not in spec.

name: Custom SVG edge path strings (M/L/Q commands)
description: Beyond the four utils, you can author the path string yourself with SVG commands — M (move to), L (line to), Q (quadratic bezier control+end) — starting at sourceX/sourceY and ending at targetX/targetY, to build shapes like step or sinusoidal edges.
source: custom-edges learn page 'Custom SVG edge paths' (Step + Sine examples) — https://reactflow.dev/learn/customization/custom-edges
terminal_note: The 'compute your own polyline' freedom ports via a custom route() hook (push cells); M/L map to cell moves/lines, Q-curves need rasterization.
evidence: A custom flow_edge_type.route() can push any cell sequence via flow_route_push (src/flow_route.h:12) — the orthogonal and straight routers prove this seam (src/flow_route.h:34,70); register via flow_register_edge_type. Delta: the unit is cells (no Q-curve primitive).

name: Built-in animated edge (dashdraw)
description: Setting animated:true adds the .react-flow__edge.animated class which animates the SVG stroke dash (moving dashes) on the default edge path to indicate flow direction.
terminal_note: Marching-ants: cycle a dash glyph pattern along the routed polyline on a timer; needs an animation tick.
evidence: No animated edge field or per-frame dash cycling; edges draw static box-drawing glyphs (src/flow_render.h:180-182). grep 'animated'/'dash' → no hits. §1's animation cut targets smooth/sub-cell tweens; in-grid marching-ants is unaddressed → genuine candidate.

name: Animating SVG element along edge path (animateMotion)
description: Example: a custom edge renders an SVG <circle> with <animateMotion path={edgePath}/> so a shape travels along the edge path (using getSmoothStepPath). Demonstrates motion along the computed path.
terminal_note: Step a glyph (e.g. ●) cell-by-cell along the routed polyline over time; ports conceptually but needs per-frame interpolation along the cell path.
evidence: No traveling-glyph animation; routers emit a static cell polyline (src/flow_route.h) with no per-frame position along it. grep 'animateMotion'/'animated' → no hits. §1 cuts only smooth/sub-cell tweens, not in-grid glyph stepping → candidate.

name: Animating a node along an edge (offsetPath/offsetDistance)
description: Example: a custom edge sets a DOM node's CSS offsetPath to the edge path and animates offsetDistance 0%→100% via the Web Animations API to move an actual node along the edge; temporarily disables the node's draggable.
terminal_note: Relies on CSS offset-path/offset-distance + Web Animations API — DOM/CSS only. Nearest analog: manually step the node's cell position along the routed polyline.
evidence: CSS offsetPath/offsetDistance and the Web Animations API have no char-cell equivalent; flow has no node-along-path animation primitive (grep 'offsetPath'/'offsetDistance' → no hits). Nearest analog is manual per-frame flow_move_node stepping.

name: Edge intersection detection (node dragged over edge)
description: Example: detect when a dragged node intersects an edge by widening interactionWidth (set to 75 via defaultEdgeOptions) and testing node/edge overlap during drag. Used to e.g. drop a node onto an edge.
terminal_note: A geometric overlap test between a node's cell rect and an edge's routed polyline; portable (flow_hit_edge already does point/segment distance), but no node-vs-edge intersection during drag is wired.
evidence: flow_hit_edge does point-to-path Chebyshev distance (src/flow_render.h:122-138) but there is no node-rect-vs-edge intersection check during a node drag (drag release does group reparent only, src/flow_input.h:200-242). Not in spec.

name: Floating edges (dynamic endpoint computation)
description: Example: source/target connection points are computed dynamically so the edge attaches to the nearest side of each node. getNodeIntersection() computes the rect-perimeter intersection point toward the other node's center; getEdgePosition() picks Top/Right/Bottom/Left; getEdgeParams() returns sx,sy,tx,ty,sourcePos,targetPos for the edge.
terminal_note: Rect-perimeter intersection math ports to integer cells; flow currently anchors to the declared handle side, not a dynamically chosen nearest side.
evidence: flow__edge_screen_ends fixes the endpoint facing from the declared handle's side (sh->pos/th->pos, src/flow_render.h:108-114), not by computing the perimeter intersection toward the other node's center. No getNodeIntersection/getEdgeParams analog. Not in spec.

name: Simple floating edges (side-snapping variant)
description: Simplified floating-edge implementation where edges stick to the top/right/bottom/left side of nodes (less flexible than full floating edges) using a utils helper to pick the side.
terminal_note: Snap endpoints to the nearest of 4 node sides; TUI-friendly. flow snaps to a declared handle side rather than dynamically picking the nearest side to the peer.
evidence: Endpoint sides come from the static declared handle (src/flow_render.h:108-114); there is no helper that dynamically picks the nearest of the 4 sides toward the other node. Not in spec.

name: Edge reconnection (onReconnect + reconnectEdge)
description: An edge is reconnectable by dragging its end to another handle when the onReconnect handler is set. onReconnect(oldEdge, newConnection) fires after drop on a new handle; use the reconnectEdge(oldEdge, newConnection, edges) helper to update edges state. Per-edge `reconnectable` ('source'|'target'|true) limits which end is draggable.
terminal_note: State update + the drag-to-new-handle gesture both port. flow implements the full gesture and mutator for both ends.
evidence: flow_reconnect_edge(f, edge, endpoint_node, handle, which) repoints an endpoint with revalidation (src/flow_model.h:106); the drag gesture arms on an endpoint cell and drops on a node (src/flow_input.h:115-130, 235-242), bracketed as one undo step. Delta: drop retargets the node (handle passed ""), and there is no per-edge reconnectable gate (see Edge.reconnectable).

name: onReconnectStart / onReconnectEnd handlers
description: onReconnectStart fires when the user begins dragging an edge's source/target; onReconnectEnd fires when released (with the edge, handleType, and final connectionState). Used together to detect drops that miss a handle.
terminal_note: Begin/end gesture callbacks for endpoint dragging; flow runs the gesture internally but exposes no start/end reconnect callbacks.
evidence: The reconnect gesture has internal arm/release branches (src/flow_input.h:115-130,235-242) but the flow_callbacks struct (src/flow_model.h:180-189) has no on_reconnect_start/end. grep 'on_reconnect' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Delete edge on drop (drop on pane removes edge)
description: Pattern using a useRef flag set false in onReconnectStart and true in onReconnect; in onReconnectEnd, if reconnection wasn't successful (dropped on empty pane, not a handle) the edge is filtered out of edges. Net effect: dragging an edge end onto blank canvas deletes it.
terminal_note: State-machine pattern (track success across start/reconnect/end, delete on miss); portable. flow's reconnect release leaves the edge intact on a missed drop rather than deleting it.
evidence: On reconnect release, if no node is hit flow leaves the edge unchanged (it only calls flow_reconnect_edge when hit != -1, src/flow_input.h:237-238) — no delete-on-miss. The primitive flow_remove_edge exists (src/flow_model.h:105) but the pattern is not wired; not in spec.

name: Edge routing with libavoid (Pro)
description: Pro example: a custom edge that uses the libavoid (libavoid-js) library for automatic obstacle-avoiding orthogonal edge routing around nodes.
terminal_note: Auto-routing (avoid node rects, orthogonal) could port to a grid pathfinder; the JS lib does not. flow's orthogonal router is a fixed mid-bend path that does not avoid obstacles.
evidence: flow_route_orthogonal is a naive H-V-H/V-H-V path with no obstacle avoidance (src/flow_route.h:34-65); no A*/grid router. grep 'avoid' → no hits. Not in spec (no external deps per overview goal).

name: Button Edge UI component
description: Community/registry edge (shadcn install) exposing a <ButtonEdge> wrapper that renders a centered button (children) on the edge to trigger custom actions (e.g. alert/delete). Installed via `shadcn add https://ui.reactflow.dev/button-edge`. Depends on @xyflow/react, lucide-react, shadcn button.
terminal_note: A centered actionable hotspot on an edge could port as a focusable midpoint control; the shadcn/React/lucide packaging is web-only.
evidence: No edge-midpoint button control in flow (label is plain text, src/flow_render.h:183-186); no community-component story. grep 'ButtonEdge' → no hits; not in spec.

name: Data Edge UI component
description: Community/registry edge that displays one field from the source node's data object on the edge label. Edge data has a `key` naming which node-data field to show; TypeScript `satisfies DataEdge<NodeType>` enforces the key exists on the node's data. Installed via shadcn.
terminal_note: Render a value pulled from the source node's data as the edge label — portable (read source node field, draw at midpoint); only the TS type-safety is web tooling.
evidence: Edge labels are static heap strings, not bound to live source-node data (src/flow_render.h:183-186; flow_set_edge_label src/flow_model.h:107). No data-key→source-field binding. Not in spec; an app could approximate it by setting the label, but no built-in does.

name: Animated SVG Edge UI component
description: Community/registry edge that animates a custom SVG shape along the edge path. Edge data: { duration, shape, path:'smoothstep'|... }. Shapes are a `shapes` record mapping a shape key to a component taking animateMotionProps and returning SVG (e.g. box, package); HTML can be embedded via <foreignObject>. Installed via shadcn.
terminal_note: A configurable glyph traveling along the path could port as a parameterized marching glyph; SVG/foreignObject shape rendering does not.
evidence: No traveling-glyph animation or per-edge animation config (static routed polyline, src/flow_route.h). grep 'animated'/'shape' → no hits; not in spec (in-grid glyph animation is unaddressed, see Built-in animated edge).

name: Edge CSS class hooks
description: Edges expose styling hooks: .react-flow__edge (each edge), .react-flow__edge.selected (when selected), .react-flow__edge.animated (when animated), .react-flow__edge-path (the SVG <path>), .react-flow__edge-text (label text), .react-flow__edge-textbg (label background), and .updating (while reconnecting via onReconnect).
source: Theming/CSS class reference — https://reactflow.dev/learn/customization/theming (edge classes table in llms-full.txt)
terminal_note: CSS class selectors are DOM-only. Nearest analog: per-state style tokens chosen at draw time — flow only hardcodes a selected→bold rule, with no general token system.
evidence: No stylesheet/class cascade in a char-cell renderer; the only per-state styling is the inline selected→FLOW_BOLD branch (src/flow_render.h:180). grep 'className'/'css' → no hits. Nearest analog (a style-token enum) is not built.

name: onNodeClick
description: Fires when a user clicks (single-click) on a node. Signature NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event: React.MouseEvent, node: Node) => void. Receives the DOM mouse event and the clicked node object.
terminal_note: Map to a left-button press SGR mouse report (button 0 release) whose cell falls inside a node's bounding box; dispatch handler with the hit node.
evidence: on_node_click callback declared src/flow_model.h:183; fired on a no-drag left-click over a node body at src/flow_input.h:258. Delta: handler shape is (flow_t*,int node,void*), no event object.

name: onNodeDoubleClick
description: Fires when a user double-clicks a node. NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event, node) => void.
terminal_note: Terminals have no native double-click; synthesize by timing two left-clicks on the same node within a threshold (e.g. <400ms, same cell region).
evidence: on_node_dblclick declared src/flow_model.h:186, fired src/flow_input.h:259-261. Delta: NO time threshold — two consecutive plain clicks on the same node id at any interval count as a double-click (last_click_node compare, src/flow_input.h:259); the <400ms timing in the note is not implemented.

name: onNodeDragStart
description: Fires when a user begins dragging a node. OnNodeDrag<Node>: (event, node, nodes) => void; third arg is the array of all dragged nodes (relevant for multi-select drags). Also fires when dragging a multi-node selection.
terminal_note: Emit on first pointer-motion report after a press on a node that crosses nodeDragThreshold; carry the node and (for selections) the full dragged set.
evidence: Node drag interaction exists (arm at src/flow_input.h:166-174, move via flow_move_node), but NO drag callback fires: grep 'on_node_drag\|dragstart' src/ → no hits. The flow_callbacks struct (src/flow_model.h:181-188) has no drag observer.

name: onNodeDrag
description: Fires continuously while a node is being dragged. OnNodeDrag<Node>: (event, node, nodes) => void. Per migration notes, also fires when dragging a selection (alongside onSelectionDrag).
terminal_note: Emit on each mouse-motion report while a drag is active; throttle to terminal frame cadence since reports can be frequent.
evidence: Continuous node drag works (motion branch src/flow_input.h:204-220 calls flow_move_node each report) but emits no callback: grep 'on_node_drag' src/ → no hits; no drag field in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188).

name: onNodeDragStop
description: Fires when a user stops dragging a node (pointer release). OnNodeDrag<Node>: (event, node, nodes) => void. Also fires at the end of a selection drag.
terminal_note: Emit on the button-release mouse report that ends an active node/selection drag.
evidence: Drag release is handled (src/flow_input.h:275-308, closes undo txn, may reparent) but no stop callback fires: grep 'on_node_drag\|dragstop' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodeMouseEnter
description: Fires when the pointer enters a node. NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event, node) => void.
terminal_note: Requires SGR mouse-motion tracking (mode 1003) so the app sees the cursor cell each move; derive enter by detecting the move where the hit-tested node changes from none/other to this node. Coarser than DOM hover.
evidence: No enter callback and no any-motion plumbing: terminal enables mode 1002 (button-event motion only), NOT 1003, at src/flow_term.h:16. FLOW_HOVERED flag + flow_set_hover exist (src/flow_model.h:82,970) but are not auto-driven by motion, and no on_node_mouse_enter callback exists. grep 'mouse_enter\|on_node_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodeMouseMove
description: Fires when the pointer moves over a node. NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event, node) => void.
terminal_note: Needs any-motion mouse reporting (mode 1003); emit per motion report while the hit node is unchanged. Cell granularity, not pixel.
evidence: No move-over-node callback; only mode 1002 enabled (src/flow_term.h:16) so button-up motion is not reported, and motion events are only consumed for active drag/pan/connect (src/flow_input.h:149-225). grep 'mouse_move\|on_node_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodeMouseLeave
description: Fires when the pointer leaves a node. NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event, node) => void.
terminal_note: Derive from motion reporting: emit when hit-tested node transitions away from the previously-entered node. Depends on mode 1003 being enabled.
evidence: No leave callback; no hover-transition tracking (mode 1002 only, src/flow_term.h:16). grep 'mouse_leave\|on_node_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodeContextMenu
description: Fires when a user right-clicks a node. NodeMouseHandler<Node>: (event, node) => void.
terminal_note: Map to an xterm button-2 (right button, code 2) press report over a node; the app must render its own menu since there is no OS context menu.
evidence: on_node_context callback declared src/flow_model.h:182, fired on a button==2 (right) press over a hit node at src/flow_input.h:82-85; topo demo wires it to a details overlay (demos/topo.c:114). Delta: signature carries screen pt, not a DOM event.

name: onEdgeClick
description: Fires when a user clicks an edge. (event: MouseEvent, edge: Edge) => void.
terminal_note: Edges are thin paths; hit-testing a click against a rendered line in a char grid needs a per-cell proximity test along the routed edge cells. Feasible but coarse.
evidence: Edge click-select works (flow_hit_edge + flow_select_edge, src/flow_input.h:266-268) but no observer callback fires: grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits. flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188) has no edge events.

name: onEdgeDoubleClick
description: Fires when a user double-clicks an edge. EdgeMouseHandler<Edge>: (event, edge) => void.
terminal_note: Combine synthesized double-click timing with edge-cell hit-testing; both pieces are approximations in a TUI.
evidence: No edge double-click; the dblclick path (src/flow_input.h:259) is node-only (last_click_node), and there is no on_edge callback at all: grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: onEdgeMouseEnter
description: Fires when the pointer enters an edge. EdgeMouseHandler<Edge>: (event, edge) => void.
terminal_note: Needs motion reporting plus edge-cell hit-test; emit when cursor cell first lands on an edge's drawn cells.
evidence: No edge hover callback and no any-motion tracking (mode 1002 only, src/flow_term.h:16). grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: onEdgeMouseMove
description: Fires when the pointer moves over an edge. EdgeMouseHandler<Edge>: (event, edge) => void.
terminal_note: Per-motion-report emission while cursor stays on edge cells; requires mode 1003 and edge rasterization to cells.
evidence: No edge move-over callback; no on_edge events in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188); mode 1002 only (src/flow_term.h:16). grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: onEdgeMouseLeave
description: Fires when the pointer leaves an edge. EdgeMouseHandler<Edge>: (event, edge) => void.
terminal_note: Emit when cursor cell transitions off the previously-hovered edge's cells.
evidence: No edge leave callback; no hover-transition tracking. grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: onEdgeContextMenu
description: Fires when a user right-clicks an edge. EdgeMouseHandler<Edge>: (event, edge) => void.
terminal_note: Right-button (code 2) report hit-tested against edge cells; app renders its own menu.
evidence: Right-click handling (src/flow_input.h:82-86) hit-tests NODES only (flow_hit_node) and fires only on_node_context; no edge context callback exists. grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: onReconnect
description: Fires when the source or target of a reconnectable edge is dragged off the current node and dropped, even if endpoints don't change. OnReconnect<Edge>: (oldEdge: Edge, newConnection: Connection) => void. Use reconnectEdge utility to apply. Gated by edgesReconnectable + a provided handler (and per-edge reconnectable).
terminal_note: Implement as a drag begun on an edge endpoint cell and released on a handle cell; needs endpoint hit-zones rendered as distinct cells. Connection model itself ports.
evidence: Reconnect interaction is fully built (endpoint-drag arm src/flow_input.h:114-132, release repoint src/flow_input.h:236-241 via flow_reconnect_edge) but emits NO callback: grep 'on_reconnect' src/ → no hits. The reconnectEdge result is applied directly to the store, no observer hook.

name: onReconnectStart
description: Fires when the user begins dragging an edge's source/target endpoint. (event, edge: Edge, handleType: HandleType) => void; handleType is 'source' | 'target'.
terminal_note: Detect a press on a rendered edge-endpoint cell that begins a drag; pass which endpoint (source/target) was grabbed.
evidence: Endpoint-grab is detected (reconnect_which set src/flow_input.h:124-127) but no start callback fires: grep 'on_reconnect' src/ → no hits.

name: onReconnectEnd
description: Fires when the user releases an edge endpoint drag, even if no reconnection occurred. (event, edge, handleType, connectionState: FinalConnectionState) => void. connectionState lets you handle drop-on-pane (e.g. delete the edge).
terminal_note: Emit on release of an endpoint drag; supply a final-state struct indicating whether release landed on a valid handle cell or empty pane (drop-on-pane = delete pattern).
evidence: Endpoint-drag release is handled (src/flow_input.h:236-245) but no end callback fires: grep 'on_reconnect' src/ → no hits.

name: onPaneClick
description: Fires when the user clicks inside the empty pane (background). (event: MouseEvent) => void. Commonly used to clear selection.
terminal_note: Left-click report whose hit-test matches no node/edge cell; dispatch as pane click.
evidence: on_pane_click declared src/flow_model.h:184; fired on a no-drag left-click that hits neither node nor edge (after clear_selection) at src/flow_input.h:270-271, passing the world point. Delta: payload is flow_pt world, not a DOM event.

name: onPaneContextMenu
description: Fires when the user right-clicks the pane. (event: MouseEvent | React.MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Right-button (code 2) report over empty pane; app must draw its own context menu overlay.
evidence: Right-click (src/flow_input.h:82-86) fires only when a node is hit (on_node_context); a right-click on empty pane fires nothing. grep 'on_pane_context' src/ → no hits.

name: onPaneScroll
description: Fires when the user scrolls inside the pane. (event?: WheelEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Map to mouse wheel reports (xterm button 64/65 = wheel up/down) over the pane; no pixel delta, only discrete notch events.
evidence: Wheel reports are decoded and pan the viewport (src/flow_input.h:73-77) but no scroll observer callback fires: grep 'on_pane_scroll\|on_scroll' src/ → no hits.

name: onPaneMouseMove
description: Fires when the pointer moves over the pane. (event: MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Requires any-motion mouse reporting (mode 1003); emit per motion report when the cursor cell is over empty pane.
evidence: No pane-move callback and no any-motion tracking (mode 1002 only, src/flow_term.h:16). grep 'on_pane_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onPaneMouseEnter
description: Fires when the pointer enters the pane. (event: MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Approximate as the cursor entering the flow widget's terminal region; depends on motion reporting and widget-bounds tracking.
evidence: No pane-enter callback; the flow widget owns the whole screen and no motion-into-region tracking exists (mode 1002 only). grep 'on_pane_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onPaneMouseLeave
description: Fires when the pointer leaves the pane. (event: MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Emit when cursor cell exits the flow widget bounds; terminals report motion only while focused/over-terminal so leaving the terminal entirely may not report.
evidence: No pane-leave callback; no widget-bounds motion tracking. grep 'on_pane_mouse' src/ → no hits.

name: onMove
description: Fires continuously while the user pans or zooms the viewport. OnMove: (event: MouseEvent | TouchEvent | null, viewport: Viewport) => void. Also fires for library-invoked viewport updates (fitView, zoomTo); event is null when movement is not user-initiated.
source: <ReactFlow> prop `onMove` (Pane Events); OnMove type; v12 migration note — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/on-init
terminal_note: Emit during pan (drag on empty pane) and zoom; supply the new {x,y,zoom} viewport. Pass null source-event for programmatic fit/zoom, matching docs.
evidence: Pan/zoom mutate f->view (flow_pan, flow_set_zoom, flow_fit_view in src/flow_view.h / flow_model.h) but emit no viewport-change callback: grep 'on_move' src/ → no hits. flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188) has no viewport observer.

name: onMoveStart
description: Fires when the user begins panning or zooming the viewport. OnMove signature.
terminal_note: Emit on first pan-drag motion or first zoom step; carry viewport snapshot.
evidence: Pan/zoom begin is not observable: grep 'on_move' src/ → no hits; no viewport callback field in flow_callbacks.

name: onMoveEnd
description: Fires when panning/zooming movement stops. OnMove signature; event param is null if movement was not user-initiated.
terminal_note: Emit on pan-drag release or settle of a zoom animation; null event for programmatic moves.
evidence: No move-end callback: grep 'on_move' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionChange
description: Fires whenever the set of selected nodes/edges changes. OnSelectionChangeFunc: (params: { nodes: Node[]; edges: Edge[] }) => void — receives the full current selection arrays.
terminal_note: Recompute selected sets on any select/deselect (click, box-select, keyboard) and invoke with current selected nodes+edges. Pure state callback, ports directly.
evidence: on_selection_change declared src/flow_model.h:187; flow__notify_selection (src/flow_model.h:653-661) fires it (only on actual change, via flow__sel_sig) from flow_select_node/toggle/clear/select_in_rect (src/flow_model.h:675-735) and after deletes. Delta: payload is NODE ids only (sel_sig is over nodes, src/flow_model.h:647), not the xyflow {nodes, edges} pair — edge selection does not drive it.

name: onSelectionDragStart
description: Fires when the user starts dragging a multi-node selection box. SelectionDragHandler<Node>: (event, nodes: Node[]) => void.
terminal_note: Emit when a drag begins on the selected group; note onNodeDragStart also fires (per migration). Carry the selected node array.
evidence: Multi-node group drag exists (selection-roots delta src/flow_input.h:204-220) but no selection-drag callback fires: grep 'on_selection_drag\|selection_drag' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionDrag
description: Fires continuously while a selection of nodes is dragged. SelectionDragHandler<Node>: (event, nodes) => void.
terminal_note: Emit per motion report during group drag; onNodeDrag also fires for the same gesture.
evidence: Group drag motion works but emits no callback: grep 'on_selection_drag' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionDragStop
description: Fires when dragging of a node selection stops. SelectionDragHandler<Node>: (event, nodes) => void.
terminal_note: Emit on release of group drag; onNodeDragStop also fires.
evidence: Group drag release is handled (src/flow_input.h:304-308) but no stop callback fires: grep 'on_selection_drag' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionStart
description: Fires when the user begins drawing a selection box. (event: MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Emit when a box-select gesture begins (selectionKeyCode held + drag, or selectionOnDrag); anchor at the press cell.
evidence: Marquee box-select begins on shift-drag (marquee_active armed src/flow_input.h:145-146) but emits no callback: grep 'on_selection_start\|on_selection_end' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionEnd
description: Fires when the user finishes drawing a selection box. (event: MouseEvent) => void.
terminal_note: Emit on release that completes the box-select; finalize the rectangular cell region selection.
evidence: Marquee finalize on release works (src/flow_input.h:302-303) but emits no box-select callback (only on_selection_change fires via the underlying flow_select_in_rect): grep 'on_selection_end' src/ → no hits.

name: onSelectionContextMenu
description: Fires when the user right-clicks on a node selection. (event: MouseEvent, nodes: Node[]) => void — receives the selected nodes.
terminal_note: Right-button report over a multi-selected group; supply selected nodes. App renders its own menu.
evidence: Right-click fires only on_node_context for the single hit node (src/flow_input.h:82-85); no selection-aware context callback. grep 'on_selection_context' src/ → no hits.

name: onInit
description: Called once when the viewport/instance is initialized. (reactFlowInstance: ReactFlowInstance<Node, Edge>) => void. At this point you can call fitView, zoomTo, addNodes, etc. In uncontrolled mode this is the way to grab the instance for programmatic updates.
terminal_note: Fire after first layout/measure pass when node sizes and viewport are known; hand the caller an instance/context struct with fit/zoom/add helpers.
evidence: No init callback; the caller already holds the flow_t* from flow_new (src/flow_model.h) and calls fit/zoom/add directly, so the React 'grab the instance after mount' hook has no analog and none is provided. grep 'on_init' src/ → no hits.

name: onError
description: Called instead of throwing when React Flow hits a recoverable error: (id: string, message: string) => void. The library logs to console then calls this; use for custom logging or user messaging. Docs do not enumerate a fixed error-code list beyond id+message.
terminal_note: Implement as a library-wide error callback taking an id string and message; default behavior writes to a log/stderr then invokes the hook. Pure callback.
evidence: No error callback or error pipeline; mutators reject silently (e.g. flow_add_edge validation returns -1, flow_set_parent cycle guard is a no-op) without an observer hook. grep 'on_error' src/ → no hits; flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188) has no error field.

name: onDelete (combined delete handler)
description: Combined handler called when nodes OR edges are deleted: OnDelete: (params: { nodes: Node[]; edges: Edge[] }) => void. New in v12 to react to both deletions in one place (vs separate onNodesDelete/onEdgesDelete).
terminal_note: Fire once per delete operation with the combined deleted nodes+edges arrays; pure data callback, ports directly.
evidence: Only on_nodes_delete exists (src/flow_model.h:188); no combined node+edge delete callback. Cascaded edge removal during node delete fires nothing for edges. grep 'on_delete\b\|on_edges_delete' src/ → no hits.

name: onBeforeDelete (abort/modify deletion)
description: Async guard called before a delete proceeds. OnBeforeDelete: (params: { nodes: Node[]; edges: Edge[] }) => Promise<boolean | { nodes: Node[]; edges: Edge[] }>. Returning false aborts the deletion; returning an object replaces the to-be-deleted set, letting you filter/modify what actually gets removed.
terminal_note: Implement as a pre-delete hook returning a bool or a revised delete set; if async isn't available in the C event loop, support a synchronous predicate returning the same shapes.
evidence: No pre-delete guard hook; flow_delete_selection/flow_remove_node delete immediately (src/flow_model.h:788-842) with no abort/modify predicate. grep 'on_before_delete\|before_delete' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodesDelete
description: Called when one or more nodes are deleted. OnNodesDelete<Node>: (nodes: Node[]) => void — receives the deleted nodes.
terminal_note: Fire with the array of removed nodes after a delete commits; pure callback.
evidence: on_nodes_delete declared src/flow_model.h:188; fired once per delete op with removed node ids (incl. cascaded descendants) from flow_remove_node (src/flow_model.h:800-804) and flow_delete_selection (src/flow_model.h:838-842), cb_suppress-gated so a cascade reports once. Delta: payload is int ids, not Node objects.

name: onEdgesDelete
description: Called when one or more edges are deleted. OnEdgesDelete<Edge>: (edges: Edge[]) => void — receives the deleted edges (includes edges cascaded from node deletion).
terminal_note: Fire with removed edges array, including edges auto-removed because a connected node was deleted; pure callback.
evidence: No edge-delete callback. flow_remove_node cascades incident edges (src/flow_model.h handoff line 43) but fires nothing for them; only on_nodes_delete exists. grep 'on_edges_delete\|on_edge_delete' src/ → no hits.

name: onNodesChange (controlled change handler)
description: Primary controlled-mode handler: OnNodesChange<Node>: (changes: NodeChange<Node>[]) => void. Called on node drag, select, move, dimension, add, remove. You apply the changes (typically via applyNodeChanges) to your own state. Without it, a controlled flow's nodes are static.
terminal_note: Core of the change pipeline: emit a list of typed change records each tick of an interaction; caller mutates its node store. Maps to a change-event callback in C taking an array of tagged-union changes.
evidence: Spec §1 (lines 69-74) deliberately collapses the controlled change-descriptor contract: 'In C we own one store and mutate it directly. We keep only the discipline — every change goes through a small set of mutator functions... The change vocabulary returns, deliberately, as the undo/redo command layer (§11).' So there is no onNodesChange callback by design; the inverse-op journal (flow_undo, landed §11) is the spec's substitute. grep 'on_nodes_change' src/ → no hits.

name: onEdgesChange (controlled change handler)
description: Primary controlled-mode edge handler: OnEdgesChange<Edge>: (changes: EdgeChange<Edge>[]) => void. Called on edge select and remove. Apply via applyEdgeChanges.
terminal_note: Emit typed edge-change records on select/remove; caller updates its edge store. Tagged-union change array in C.
evidence: Same §1 collapse as onNodesChange (spec lines 69-74): the controlled change contract is intentionally replaced by direct mutators + the undo/redo command layer (§11). No onEdgesChange callback by design. grep 'on_edges_change' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeChange union (six variants)
description: Union of six change records emitted to onNodesChange: NodeDimensionChange {id,type:'dimensions',dimensions?,resizing?,setAttributes?:boolean|'width'|'height'}, NodePositionChange {id,type:'position',position?,positionAbsolute?,dragging?}, NodeSelectionChange {id,type:'select',selected}, NodeRemoveChange {id,type:'remove'}, NodeAddChange {item,type:'add',index?}, NodeReplaceChange {id,item,type:'replace'}.
terminal_note: Model as a tagged union enum (DIMENSIONS/POSITION/SELECT/REMOVE/ADD/REPLACE) with a payload struct per variant; dimensions/position carry cell-grid coords instead of pixels.
evidence: Spec §1 (lines 69-74) re-targets the change vocabulary onto the undo/redo command layer (§11). The landed journal (src/flow_undo.h, src/flow_model.h journal block) has command kinds add/remove node+edge, move (absolute), reconnect, set-label, reparent — analogous to the position/select(no)/remove/add variants. The DIMENSIONS variant is explicitly cut as a browser-only artifact (spec §4 lines 196-199: measure() collapses NodeDimensionChange). Not a public emitted NodeChange union. grep 'node_change\|NodeChange' src/ → no hits.

name: EdgeChange union (four variants)
description: Union of four change records emitted to onEdgesChange: EdgeAddChange {item,type:'add',index?}, EdgeRemoveChange {id,type:'remove'}, EdgeReplaceChange {id,item,type:'replace'}, EdgeSelectionChange {id,type:'select',selected}. (No position/dimension variants — edges are derived from node positions.)
terminal_note: Tagged union enum (ADD/REMOVE/REPLACE/SELECT) with per-variant payload; mirrors NodeChange minus geometry.
evidence: Same §1 collapse (spec lines 69-74): edge change records become undo journal commands (add/remove edge, reconnect, set-label in src/flow_undo.h). No public EdgeChange union is emitted. grep 'edge_change\|EdgeChange' src/ → no hits.

name: applyNodeChanges() utility
description: Drop-in reducer applyNodeChanges(changes: NodeChange[], nodes: Node[]) => Node[]. Takes an array of NodeChange records and returns a new nodes array with those changes applied (position updates, selection toggles, add/remove/replace, dimensions). Used inside onNodesChange when no custom behavior is needed.
terminal_note: Implement as a pure function folding the change list over a node array; pure data transform, no DOM. Direct C port.
evidence: Spec §1 (lines 69-74) collapses the apply-changes reducer: because C owns one mutable store, changes are applied by mutators directly (flow_move_node/flow_select_node/etc.) and inverses live in the undo journal, rather than a pure reducer over an immutable array. No applyNodeChanges analog. grep 'apply_node' src/ → no hits.

name: applyEdgeChanges() utility
description: Drop-in reducer applyEdgeChanges(changes: EdgeChange[], edges: Edge[]) => Edge[]. Applies add/remove/replace/select edge changes and returns a new edges array. Used inside onEdgesChange.
terminal_note: Pure fold over the edge array; direct C port.
evidence: Same §1 collapse (spec lines 69-74) as applyNodeChanges: edge mutations go through mutators on the single store, not a pure reducer. grep 'apply_edge' src/ → no hits.

name: deleteElements() — cascade deletion semantics
description: DeleteElements(payload: { nodes?: ({id}&Partial<Node>)[]; edges?: ({id}&Partial<Edge>)[] }) => Promise<{ deletedNodes: Node[]; deletedEdges: Edge[] }>. Deletes the given nodes/edges AND automatically cascades: connected edges of deleted nodes are removed, and child nodes of deleted parent nodes are removed. Returns the actually-deleted sets asynchronously.
terminal_note: Pure graph operation: collect target ids, transitively gather edges touching deleted nodes and descendant child nodes, remove all, return deleted sets. No DOM; the async Promise can be a synchronous return or callback in C.
evidence: flow_remove_node implements the exact cascade: removes incident edges AND child nodes (spec §4 line 206; handoff line 43 'cascades children + incident edges'); flow_delete_selection (src/flow_model.h:832) batch-deletes the selection with the same cascade. Delta: no single deleteElements(payload) entry taking a node+edge id set in one call and returning the deleted sets — caller loops the per-id mutators; on_nodes_delete reports deleted nodes but not the cascaded edges.

name: onBeforeDelete abort/modify protocol with onDelete/onNodesDelete/onEdgesDelete sequencing
description: Deletion pipeline: a delete trigger (deleteKeyCode press or deleteElements) first runs onBeforeDelete (may abort by returning false or modify the set), then the resulting nodes+edges are removed with edge/child cascade, then onNodesDelete, onEdgesDelete, and combined onDelete fire with the deleted sets.
terminal_note: Sequence is pure control flow: guard hook -> cascade compute -> commit -> fire delete callbacks. Ports as an ordered function pipeline.
evidence: Only a partial pipeline exists: trigger (Delete/x → flow_delete_selection, src/flow_model.h:901-902) → cascade (flow_remove_node) → on_nodes_delete fires (src/flow_model.h:838). Missing the onBeforeDelete guard, onEdgesDelete, and combined onDelete stages. grep 'on_before_delete\|on_edges_delete\|on_delete\b' src/ → no hits.

name: deleteKeyCode prop (delete-pipeline trigger)
description: deleteKeyCode?: KeyCode | null. Key or chord that deletes selected nodes/edges (default Backspace; commonly ['Delete','Backspace']). Array means any of the listed keys triggers deletion; null disables. This is the keyboard entry point into the delete/change pipeline.
terminal_note: Bind a configurable keycode (Delete/Backspace) that invokes deleteElements on the current selection; chord support maps to a set of accepted key sequences. Disable with null.
evidence: Delete (ESC[3~) and 'x' both invoke flow_delete_selection in the built-in dispatch (src/flow_model.h:901-902). Delta: NOT a configurable prop — the keys are hardwired built-ins; no null-to-disable. flow_bind_key (src/flow_model.h) lets an app override a key sequence to its own fn, but there is no deleteKeyCode setter/array/disable.

name: onNodeDrag* and onSelectionDrag* dual-firing on selection drag
description: Documented behavior: when a user drags a multi-node selection, onNodeDragStart/onNodeDrag/onNodeDragStop fire in addition to onSelectionDragStart/onSelectionDrag/onSelectionDragStop. Both families are emitted for the same gesture.
terminal_note: When porting group drag, emit both the per-node-drag and selection-drag event families for one gesture to preserve handler parity.
evidence: Neither the node-drag nor the selection-drag callback family exists (grep 'on_node_drag\|on_selection_drag' src/ → no hits), so there is nothing to dual-fire. Multi-node drag itself works (selection-roots delta src/flow_input.h:204-220) but is silent.

name: nodeDragThreshold prop (click-vs-drag gate)
description: nodeDragThreshold?: number. Pixels the pointer must move before drag events fire; default 1 so plain clicks don't trigger drags. A larger value delays drag onset.
terminal_note: Pixel threshold becomes a cell-distance threshold (e.g. >=1 cell moved) to distinguish a click from a drag in mouse reports; needs reinterpretation since terminal motion is cell-quantized.
evidence: A 1-cell move gate is the effective threshold: moved is set only when scr differs from down_pos (src/flow_input.h:166), so a same-cell press/release stays a click and any cell-move begins a drag — exactly xyflow's default=1 behavior. Delta: fixed at 1 cell, NOT a configurable prop (no setter); spec §8 documents the '1-cell threshold'.

name: nodeClickDistance prop
description: nodeClickDistance?: number. Max distance the mouse may move between mousedown and mouseup on a node to still count as a click (vs a drag).
terminal_note: Reinterpret as max cells moved between press and release to register a node click; coarse at cell granularity.
evidence: The click-vs-drag classification is fixed: any 1-cell move flips moved=1 and a node press only counts as a click if !moved (src/flow_input.h:248-264). There is no tunable click-distance prop — it is implicitly 0 cells of tolerance. grep 'click_distance' src/ → no hits.

name: paneClickDistance prop
description: paneClickDistance?: number. Max distance the mouse may move between mousedown and mouseup to register as a pane click (vs a pan).
terminal_note: Reinterpret as a max-cell-movement tolerance to distinguish a pane click from a pan drag.
evidence: Pane click vs pan is decided by the same fixed moved flag (pane click fires only when !moved, src/flow_input.h:265-272); no configurable pane-click-distance tolerance. grep 'click_distance' src/ → no hits.

name: debug prop (event logging)
description: debug?: boolean. When true, logs debug information to the console including which events are fired.
terminal_note: Map to a debug flag that writes fired-event traces to a log file or stderr (console has no terminal-UI equivalent while the TUI owns the screen).
evidence: No debug/event-trace flag in the engine. grep -i 'debug' src/ → no hits in the library. The TUI owns the screen so a logging seam would be a genuine future addition.

name: edgesReconnectable prop (reconnect lifecycle gate)
description: edgesReconnectable?: boolean. Whether edges can be reconnected after creation. Reconnect drag only works when this is true AND an onReconnect handler is provided; individual edges override via their reconnectable property.
terminal_note: Boolean gate is trivial to port; the reconnect interaction it enables is the partial-fit endpoint-drag described under onReconnect.
evidence: Reconnect is always-on: endpoint-drag (src/flow_input.h:114-132) is unconditional, with no global or per-edge reconnectable gate and no on_reconnect handler requirement. grep 'reconnectable\|edges_reconnectable' src/ → no hits.

name: reconnectRadius prop
description: reconnectRadius?: number. Radius around an edge connection that can trigger an edge reconnection when grabbing an endpoint.
terminal_note: Pixel radius becomes a small cell neighborhood around an endpoint within which a press grabs the edge for reconnection.
evidence: Endpoint grab requires an EXACT endpoint cell hit (ts.x==scr.x && ts.y==scr.y, src/flow_input.h:124-125) — a fixed zero-radius rule, not a configurable reconnectRadius neighborhood. grep 'reconnect_radius' src/ → no hits.

name: Controlled flow model
description: Controlled mode: pass nodes and edges props plus onNodesChange/onEdgesChange (and onConnect). React Flow manages no node/edge state besides the viewport; you own the arrays and apply emitted changes yourself. This is the recommended pattern for interactive apps.
terminal_note: Library renders from caller-owned node/edge arrays and emits change events; caller mutates and passes back. State ownership pattern ports directly to C.
evidence: Spec §1 (lines 69-74) explicitly REJECTS the controlled/owned-outside model as a React-immutability artifact: 'In C we own one store and mutate it directly.' The controlled pattern is intentionally not ported; its discipline survives as mutators + the undo command layer (§11). So it is spec-covered as a deliberate non-implementation.

name: Uncontrolled flow model (defaultNodes/defaultEdges)
description: Uncontrolled mode: pass defaultNodes and defaultEdges (initial state); React Flow manages node/edge state internally and handles drag/select/connect/remove with no handlers needed. To mutate, you must use the instance (via onInit or useReactFlow): addNodes, etc. defaultEdgeOptions supplies options for internally-created edges since onConnect isn't used.
terminal_note: Provide an internal-state mode seeded from default arrays where the library owns mutation, exposing an instance handle for programmatic add/remove. Pure state management, ports fully.
evidence: This IS the flow model: spec §4 'State lives in one engine-owned store' and §1 'we own one store and mutate it directly'. The engine owns nodes/edges (flow_t store, src/flow_model.h) and handles drag/select/connect/delete internally; the app mutates via flow_add_node/flow_add_edge with the flow_t* handle. Delta: no defaultNodes/defaultEdges seed props (caller builds the graph via add mutators after flow_new) and no onInit instance hook.

name: defaultNodes prop
description: defaultNodes?: Node[]. Initial nodes rendered in an uncontrolled flow (library then owns their state).
terminal_note: Seed value for internal node store in uncontrolled mode; trivial to port.
evidence: No seed-array prop; the caller populates the engine-owned store by calling flow_add_node per node after flow_new (demos/topo.c, demos/hello_flow.c do exactly this). grep 'default_nodes\|defaultNodes' src/ → no hits. A defaultNodes-style bulk seed has no spec coverage.

name: defaultEdges prop
description: defaultEdges?: Edge[]. Initial edges rendered in an uncontrolled flow.
