terminal_note: Seed value for internal edge store in uncontrolled mode; trivial to port.
evidence: No seed-array prop; edges are added via flow_add_edge after flow_new. grep 'default_edges\|defaultEdges' src/ → no hits. (flow_load rebuilds a whole graph from JSON, but that is persistence, not an initial-seed prop.)

name: NodeMouseHandler signature
description: Shared callback type for node mouse events (click/dblclick/enter/move/leave/contextmenu): (event: React.MouseEvent, node: Node) => void; generic over node type. Defines the (event, node) contract all node mouse handlers share.
terminal_note: In C, a function-pointer typedef taking a parsed terminal mouse-event struct and a node pointer; one shape reused across all node mouse callbacks.
evidence: A shared node-event shape exists and is reused: on_node_click/on_node_dblclick are (flow_t*, int node, void*) (src/flow_model.h:183,186) and on_node_context adds a screen pt (src/flow_model.h:182). Delta: realized as inline fn-ptr fields, not a single named typedef; payload is node id + optional pt, not a (MouseEvent, Node) pair; no enter/move/leave members.

name: EdgeMouseHandler signature
description: Shared callback type for edge mouse events (dblclick/enter/move/leave/contextmenu): (event: React.MouseEvent, edge: Edge) => void; generic over edge type. (Note: onEdgeClick is documented with an explicit inline signature rather than this alias, but matches the same shape.)
terminal_note: Function-pointer typedef taking a mouse-event struct and an edge pointer; reused across edge mouse callbacks.
evidence: No edge mouse callbacks exist at all, so no shared edge-handler signature is defined. grep 'on_edge' src/ → no hits; flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-188) has no edge-event member.

name: SelectionDragHandler signature
description: Callback type for dragging a node selection: (event: React.MouseEvent, nodes: Node[]) => void; generic over node type. Used by onSelectionDragStart/Drag/Stop.
terminal_note: Function-pointer typedef taking a mouse-event struct and an array of dragged nodes; reused across the three selection-drag callbacks.
evidence: No selection-drag callbacks exist, so no shared (event, nodes[]) selection-drag signature is defined. grep 'on_selection_drag' src/ → no hits.

name: useNodesState hook (change-pipeline wrapper)
description: Convenience hook wrapping React useState + applyNodeChanges: const [nodes, setNodes, onNodesChange] = useNodesState(initialNodes). Returns state, a setter, and a ready-made onNodesChange handler that applies changes. Sugar over the manual controlled pattern.
terminal_note: Port as a helper that bundles an internal node array, a mutator, and a pre-wired change-apply callback — a convenience constructor over applyNodeChanges; no React specifics needed.
evidence: This is React-state sugar over the controlled change pipeline that spec §1 (lines 69-74) deliberately collapses into the engine-owned store + mutators. With no useState/applyNodeChanges to wrap, the hook has no analog by design. grep 'use_nodes' src/ → no hits.

name: useEdgesState hook (change-pipeline wrapper)
description: Convenience hook wrapping useState + applyEdgeChanges: const [edges, setEdges, onEdgesChange] = useEdgesState(initialEdges). Returns edge state, setter, and a ready-made onEdgesChange handler.
terminal_note: Helper bundling an internal edge array, mutator, and pre-wired change-apply callback; convenience over applyEdgeChanges.
evidence: Same as useNodesState: React-state sugar over the change pipeline that spec §1 (lines 69-74) intentionally replaces with the single mutable store + mutators. grep 'use_edges' src/ → no hits.

name: Connection event family ownership note (onConnect/onConnectStart/onConnectEnd/onClickConnect*/isValidConnection)
description: The Event Handlers section also lists a Connection Events group: onConnect (OnConnect), onConnectStart, onConnectEnd (receives FinalConnectionState; fires whether or not a valid connection was made), onClickConnectStart, onClickConnectEnd, and isValidConnection (return false to reject an edge). These fire during interactive edge creation and overlap the event surface but are primarily the connections slice's domain.
terminal_note: Connection-creation drag from handle cells to handle cells with a validity callback; cataloged in depth by the connections agent. Listed here only to mark the event-surface boundary.
evidence: Partial: only on_connect fires, after a successful edge create (declared src/flow_model.h:185; fired post-flow_add_edge at src/flow_model.h:1016). Connect-by-drag and connectOnClick both work (src/flow_input.h:89-112, 228-232). Delta/missing: NO on_connect_start, on_connect_end, click-connect start/end, or isValidConnection reject hook — grep 'on_connect_start\|on_connect_end\|is_valid' src/ → no hits; validity is enforced only inside flow_add_edge (self-edge/dup/missing-endpoint reject, spec §4 line 214), not via a caller callback.

name: Viewport transform (x, y, zoom)
description: The viewport is the camera state: {x, y, zoom}. x/y are screen-pixel translation offsets and zoom is a multiplicative scale factor. Applied to content as CSS `translate(x px, y px) scale(zoom)` (confirmed in download-image example). Internally stored as `transform` = [x, y, zoom] array (accessed via useStore s.transform[0..2]). A Transform has identical fields to Viewport but is semantically the store representation.
terminal_note: Implemented as a genuine float transform: flow_viewport{ox,oy,zoom} with screen=world*zoom+offset and real-scale node spacing zoom (not just LOD) — the repo proves continuous-scale zoom works in a char grid.
evidence: src/flow_geom.h:5 `typedef struct {float ox,oy,zoom;} flow_viewport`; flow_project/flow_unproject src/flow_geom.h:14-26; f->view in struct flow

name: defaultViewport (uncontrolled initial camera)
description: Sets the initial position and zoom of the viewport. Default {x:0, y:0, zoom:1}. Used when the viewport is uncontrolled.
terminal_note: flow_new initializes view (zoom=1, ox/oy=0); apps may flow_pan/flow_set_zoom at startup. No single defaultViewport arg, but the initial camera state exists and is settable.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:401-403 flow_new sets view defaults (view.zoom=1 path), marquee_mode, autopan; flow_view_get src/flow_model.h:643

name: Controlled viewport (viewport prop + onViewportChange)
description: Passing `viewport` makes the camera controlled; the app owns viewport state and must update it via `onViewportChange: (viewport: Viewport) => void` which fires on every pan/zoom. Mirrors React's controlled-input pattern.
terminal_note: Nearest analog would be an on-viewport-change callback; the engine owns one store and exposes flow_view_get for reads, but no callback fires on camera change.
evidence: flow_callbacks struct src/flow_model.h:181-188 has no viewport/move callback; spec §8 callback list (lines 352-361) omits it; grep 'on_viewport\|onViewportChange' src/ → no hits

name: Drag-to-pan (panOnDrag) incl. mouse-button selection
description: panOnDrag (default true) pans the viewport by clicking/dragging the pane. boolean | number[]: the array form restricts to specific mouse-button indices (0 = left, 1 = middle, 2 = right). E.g. [1,2] = pan only with middle/right drag (the design-tool preset described as 'middle / right mouse drag'), freeing left-drag for selection.
terminal_note: Left-drag on empty pane pans (always-on, not button-array-configurable). Drag threshold tracked manually. No per-button index gating config.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:178-180 sets dragging_pan on empty-pane drag; src/flow_input.h:62 flow_pan applied; drag threshold at src/flow_input.h:166-167

name: Scroll-to-pan (panOnScroll)
description: panOnScroll (default false) pans the viewport when scrolling inside the container instead of zooming. Common in design-tool UX (combined with panOnDrag=false, selectionOnDrag=true).
terminal_note: Wheel buttons 0-3 (SGR 64/65/66/67) pan the viewport by one cell per tick; this is the default and only wheel-without-modifier behavior, matching panOnScroll-on.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:72-78 wheel buttons 0-3 → flow_pan(0,±1)/(±1,0)

name: panOnScrollSpeed
description: Multiplier controlling how fast the viewport pans on scroll. Default 0.5.
terminal_note: Scalar applied to wheel-delta-to-cells conversion; trivial. Wheel pan is currently hardcoded to 1 cell/tick with no speed knob.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:72-78 pans a fixed ±1 per wheel event; grep 'scroll_speed\|panOnScrollSpeed\|wheel.*speed' src/ → no hits

name: PanOnScrollMode (Free/Vertical/Horizontal)
description: Limits scroll-pan direction when panOnScroll is enabled. Enum 'free' (unrestricted, default), 'vertical' (up/down only), 'horizontal' (left/right only).
terminal_note: Axis-locking the pan delta is trivial integer arithmetic, but no axis-lock mode is exposed; all four wheel directions pan freely.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:72-78 all wheel axes pan unrestricted; grep 'PanOnScrollMode\|scroll_mode\|axis' src/ → no hits

name: Key-activated pan (panActivationKeyCode)
description: Hold this key to force drag-to-pan even when panOnDrag is false / in selection mode. Default 'Space'. KeyCode | null.
terminal_note: Shipped: hold-Space forces drag-to-pan over a node OR the pane (space_held state-tracked). The key is fixed to Space, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:92 `if (f->space_held)` forces drag-to-pan; commit c8a518c 'space-pan'; tests/test_space_pan.c

name: Zoom on scroll (zoomOnScroll)
description: Controls whether scrolling/mouse-wheel zooms the viewport. Default true. (Suppressed when panOnScroll is on unless zoomActivationKey held.)
terminal_note: Ctrl+wheel zooms pointer-centered (button 0=in, 1=out). Plain wheel pans (panOnScroll model), so the zoom-modifier convention is used rather than plain-wheel-zoom; this is real-scale float zoom, not just LOD steps.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:66-70 Ctrl+wheel → flow_zoom_in/out pointer-centered; flow_set_zoom src/flow_view.h:17-29

name: Zoom on pinch (zoomOnPinch)
description: Controls whether pinch gesture on a touch screen zooms the viewport. Default true.
terminal_note: No multi-touch pinch in a terminal; nearest analog is +/- keys and Ctrl+wheel zoom, both of which already ship.
evidence: No touch/pinch event source in SGR mouse decode (src/flow_input.h:24-26 decodes button+mods only); spec §16 cell-grid limits

name: Zoom on double-click (zoomOnDoubleClick)
description: Controls whether double-clicking the pane zooms in. Default true.
terminal_note: Double-click detection exists but is wired to fire on_node_dblclick, NOT to zoom. A zoom-on-dblclick step centered on the cursor would be a small addition.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:259-261 dblclick detection fires on_node_dblclick only; no flow_zoom_in call on the dblclick path

name: Zoom activation key (zoomActivationKeyCode)
description: Hold this key to zoom via scroll even when panOnScroll is active (so wheel pans, modifier+wheel zooms). Default 'Meta' on macOS, 'Control' elsewhere. KeyCode | null.
terminal_note: Implemented as the fixed convention Ctrl+wheel=zoom / plain-wheel=pan — exactly the zoomActivationKey idea, but the activation key is hardcoded to Ctrl, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:66 `if (ev->mods & FLOW_MOD_CTRL)` gates wheel-zoom vs wheel-pan

name: minZoom / maxZoom limits
description: Clamp the achievable zoom. Defaults minZoom 0.5, maxZoom 2. Enforced for all zoom interactions and fitView.
terminal_note: f->zmin/zmax clamp every zoom op and fit-view; configurable via flow_set_zoom_limits. Real continuous clamp range, not a discrete LOD index.
evidence: src/flow_view.h:11-16 flow_set_zoom_limits; clamp in flow_set_zoom src/flow_view.h:24-25; flow_fit_view clamps src/flow_model.h:862-868

name: translateExtent (pan boundary)
description: Sets a bounding rectangle limiting how far the viewport can pan. CoordinateExtent [[minX,minY],[maxX,maxY]]. Default [[-∞,-∞],[+∞,+∞]] (unbounded).
terminal_note: Clamp pan offset to a coordinate rectangle — pure integer min/max. Not implemented: flow_pan adds the delta unconditionally, no bounding rect.
evidence: src/flow_model.h:640 flow_pan adds dx/dy with no clamp; grep 'translate_extent\|translateExtent\|pan_extent' src/ → no hits

name: nodeExtent (node-placement boundary)
description: Bounding rectangle constraining where nodes may be placed/dragged on the infinite canvas. CoordinateExtent.
terminal_note: Clamp each node's position to a rectangle on drag — pure math. Spec §9 mentions an OPTIONAL per-group coordinate extent clamping a child within its parent, but it is not implemented; there is no global nodeExtent either.
evidence: flow_move_node src/flow_model.h:192+ applies pos with no extent clamp; spec §9 'Optional coordinate extent' (line 376) unbuilt; grep 'node_extent\|nodeExtent\|extent' src/ → only autopan comment hits

name: preventScrolling
description: Default true; prevents the surrounding page from scrolling when the pointer is over the flow. Disabling lets page scroll through.
terminal_note: Browser page-scroll concept; no analog in a full-screen TUI which owns the whole terminal via alt-screen.
evidence: flow_term.h enters alt-screen (?1049h) owning the whole terminal; no surrounding scroll container exists; grep 'preventScroll' src/ → no hits

name: paneClickDistance
description: Max distance (px) the pointer may move between mousedown and mouseup for the gesture to still count as a pane click (vs a drag/pan). Default 0.
terminal_note: A fixed 1-cell movement threshold disambiguates click from drag (the `moved` flag flips when scr != down_pos), matching paneClickDistance in spirit, but the distance is hardcoded to 1 cell, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:166-167 sets f->moved on first cell of movement; release path at src/flow_input.h:303 'A marqueed drag sets moved==1, so on_pane_click was never fired'

name: fitView (initial fit) + boolean prop
description: fitView prop (boolean): when true, on init the flow is zoomed/panned to fit all initially-provided nodes. Customized by fitViewOptions.
terminal_note: flow_fit_view(f, margin) computes bounds+center+zoom (clamped) and applies it — real continuous zoom, callable at init or via 'f' key. No declarative init-time boolean prop, but the capability is present.
evidence: flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:862-868; bound to 'f' key src/flow_model.h:904; flow_run reads term size then can fit

name: FitViewOptions: padding (number/string-unit/directional)
description: Padding around the fitted content. New 12.x Padding type: a plain number (fraction), a unit string `${number}px` or `${number}%` (PaddingUnit = 'px'|'%'), or a directional object {top?,right?,bottom?,left?,x?,y?} each PaddingWithUnit. (Doc default padding value not stated on the page; examples use 0.1/0.2/0.5.)
terminal_note: flow_fit_view takes an int `margin` of padding cells — a single uniform cell-count margin. No fractional/percent/px-unit or directional (per-side) padding.
evidence: flow_fit_view(flow_t*, int margin) src/flow_model.h:120,862; uniform cell margin only — directional/percent forms absent

name: FitViewOptions: includeHiddenNodes
description: Whether hidden (non-visible) nodes are included when computing the fit bounds. boolean.
terminal_note: Boolean flag in the bounds-accumulation loop. There is no node-hidden concept in the model yet, so the option is moot; flow_bounds counts every node.
evidence: flow_bounds src/flow_model.h:572 unions all nodes; no hidden flag in flow_node (src/flow_model.h:12 flags = SELECTED/DRAGGING/HOVERED); grep 'hidden' src/ → no hits

name: FitViewOptions: minZoom / maxZoom
description: Per-call zoom clamps for the fit, overriding the component-level min/max for this fitView only.
terminal_note: flow_fit_view clamps to the engine-level f->zmin/zmax (settable via flow_set_zoom_limits), but takes no per-call zoom-clamp override argument.
evidence: flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:862-868 clamps to f->zmin/zmax only; signature is (flow_t*, int margin) — no per-call min/max args

name: FitViewOptions: duration
description: Animation duration in ms for the fit transition. When set, the viewport tweens to the fit target instead of snapping.
terminal_note: Camera-tween animation; the spec explicitly cuts eased/animated transitions as a cell-grid limitation (§16, workpackages 'Deferred'). fit_view snaps. Nearest analog: instant snap.
evidence: spec §1 line 32 'Animation smoothness ... out of scope'; workpackages 'Deferred' line 152 'Smooth / eased ... zoom ... cut'; flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:862 snaps instantly

name: FitViewOptions: ease (custom easing fn)
description: Easing function (t:number)=>number applied to the transition interpolation. Default easing not documented on page.
terminal_note: Easing only matters for an animated camera tween, which the spec cuts (§16). No analog without animation.
evidence: spec §16 / workpackages 'Deferred' line 152-154 cut eased zoom/animation; no tween loop in flow_run.h (immediate present)

name: FitViewOptions: interpolate ('smooth' | 'linear')
description: Controls how the viewport is interpolated during a transition: 'smooth' (default-style eased camera path) or 'linear' (constant-rate). Confirmed `interpolate?: "smooth" | "linear"`.
terminal_note: Interpolation strategy only matters for an animated camera, which the spec cuts (§16). No analog.
evidence: spec §16 / workpackages 'Deferred' line 152 cut animated/eased camera; flow_fit_view snaps (src/flow_model.h:862)

name: FitViewOptions: nodes subset
description: Fit to a subset of nodes only: `nodes?: (NodeType | {id:string})[]`. Used heavily for 'focus this node' (fitView({nodes:[{id}], duration})).
terminal_note: Filter bounds accumulation to listed node ids — pure math. flow_fit_view always fits ALL nodes; no subset argument.
evidence: flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:862 uses flow_bounds (all nodes); no id-list parameter; grep 'fit.*nodes\|subset' src/ → no hits

name: Smooth viewport transitions (duration on all camera fns)
description: Smooth transitions are built-in: passing a `duration` option animates the viewport for every camera-altering function — zoomIn, zoomOut, zoomTo, setViewport, fitView, setCenter, fitBounds. Demonstrated with duration:800 buttons.
terminal_note: All camera functions apply instantly; the spec explicitly cuts eased camera tweens as a cell-grid limit (§16). Nearest analog: immediate snap.
evidence: spec §1 line 32 & §16 / workpackages 'Deferred' line 152-154; flow_set_zoom/flow_pan/flow_fit_view all mutate view immediately, no animation loop

name: zoomIn / zoomOut (options?)
description: zoomIn multiplies zoom by 1.2; zoomOut by 1/1.2. options {duration?, ease?, interpolate?}. Return Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: flow_zoom_in/flow_zoom_out multiply/divide by FLOW_ZOOM_STEP pointer-centered, clamped — real continuous-scale zoom. Animation options §16-cut.
evidence: flow_zoom_in/out src/flow_view.h:30-31; bound to +/=/-/_ keys in flow_feed src/flow_run.h:35-36

name: fitView() instance method
description: Imperative fitView taking the full FitViewOptions (padding/includeHiddenNodes/minZoom/maxZoom/duration/ease/interpolate/nodes). Return Promise<boolean>.
terminal_note: flow_fit_view(f, margin) is the runtime-callable fit; it takes only a uniform cell margin, not the full options object (no nodes-subset / per-call zoom clamps / animation).
evidence: flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:120,862; callable at runtime and bound to 'f' src/flow_model.h:904

name: screenToFlowPosition(clientPosition, options?)
description: Converts a screen/client pixel coordinate to a flow-canvas coordinate, accounting for the viewport transform. options {snapToGrid?, snapGrid?}. Returns XYPosition. Heavily used for drop/add-node-at-cursor. (Replaced the deprecated `project`.)
terminal_note: flow_to_world inverse-transforms a terminal cell to world coords ((cell-offset)/zoom, rounded). Grid snap is automatic since world coords are integer cells. No snapToGrid option arg (it's inherent).
evidence: flow_to_world src/flow_model.h:70,642 (flow_unproject); used throughout flow_input.h e.g. :185 marquee, :173 drag-grab

name: flowToScreenPosition(flowPosition)
description: Converts a flow-canvas coordinate to a screen/client pixel coordinate (forward viewport transform). Returns XYPosition.
terminal_note: flow_to_screen forward-transforms world cells to terminal cells (world*zoom+offset, rounded).
evidence: flow_to_screen src/flow_model.h:69,641 (flow_project); used in flow_render.h:203,237

name: getNodesBounds(nodes, params?)
description: Computes the bounding Rect {x,y,width,height} enclosing the given nodes. params {nodeOrigin?:[default [0,0]], nodeLookup?}. nodeOrigin lets bounds be computed from node center ([0.5,0.5]) vs top-left. Formerly getRectOfNodes.
terminal_note: flow_bounds returns the enclosing rect over ALL nodes (top-left origin). No node-subset argument and no nodeOrigin (center) option — both pure-math extensions.
evidence: flow_bounds src/flow_model.h:66,572 unions every node's rect; no subset/origin params

name: getIntersectingNodes(node|rect, partially?, nodes?)
description: Spatial query: returns all nodes intersecting a given node, {id}, or Rect. `partially` false (default) = requires full containment; true = any overlap counts. Optional nodes[] limits the search set. Returns Node[].
terminal_note: The rect-overlap and rect-contains primitives exist (flow_rect_intersects/contains) and are used by marquee select, but there is no public node-intersection QUERY API returning a node list for an arbitrary node/rect.
evidence: flow_rect_intersects src/flow_geom.h:30; flow_select_in_rect src/flow_model.h:702 selects (not queries); no flow_get_intersecting_nodes; grep 'intersecting_nodes' src/ → no hits

name: isNodeIntersecting(node|rect, area, partially?)
description: Spatial query: boolean test of whether a node/{id}/Rect intersects a target Rect `area`. `partially` false (default)=full containment, true=partial overlap. Returns boolean.
terminal_note: The underlying predicate flow_rect_intersects/flow_rect_contains exists, but no node-level isNodeIntersecting(id, rect) convenience API is exposed.
evidence: flow_rect_intersects/contains src/flow_geom.h:27-34 (primitives only); no node-id intersection API; grep 'is_node_intersect' src/ → no hits

name: Rect type (bounds)
description: {x, y, width, height} rectangle in flow space; return type of getNodesBounds and input to getViewportForBounds/fitBounds/intersection queries.
terminal_note: flow_rect{x,y,w,h} is the native 4-int rect used across geom/bounds/hit-test.
evidence: flow_rect src/flow_geom.h:3 `typedef struct {int x,y,w,h;} flow_rect`; returned by flow_bounds src/flow_model.h:572

name: XYPosition type
description: {x, y} point used for node positions, coordinate conversions, and viewport math. All positions stored as x/y objects.
terminal_note: flow_pt{x,y} is the native 2-int (cell) point used for node positions and all projection math.
evidence: flow_pt src/flow_geom.h:2 `typedef struct {int x,y;} flow_pt`; flow_node.pos is flow_pt src/flow_model.h

name: onMove / onMoveStart / onMoveEnd events
description: OnMove = (event: MouseEvent|TouchEvent|null, viewport: Viewport)=>void. onMoveStart fires when pan/zoom begins, onMove continuously during, onMoveEnd when it stops. event is null when the change is programmatic (not direct user input).
terminal_note: Three callbacks around pan/zoom gestures are a clean port, but the callbacks struct has no on_move/on_viewport_change family; pan/zoom mutate the view silently.
evidence: flow_callbacks src/flow_model.h:181-188 lists no move/viewport callback; flow_pan/flow_set_zoom fire nothing; grep 'on_move\|on_pan\|on_zoom' src/ → no hits

name: onPaneScroll event
description: (event?: WheelEvent)=>void — called when the user scrolls the wheel inside the pane (independent of pan/zoom handling).
terminal_note: Wheel events are decoded and drive pan/zoom (SGR buttons 0-3), but there is no separate on_pane_scroll observer callback exposed to the app.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:64-78 consumes wheel internally; flow_callbacks src/flow_model.h:181-188 has no scroll callback; grep 'on_pane_scroll\|on_scroll' src/ → no hits

name: useViewport hook
description: Returns the current {x, y, zoom} and re-subscribes the component to re-render on every viewport change. No params. Must be inside ReactFlow/Provider.
terminal_note: flow_view_get is the C equivalent of reading the live camera; the 're-render on change' is just the per-frame redraw (damage diff) in flow_present.
evidence: flow_view_get src/flow_model.h:643; per-frame compose+diff in flow_present (flow_render.h)

name: useOnViewportChange hook (onStart/onChange/onEnd)
description: Registers lifecycle callbacks {onStart, onChange, onEnd}, each receiving a Viewport, for the pan/zoom gesture phases. Component-scoped alternative to the onMove* props.
terminal_note: Same three-phase model as onMove*; no such registration exists. Apps can poll flow_view_get each frame instead, but no change callback fires.
evidence: flow_callbacks src/flow_model.h:181-188 has no viewport-change family; grep 'viewport_change\|on_viewport' src/ → no hits

name: Direct store transform access (useStore s.transform[2])
description: The viewport is exposed in the internal store as `transform` = [x, y, zoom]; components read transform[2] for live zoom (used by contextual-zoom and viewport-logger panels). Lower-level than useViewport.
terminal_note: The camera is a plain struct field f->view; flow_view_get and flow_zoom expose it directly for selector-style reads — trivial in C.
evidence: flow_view_get src/flow_model.h:643 returns f->view; flow_zoom src/flow_view.h:10; render reads f->view.zoom for LOD (src/flow_render.h:198)

name: SelectionMode (Full / Partial) for box selection
description: Controls box-selection hit test: 'full' (default) selects nodes only if the selection rectangle fully contains them; 'partial' selects nodes the box partially overlaps.
terminal_note: Both modes implemented: FLOW_SELECT_FULL (rect-contains) vs FLOW_SELECT_PARTIAL (rect-intersects); default marquee mode is PARTIAL, settable via flow_set_marquee_mode.
evidence: flow_select_mode enum src/flow_model.h:91; branch src/flow_model.h:712-716; flow_set_marquee_mode src/flow_model.h:723

name: selectionOnDrag (box-select without modifier)
description: Default false; when true, left-drag on the pane draws a selection box without holding selectionKeyCode (design-tool mode, paired with panOnDrag=false).
terminal_note: Marquee currently requires holding Shift; a plain (no-modifier) left-drag on the pane pans instead. No mode flag to swap left-drag to marquee.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:145 marquee is gated on FLOW_MOD_SHIFT; src/flow_input.h:178 plain empty-pane drag → dragging_pan; grep 'selection_on_drag\|selectionOnDrag' src/ → no hits

name: selectionKeyCode
description: Hold this key to draw the box-selection rectangle. Default 'Shift'. KeyCode | null.
terminal_note: Shift-drag on the pane draws the marquee, exactly matching the Shift default. The modifier rides in the SGR mouse encoding. Key is fixed to Shift, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:145 `else if (ev->mods & FLOW_MOD_SHIFT)` arms marquee; mod decode src/flow_input.h:25

name: Design-tool interaction preset
description: Documented preset: panOnScroll=true + selectionOnDrag=true + panOnDrag=false gives Figma-like UX — pan via scroll / middle-right drag / space+drag, zoom via pinch or cmd+scroll, left-drag = selection box.
terminal_note: Several constituent behaviors ship (wheel-pan, space-drag-pan, Ctrl+wheel zoom, shift-drag marquee), but they are NOT bundled as a togglable named preset, and the key piece selectionOnDrag (plain left-drag = box) is absent — plain left-drag pans.
evidence: wheel-pan src/flow_input.h:72; space-pan src/flow_input.h:92; Ctrl+wheel zoom src/flow_input.h:66; but no preset toggle and no plain-left-drag marquee (src/flow_input.h:145 Shift-gated)

name: Contextual zoom / level-of-detail (LOD) pattern
description: Example: a node reads current zoom (useStore zoomSelector = s.transform[2] >= 0.9) and renders full content when zoomed in, a placeholder when zoomed out. Default viewport zoom 1.5. Demonstrates threshold-based detail switching.
terminal_note: This is the canonical TUI zoom model and it ships: the renderer passes zoom+lod in flow_render_ctx; below FLOW_LOD_THRESHOLD (0.6) the default node collapses to a marker, above it draws a full box. Custom types key off ctx.lod.
evidence: flow__lod_for src/flow_model.h:584 (threshold 0.6, src/flow_model.h:8); flow_render_ctx passed src/flow_render.h:208; LOD snapshot tested in tests/test_zoom.c

name: Save & restore viewport (toObject persistence)
description: instance.toObject() serializes {nodes, edges, viewport} to JSON for localStorage; restore reads flow.viewport {x,y,zoom} and applies via setViewport. Viewport persists so users return to the exact camera.
terminal_note: flow_save/flow_load round-trip the full graph plus the viewport (ox/oy/zoom as %.9g floats) — real zoom survives, not just zoom==1.
evidence: flow_save writes viewport src/flow_json.h:46-47; flow_load restores f->view src/flow_json.h:306-312; viewport float round-trip test in tests/test_json.c

name: Context-menu pattern (right-click on node/pane)
description: App-level pattern using onNodeContextMenu (right-click a node) and onPaneClick (close menu); menu position derived from clientX/clientY with edge-aware flipping (200px buffer) to stay on-screen. Offers actions like duplicate/delete.
terminal_note: The library fires on_node_context(node, screen) on right-click and on_pane_click to dismiss; the topo demo uses these to drive a details panel via on_overlay. What is NOT built: edge-aware cell-flip positioning of the menu near the buffer edge.
evidence: on_node_context callback src/flow_model.h:182; fired on right-click src/flow_input.h:82-85; topo demo wires it demos/topo.c:114 `cb.on_node_context = on_context`; on_pane_click src/flow_model.h:184

name: Touch-device support (tap-to-connect, larger handles)
description: Example shows touch UX: tap two handles in a row to connect (instead of drag), enlarged handle hit areas for tappability, connectOnClick toggle. Core pan/zoom on touch via pinch + one-finger pan handled by the framework.
terminal_note: Touch/multi-touch has no terminal analog, but the spinoff idea — click-a-handle-then-click-another to connect — IS shipped as connectOnClick, the keyboard/click two-step connect mode the note anticipated.
evidence: no touch event source (SGR mouse only, src/flow_input.h:24); connectOnClick two-step connect exists per workpackages line 34 & commit 5622b93; tests/test_connect.c

name: ZoomSlider UI widget
description: shadcn-style registry component: a slider to set zoom plus zoom-in/out buttons and fit-view, with horizontal/vertical orientation and Panel position. Uses useReactFlow, useViewport, zoomTo, setViewport, fitView, and useStore for min/max zoom bounds.
terminal_note: A discrete zoom-level stepper widget ([- | level | +]) drawable via the on_overlay hook; the underlying ops (zoom_in/out, set_zoom, fit_view) all exist, but no such widget ships. Keys +/-/f already cover the actions.
evidence: on_overlay hook src/flow_model.h:181 enables overlays; no zoom-slider widget; grep 'slider\|zoom_slider' src/ → no hits

name: ZoomSelect UI widget
description: shadcn-style registry component: a dropdown/select of zoom presets and fit options, positioned via a Panel position prop. Uses useReactFlow/zoomTo/fitView and useViewport to reflect current zoom.
terminal_note: A popup list of zoom presets + 'fit view' drawable via on_overlay; underlying flow_set_zoom/flow_fit_view exist but no such widget ships.
evidence: on_overlay hook src/flow_model.h:181; no preset-select widget; grep 'zoom_select\|preset' src/ → no hits

name: onlyRenderVisibleElements (viewport culling)
description: Default false; when true, only nodes/edges intersecting the current viewport are rendered — a viewport-bounds spatial cull for large graphs.
terminal_note: A rect-overlap cull against the visible cell rect is a natural perf win, but the render loop visits EVERY node each frame; the only clipping present is per-node ancestor-frame clip (groups), not a viewport visibility cull, and offscreen draws are clipped by flow_put bounds rather than skipped.
evidence: render node loop src/flow_render.h:200 `for k < flow_node_count(f)` visits all; flow__node_clip src/flow_render.h:147 is ancestor-frame clip not a viewport cull; grep 'cull\|onlyRender\|visible' src/ → no viewport-cull hits

name: autoPanOnNodeDrag / autoPanSpeed
description: autoPanOnNodeDrag (default true): the viewport auto-pans when the cursor nears the pane edge during a node drag; autoPanSpeed (default 15) sets the pan rate (also used for selection box).
terminal_note: Shipped: flow__autopan pans toward a buffer edge during a node drag, with autopan_margin (band) + autopan_speed (step). A public flow_set_autopan(margin,speed) tuning/disable setter is deferred to the integration pass (fields exist, defaults 3/2).
evidence: flow__autopan src/flow_input.h:58; called on drag motion src/flow_input.h:193; fields+defaults src/flow_model.h:213,403; commit 051cc62; tests/test_autopan.c

name: autoPanOnConnect
description: Default true; viewport auto-pans toward the pane edge while dragging a connection so off-screen targets become reachable.
terminal_note: Shipped: the same flow__autopan fires on the connection-drag motion branch (and reconnect), deliberately including connectOnClick hover, so off-screen targets become reachable.
evidence: flow__autopan called in conn_active branch src/flow_input.h:156 and reconnect branch src/flow_input.h:162; comment src/flow_input.h:151-155; model.h:213 'node/connect/reconnect'

name: autoPanOnNodeFocus
description: Default true; the viewport pans to bring a node into view when it receives (keyboard) focus.
terminal_note: Panning to reveal a focused node's bounds is pure math, but there is no keyboard node-focus / tab-traversal concept in the engine to trigger it.
evidence: no keyboard node-focus concept (selection is mouse-driven; flow_dispatch_key built-ins x/n/f/? have no focus-traverse); grep 'focus' src/ → no hits

name: snapToGrid / snapGrid
description: snapToGrid (boolean): dragged nodes snap to a grid; snapGrid (SnapGrid [x,y]) sets the grid step. Also consumable by screenToFlowPosition via its {snapToGrid, snapGrid} options.
terminal_note: A 1-cell snap is INHERENT: all world positions are integer cells (spec §3 'snap-to-grid is automatic') because flow_pt is int and flow_to_world rounds. A configurable multi-cell snap step (snapGrid [x,y]) is not exposed.
evidence: flow_pt int coords src/flow_geom.h:2; flow_unproject lroundf rounds to a cell src/flow_geom.h:23-24; spec §3 lines 134-136 'Integer positions mean snap-to-grid is automatic'; no configurable step (grep 'snap_grid\|snapGrid' src/ → no hits)

name: Background component
description: Renders a grid backdrop behind the flow, drawn in viewport coordinates so it pans/zooms with the canvas. Three pattern variants. Mounted as a child of <ReactFlow>. Has a corresponding GitHub source under additional-components/Background.
terminal_note: A char-cell grid of background glyphs (dots/lines/cross) drawn under nodes/edges in viewport space is natural in a TUI; draw it during the pane render pass before nodes.
evidence: flow__background composed before edges/nodes via flow_to_world world-alignment (src/flow_render.h:9-27); composed first at src/flow_render.h:168; flow_set_background setter src/flow_model.h:728. Spec §7. Tested test_render.c:73-130.

name: Background variant (dots/lines/cross)
description: BackgroundVariant enum: Dots='dots' (dotted grid), Lines='lines' (horizontal+vertical grid lines), Cross='cross' (small + crosshatch marks at grid intersections). Default BackgroundVariant.Dots. Accepts the enum or the raw string.
terminal_note: Map each variant to a glyph: dots -> '·', lines -> box-drawing '│'/'─', cross -> '+' at intersections. All three render fine in monospace.
evidence: enum FLOW_BG_NONE/DOTS/LINES/CROSS (src/flow_model.h:198); glyphs · (0x00B7), + (0x002B), ┼│─ for lines (src/flow_render.h:16-22). Tested for all three variants test_render.c:73,93,106.

name: Background gap
description: Spacing between repeated pattern units, in flow units. Default 20. Accepts a number or a [x,y] tuple to set horizontal/vertical gap independently.
terminal_note: Maps to grid spacing in cells; only a single scalar gap is supported in code — independent x/y gap is NOT implemented (sweep claim of independent x/y is incorrect for this repo). Sub-cell gaps clamp to >=1 cell.
evidence: flow_set_background takes one `int gap`, applied to both axes via the same modulo (src/flow_render.h:13, src/flow_model.h:728-729); gap clamped to >=1. No [x,y] tuple variant: grep '[x,y]'/gap_x/gap_y src/ → no hits.

name: Background size
description: Radius of each dot or size of each cross mark. Documented to default to 1 (Dots) or 6 (Cross) respectively; ignored entirely for the Lines variant.
terminal_note: Sub-glyph dot radius / mark thickness has no analog at 1-cell resolution; a dot is always exactly one cell. Nearest analog: the fixed glyph choice per variant.
evidence: Each pattern unit is a single fixed glyph at one cell (src/flow_render.h:16-22); no size param. grep 'size' in flow_set_background → no hits.

name: Background lineWidth
description: Stroke thickness used when drawing the pattern lines/marks. Default 1.
terminal_note: Stroke width in pixels has no analog at character-cell resolution; lines are always one cell thick. Nearest analog: none — box-drawing glyphs are 1 cell wide.
evidence: Lines drawn with single box-drawing glyphs │─┼ (src/flow_render.h:18-22); no width param. grep 'lineWidth'/'linewidth' src/ → no hits.

name: Background offset
description: Shifts the whole pattern by an amount; default 0. Accepts a number or [x,y] tuple for independent axis offset.
terminal_note: Maps to a cell-grid phase shift (offset mod gap) on each axis; round to integer cells. Nearest analog: the grid phase is currently locked to world origin (w.x%gap==0).
evidence: Grid phase is fixed to world origin via (w.x % gap)==0 (src/flow_render.h:13); no phase-shift param. grep 'offset' src/flow_render.h (background) → only unrelated handle-offset hits; no background offset.

name: Background color
description: Color of the pattern marks (dots/lines/cross). No default (inherits CSS theme).
terminal_note: Map to an ANSI/256/truecolor foreground for the pattern glyphs; honor terminal color depth. Nearest analog: the hardcoded fg the grid uses today.
evidence: Pattern fg is hardcoded to color 8 (bright black): #define FLOW_BG_GRID_FG 8 (src/flow_render.h:8), passed at src/flow_render.h:25; flow_set_background exposes no color arg (src/flow_model.h:728).

name: Background bgColor
description: Solid backdrop color drawn behind the pattern. No default.
terminal_note: Map to a terminal background color filling the pane cells before the pattern is drawn. Nearest analog: the engine's default FLOW_BG cell background.
evidence: Background cells use the fixed FLOW_BG bg color (src/flow_render.h:25); no per-background bgColor param. grep 'bgColor'/'bg_color' src/ → no hits.

name: Background className / patternClassName / style
description: className applies to the container element; patternClassName applies to the SVG pattern element; style applies inline CSS to the container.
terminal_note: CSS class/inline-style hooks are DOM/CSS-only; the TUI equivalent is the color/glyph props, not arbitrary CSS. No DOM analog exists in a cell grid.
evidence: Pure CSS/DOM concept; library is pure ANSI cells with no class/style system. grep 'className'/'style' src/ → no hits.

name: Multiple layered backgrounds via id
description: Several <Background> components can be stacked to compose patterns (e.g. a fine 10-unit line grid plus a coarse 100-unit accent grid). Each instance MUST be given a unique `id` prop to avoid SVG pattern collisions.
terminal_note: Layering = render multiple grid passes back-to-front in the same buffer; later passes overwrite cells. No SVG pattern id needed, but the layering order semantics carry over.
evidence: Only a single background config is stored: struct { flow_bg_variant variant; int gap; } bg (src/flow_model.h:222) — one variant, one gap, one pass (src/flow_render.h:168). No list/id support.

name: MiniMap component
description: Renders a bird's-eye overview of the whole flow, each node drawn as a small SVG shape, plus a mask rectangle showing where the current viewport sits relative to the full graph. Default position BottomRight. props type exported as MiniMapProps; accepts a generic node-type argument.
terminal_note: A scaled-down overview panel of single-cell node blocks plus a box outline for the viewport is feasible in a TUI, but resolution is coarse; many nodes collapse to the same cell.
evidence: flow__minimap draws a bordered overview box with per-node dots downsampled into the panel plus a viewport rectangle (src/flow_render.h:28-70); flow_set_minimap setter src/flow_model.h:725; composed at src/flow_render.h:266. Spec §7/§14. Tested test_render.c:43-54.

name: MiniMap position
description: Where the minimap is anchored over the pane, a PanelPosition. Default PanelPosition.BottomRight.
terminal_note: Anchor an overlay box to one of the 8 pane corners/edges; identical to Panel positioning in a TUI.
evidence: flow_corner enum supports 4 corners TL/TR/BL/BR (src/flow_model.h:194), switched at src/flow_render.h:32-37; delta: 4 corners not 8 PanelPositions, and no library default (caller passes corner to flow_set_minimap).

name: MiniMap node color/strokeColor/className as function
description: nodeColor (default '#e2e2e2'), nodeStrokeColor (default 'transparent'), nodeClassName (default '') each accept either a string OR a function GetMiniMapNodeAttribute<Node> that receives the Node and returns a per-node value (e.g. color by node.type).
terminal_note: Per-node fill/stroke color via callback maps to a fg/bg color per minimap cell; border (stroke) on a 1-cell block has no analog. className is CSS-only -> drop.
evidence: Minimap node dots use fixed glyphs/colors (• / ◉ by selection, FLOW_FG) with no per-node color callback (src/flow_render.h:61-68). grep 'nodeColor'/'GetMiniMapNodeAttribute' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap nodeBorderRadius / nodeStrokeWidth
description: nodeBorderRadius (default 5) rounds minimap node corners; nodeStrokeWidth (default 2) sets the node border thickness.
terminal_note: Corner radius and sub-cell stroke width are meaningless at 1-cell minimap node resolution. Nearest analog: none — each node is one glyph.
evidence: Minimap nodes are single 1-cell glyphs (src/flow_render.h:68); no radius/stroke concept. grep 'borderRadius'/'strokeWidth' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap custom nodeComponent
description: nodeComponent: a custom ComponentType<MiniMapNodeProps> to render each minimap node; it MUST render an SVG element (e.g. a <circle>). Replaces the default rect rendering.
terminal_note: Analog: a callback returning a glyph/color for a minimap cell instead of an SVG element; arbitrary SVG shapes don't translate but a per-node glyph chooser does.
evidence: Minimap node rendering is fixed (• selected->◉) with no per-node render hook (src/flow_render.h:68). grep 'nodeComponent'/MiniMapNode src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMapNodeProps (custom minimap node props)
description: Props passed to a custom minimap node component: id (string), x (number), y (number), width (number), height (number), borderRadius (number), className (string), color (string), shapeRendering (string), strokeColor (string), strokeWidth (number), style (CSSProperties), selected (boolean), onClick ((event, id)=>void).
terminal_note: The geometry/identity fields (id,x,y,width,height,selected) port to a cell-based renderer; shapeRendering/borderRadius/strokeWidth/className/style are SVG/CSS-only.
evidence: No custom minimap-node prop struct exists; minimap is hardcoded (src/flow_render.h:61-68). grep 'MiniMapNodeProps' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap bgColor
description: Background color of the minimap panel itself. No default.
terminal_note: Map to the minimap overlay's background cell color. Nearest analog: the fixed FLOW_BG used to blank the panel interior.
evidence: Minimap panel interior is blanked with the fixed FLOW_BG color (src/flow_render.h:42-44); no bgColor arg in flow_set_minimap (src/flow_model.h:195). grep 'bgColor' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap mask (color/strokeColor/strokeWidth)
description: The mask is the dimming overlay covering the part of the flow NOT in the current viewport, with a rectangle marking the viewport. maskColor default 'rgba(240,240,240,0.6)'; maskStrokeColor default transparent (outline of the viewport rect); maskStrokeWidth default 1.
terminal_note: Draw the viewport rectangle as a box-drawing outline in the minimap; 'mask dimming' maps to a dim attribute or alt bg color on out-of-view cells. Semi-transparent rgba alpha has no direct analog.
evidence: Viewport rectangle IS drawn: minimap strokes a box-drawing outline (─/│ at 0x2500/0x2502) for the current viewport window (src/flow_render.h:55-60). Delta: rect present; no dimming overlay and no configurable maskColor/maskStrokeColor/maskStrokeWidth (all colors fixed FLOW_FG).

name: MiniMap onClick
description: Callback (event, position: XYPosition) => void fired when the minimap surface is clicked, giving the flow-coordinate position of the click.
terminal_note: Requires mouse reporting (SGR 1006); on click, map the minimap cell back to a flow XY and invoke the callback. Works only in mouse-capable terminals.
evidence: Minimap is render-only; no hit-testing or interaction. grep 'minimap' src/flow_input.h → no hits (exit 1). No onClick wiring.

name: MiniMap onNodeClick
description: Callback (event, node: Node) => void fired when a specific node within the minimap is clicked.
terminal_note: Mouse-only: hit-test the clicked minimap cell against node bounds and fire with that node. Needs mouse reporting.
evidence: No minimap hit-testing exists. grep 'minimap' src/flow_input.h → no hits (exit 1). No onNodeClick wiring.

name: MiniMap pannable
description: When true, dragging inside the minimap pans the main viewport. Default false.
terminal_note: Mouse-drag inside the minimap to move the main viewport; feasible with mouse reporting, or via keyboard focus + arrow keys as a fallback.
evidence: Minimap has no input handling. grep 'pannable'/'minimap' src/flow_input.h → no hits (exit 1).

name: MiniMap zoomable
description: When true, scrolling inside the minimap zooms the main viewport. Default false.
terminal_note: Scroll-wheel inside the minimap zooms; needs mouse wheel reporting. Keyboard +/- when focused is an analog.
evidence: No minimap scroll/zoom interaction. grep 'zoomable'/'minimap' src/flow_input.h → no hits (exit 1).

name: MiniMap inversePan
description: Inverts the panning direction when dragging the minimap viewport. No default (falsy).
terminal_note: A boolean sign flip on the drag-to-pan delta; trivially ports wherever pannable is supported.
evidence: Depends on minimap pan which does not exist. grep 'inversePan' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap zoomStep
description: Step size for zooming in/out via the minimap. Default 10.
terminal_note: A numeric zoom increment per scroll tick; ports as a config value wherever minimap zoom is supported.
evidence: No minimap zoom interaction to configure. grep 'zoomStep' src/ → no hits (engine has a fixed FLOW_ZOOM_STEP for keyboard/wheel zoom, src/flow_model.h:7, unrelated to minimap).

name: MiniMap offsetScale
description: Offsets/pads the viewport indicator within the minimap, acting like padding around the viewport rectangle. Default 5.
terminal_note: A padding-in-cells value around the viewport box in the minimap; round to integer cells.
evidence: Viewport rect is drawn directly from projected world corners with no padding param (src/flow_render.h:49-58). grep 'offsetScale' src/ → no hits.

name: MiniMap ariaLabel
description: Accessible name for the minimap (it has no text for a screen reader). Default 'Mini Map'; can be set to null to omit.
terminal_note: Screen-reader aria labels are DOM-specific; a TUI has no equivalent unless integrating a separate screen-reader bridge. Nearest analog: none in pure ANSI output.
evidence: No accessibility layer; library emits raw ANSI cells. grep 'aria'/'ariaLabel' src/ → no hits.

name: Controls component
description: A panel of viewport control buttons. Mounted as a child of <ReactFlow>; default position BottomLeft. Houses up to four built-in buttons (zoom in, zoom out, fit view, interactivity lock) plus any custom children.
terminal_note: A small overlay button bar is natural in a TUI: render clickable/focusable glyph buttons ([+],[-],[fit],[lock]) anchored to a corner.
evidence: No Controls widget. The underlying actions exist as keys/fns (flow_zoom_in/out src/flow_view.h:3-4, flow_fit_view src/flow_model.h:120) but there is no button-bar component. grep 'control'/'Controls' src/ → only unrelated 'control char' hits.

name: Controls zoom in / zoom out buttons
description: showZoom (default true) toggles the +/- buttons that adjust the viewport zoom by a fixed increment. onZoomIn / onZoomOut callbacks fire alongside the default zoom behavior when each is clicked.
terminal_note: Zoom buttons map to [+]/[-] glyphs (mouse) or keys; underlying zoom is coarse in a TUI (integer cell scaling), but the buttons + callbacks port.
evidence: Zoom actions exist (flow_zoom_in/out bound to +/-/=/_ keys, src/flow_run.h:35-36; wheel src/flow_input.h:68-69) but there are no Controls buttons or onZoomIn/onZoomOut callbacks. grep 'showZoom'/'onZoomIn' src/ → no hits.

name: Controls fit view button
description: showFitView (default true) toggles a button that fits all nodes into the viewport. fitViewOptions (FitViewOptionsBase) customizes the fit behavior (same options as the fitView function). onFitView callback runs when clicked; if omitted the viewport adjusts automatically.
terminal_note: A 'fit all nodes' button mapping to the TUI's fit-view command ports directly; fitViewOptions (padding, minZoom/maxZoom, etc.) carry over as config.
evidence: flow_fit_view(f, margin) exists and is bound to the 'f' key (src/flow_model.h:120,904) but there is no fit-view button component or onFitView callback. grep 'showFitView'/'onFitView' src/ → no hits.

name: Controls interactivity lock toggle
description: showInteractive (default true) toggles a lock button that enables/disables interactivity (node drag, selection, pan/zoom edits). onInteractiveChange(interactiveStatus: boolean) fires when toggled.
terminal_note: A lock/unlock toggle that gates drag/selection/edit input maps to a boolean 'locked' mode with a [lock] glyph; fully portable.
evidence: No interactivity-lock mode or button. grep 'interactive'/'lock' src/ → only comment hits (no locked-mode flag, no onInteractiveChange).

name: Controls position
description: Where the controls panel is anchored on the pane, a PanelPosition. Default BottomLeft.
terminal_note: Anchor the control bar to one of the 8 pane positions, same as Panel.
evidence: No Controls component exists, so no position prop. grep 'Controls'/'control bar' src/ → no hits.

name: Controls orientation
description: Layout direction of the control buttons: 'horizontal' | 'vertical'. Default 'vertical'.
terminal_note: Stack the button glyphs vertically (one per row) or horizontally (in one row); trivial in a cell grid.
evidence: No Controls component exists. grep 'orientation' src/ → no hits.

name: Controls custom children
description: children (ReactNode) lets you add custom content/buttons (typically <ControlButton>) into the controls panel alongside or replacing the defaults.
terminal_note: Allow extra app-defined buttons to be appended to the control bar; ports as a list of custom button descriptors.
evidence: No Controls component to accept children. grep 'ControlButton'/'Controls' src/ → no hits (apps draw arbitrary content via on_overlay instead, src/flow_render.h:267).

name: Controls style / className / aria-label
description: style and className apply to the container; aria-label is the accessible name, default 'React Flow controls'.
terminal_note: CSS style/class and DOM aria labels are DOM-only; TUI styling is via color attributes on the rendered button cells. No DOM analog.
evidence: Pure CSS/DOM/aria concept; no class/style/aria system in a pure-ANSI cell library. grep 'className'/'aria' src/ → no hits.

name: ControlButton component
description: A button styled to match the Controls panel, passed as a child of <Controls> to add custom actions. Accepts any valid HTML <button> attribute (children for icon/text, className, onClick, title/tooltip, plus all ButtonHTMLAttributes).
terminal_note: A custom control-bar button with a glyph label and an on-activate callback ports directly; HTML button attrs collapse to {label, onClick, disabled, tooltip-as-statusline}.
evidence: No button component primitive. grep 'ControlButton' src/ → no hits. (Apps wire actions via flow_bind_key / on_overlay instead, src/flow_model.h:412-area dispatch.)

name: Panel component
description: Positions arbitrary content as a fixed overlay above the viewport (does NOT move with pan/zoom). Used for titles, toolbars, legends. It is the underlying positioning primitive that MiniMap and Controls build on. Accepts standard div HTML attributes.
terminal_note: A fixed overlay box anchored to a pane edge/corner, drawn on top of the flow and unaffected by viewport transform; natural as a TUI overlay region.
evidence: The on_overlay callback is the spec-designated Panel analog (src/flow_model.h:179 'panel content stays app-side, like xyflow <Panel>', spec §14): it hands the app a full-screen, viewport-untransformed flow_surface drawn after minimap/before statusbar (src/flow_render.h:267), tested test_keys.c:174. Delta: a single fixed full-screen overlay-draw hook; the app computes placement (no built-in anchoring).

name: Panel position (8 PanelPositions)
description: position (PanelPosition) default 'top-left'. Full set of anchor values: top-left, top-center, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-center, bottom-right, center-left, center-right.
terminal_note: All 8 anchor points map cleanly to corner/edge-aligned overlay placement in a cell grid (compute top/left from pane bounds + content size).
evidence: The overlay hook (on_overlay) gives the app a full-screen surface with NO anchoring logic — the app positions content itself (src/flow_render.h:267). No PanelPosition enum. grep 'PanelPosition'/'top-center' src/ → no hits. (Minimap's 4-corner flow_corner is the only built-in anchoring, src/flow_model.h:194.)

name: Panel children / className / style
description: children (ReactNode) is the overlay content; className and style style the panel; spreads remaining HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>.
terminal_note: children -> the renderable content of the overlay (ports); className/style/div attrs are CSS/DOM-only and collapse to color attributes.
evidence: The 'children' analog is implemented: the app draws arbitrary overlay content into the flow_surface passed to on_overlay (src/flow_render.h:267; topo demo draws a details panel, demos/topo.c:74-116). className/style are CSS/DOM-only with no analog. Delta: content draws via the surface API (flow_put/flow_text/flow_box), no CSS styling.

name: ViewportPortal component
description: Renders arbitrary content into the SAME coordinate system as nodes and edges, i.e. inside the viewport transform, so the content pans and scales with the flow (unlike Panel, which is fixed). Children are absolutely positioned via flow coordinates (e.g. transform: translate(x,y)). Ideal for annotations/overlays that should track the canvas.
terminal_note: Analog: render custom content at a flow-coordinate position transformed by the current viewport (offset+zoom) so it pans with the canvas. Scaling with zoom is coarse in cells, and content is text/glyphs only.
evidence: No viewport-transformed content hook. on_overlay draws in fixed screen space (src/flow_render.h:267); the only viewport-space drawing is built-in nodes/edges/background. grep 'portal'/'ViewportPortal' src/ → no hits. (An app could call flow_to_screen manually inside on_overlay, but no dedicated portal exists.)

name: ViewportPortal children
description: children (ReactNode), the content to render in flow coordinates; the only prop.
terminal_note: A list of (flow-coordinate, content) items to draw in viewport space; ports as long as content is cell-renderable.
evidence: No ViewportPortal component, hence no children. grep 'portal' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer component
description: Renders draggable resize controls (handles at corners + lines along edges) around a node so it can be resized in all directions. Rendered inside a custom node. Wraps eight NodeResizeControls.
terminal_note: Resize affordances map to corner/edge grab glyphs on a selected node box; dragging adjusts width/height in cells. Needs mouse reporting (or keyboard-driven resize mode) and snaps to integer cells.
evidence: No resize feature. Node w/h are computed by the type's measure() and never user-resized (src/flow_model.h node measure path). grep -i 'resize'/'resizer' src/ → no hits. Not in spec.

name: NodeResizer nodeId
description: nodeId (string): id of the node being resized; usually inferred from context when placed inside a custom node, but can target a specific node.
terminal_note: A node identifier the resize controls bind to; ports directly.
evidence: No resizer to bind. grep -i 'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer min/max width & height
description: minWidth (default 10), minHeight (default 10), maxWidth (default Number.MAX_VALUE), maxHeight (default Number.MAX_VALUE) clamp the resize.
terminal_note: Min/max clamps map to minimum/maximum cell dimensions (e.g. min 1-2 cells); pixel defaults must be reinterpreted as cell counts.
evidence: No resize feature to clamp. grep -i 'minWidth'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer keepAspectRatio
description: keepAspectRatio (boolean, default false): constrains resizing to maintain the node's width:height proportion.
terminal_note: Aspect-ratio lock maps to coupling cell-width and cell-height deltas; coarse because cells are integers, but the constraint logic ports.
evidence: No resize feature. grep 'keepAspectRatio' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer autoScale
description: autoScale (boolean, default true): scales the resize controls with the zoom level so handles keep a consistent on-screen size.
terminal_note: Counter-scaling control size against zoom has no analog: TUI handle glyphs are always exactly one cell regardless of zoom. Nearest analog: none — glyphs are inherently 1 cell.
evidence: No resizer, and counter-scaling sub-cell sizing is impossible at 1-cell resolution. grep 'autoScale' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer isVisible
description: isVisible (boolean, default true): controls whether the resize controls are shown; commonly bound to node.selected so handles appear only when the node is selected.
terminal_note: A boolean gate on rendering the resize glyphs (typically tied to selection); ports directly.
evidence: No resizer to gate. grep -i 'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer shouldResize callback
description: shouldResize((event, params: ResizeParamsWithDirection) => boolean): called to decide whether a given resize should be allowed; return false to veto.
terminal_note: A predicate gating each resize step; pure logic, ports directly (the event object becomes a synthetic drag event).
evidence: No resize feature. grep 'shouldResize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer onResizeStart / onResize / onResizeEnd
description: Lifecycle callbacks: onResizeStart fires when a resize drag begins, onResize fires during dragging, onResizeEnd fires when it completes. Each receives a ResizeDragEvent and ResizeParams.
terminal_note: Start/move/end resize callbacks map to a drag-resize state machine fed by mouse drags or keyboard resize mode; logic ports fully.
evidence: No resize lifecycle. The flow_callbacks struct has no resize hooks (src/flow_model.h:180-190). grep 'onResize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizer styling (color, handle/line class & style)
description: color sets the handle color; handleClassName/handleStyle style the corner handles; lineClassName/lineStyle style the edge lines.
terminal_note: color maps to handle/line glyph color attribute; className/inline CSS hooks are DOM-only and collapse to that single color choice.
evidence: No resizer and no resize-handle styling. grep 'handleClassName'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizeControl component
description: A single resize control giving full control over a custom resize UI (the building block NodeResizer composes 8 of). Rendered inside a custom node; can wrap a custom icon via children. Shares the min/max/keepAspectRatio/autoScale/shouldResize/onResize* props with NodeResizer.
terminal_note: A single grab-affordance (corner glyph or edge line) at one position of a node, draggable to resize; ports as one handle in a TUI resize implementation.
evidence: No resize subsystem. grep 'NodeResizeControl'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizeControl position (ControlPosition / ControlLinePosition)
description: position: where the control sits. A ControlLinePosition (edge: top/right/bottom/left for the line variant) OR a handle corner: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right'.
terminal_note: The 4 corners + 4 edge midpoints of a node box map directly to cell positions for grab handles.
evidence: No resize control. grep 'ControlPosition'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizeControl variant (line vs handle)
description: variant (ResizeControlVariant): 'handle' (default, a small square grab point, typically at corners) or 'line' (a thin draggable line along an edge).
terminal_note: handle -> a corner glyph (e.g. '+'/'o'); line -> drawing the whole node edge as a draggable border. Both representable; the 'line' affordance is the node's border row/column.
evidence: No resize control. grep 'ResizeControlVariant'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizeControl resizeDirection
description: resizeDirection ('horizontal' | 'vertical'): constrains resizing to a single axis; omit to allow all directions.
terminal_note: Axis-lock on a handle (only width or only height changes) ports as a constraint flag in the resize logic.
evidence: No resize control. grep 'resizeDirection' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeResizeControl children / className / style
description: children (ReactNode) renders custom content (e.g. an icon) inside the control; className and style customize it.
terminal_note: children -> a custom glyph for the handle (ports); className/style are DOM/CSS-only.
evidence: No resize control. grep 'NodeResizeControl'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: ResizeParams / ResizeParamsWithDirection
description: ResizeParams describes the resize events emitted by the resizer: x (number), y (number), width (number), height (number). ResizeParamsWithDirection extends it with a direction field (used by shouldResize); the exact direction representation was not captured (type page returned 404).
terminal_note: x/y/width/height in cell units is exactly the resize payload a TUI needs; the direction discriminator ports once its concrete shape is confirmed.
evidence: No resize payload type. grep 'ResizeParams'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: ResizeDragEvent
description: The event object passed to onResizeStart/onResize/onResizeEnd/shouldResize alongside the ResizeParams; represents the underlying pointer drag interaction driving the resize.
terminal_note: Maps to a synthetic drag event (mouse position deltas, or keyboard-resize tick) the TUI constructs; the raw DOM drag event does not port but the abstraction does.
evidence: No resize subsystem. The engine has a flow_input_event mouse abstraction (src/flow_input.h) but no resize-drag event. grep 'ResizeDragEvent'/'resize' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar component
description: Renders a toolbar adjacent to a node, used for contextual actions (cut/copy/paste, change type, etc.). By default visible only when the node is selected, and auto-hides when multiple nodes are selected. Spreads HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>.
terminal_note: A small action-button row floating next to a selected node maps to a TUI overlay anchored to the node box; buttons activate via mouse or keyboard.
evidence: No node toolbar. The only context affordance is on_node_context (right-click) which fires an app callback, not a toolbar (src/flow_model.h:182). grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits. Not in spec.

name: NodeToolbar nodeId (single or multiple)
description: nodeId (string | string[]): which node(s) the toolbar attaches to. Passing an array renders ONE toolbar shared across multiple nodes (positioned relative to their combined bounds).
terminal_note: Bind the toolbar to one node id or a set; for a set, anchor to the bounding box of the group. Pure layout logic, ports.
evidence: No node toolbar to bind. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar isVisible (forced visibility)
description: isVisible (boolean, default false): when true, the toolbar shows regardless of selection state; overrides the default 'visible only when selected' behavior.
terminal_note: A force-show boolean overriding the selection-gated default; ports directly as a render flag.
evidence: No node toolbar. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar position
description: position (Position, default Position.Top): which side of the node the toolbar sits on — Top, Right, Bottom, or Left.
terminal_note: Place the toolbar row above/below or column left/right of the node box; the 4 sides map directly to cell offsets.
evidence: No node toolbar. (flow_pos TOP/RIGHT/BOTTOM/LEFT exists for handles, src/flow_model.h, but not for a toolbar.) grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar offset
description: offset (number, default 10): pixel gap between the node and the toolbar.
terminal_note: Maps to a gap measured in cells between node and toolbar; round the pixel default to a small integer (e.g. 1 cell).
evidence: No node toolbar. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar align
description: align (Align, default 'center'): alignment of the toolbar along its axis — 'start' | 'center' | 'end'.
terminal_note: Start/center/end alignment along the node's edge maps to left/center/right (or top/middle/bottom) anchoring of the toolbar cells.
evidence: No node toolbar. grep -i 'toolbar'/'align' src/ → no hits.

name: NodeToolbar non-scaling content
description: The toolbar explicitly does NOT scale with the viewport zoom, so its content stays a consistent readable size at any zoom level (positioned in flow space but rendered at constant scale).
terminal_note: In a TUI, toolbar glyphs are inherently constant-size (1 cell each) regardless of zoom, so 'non-scaling' is automatic; just position it in flow space.
evidence: No node toolbar exists (the non-scaling property would be automatic if one were added, since glyphs are 1 cell). grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: EdgeToolbar edgeId
description: edgeId (string): the edge the toolbar is attached to (required — 'an edge toolbar must be attached to an edge').
terminal_note: An edge identifier the toolbar binds to; ports directly.
evidence: No edge toolbar to bind. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: EdgeToolbar x / y position
description: x (number) and y (number) give the explicit flow-coordinate position of the toolbar. Note: unlike NodeToolbar, EdgeToolbar takes an absolute x/y rather than a position/offset enum; typically fed the edge center from getBezierPath (e.g. centerX/centerY).
terminal_note: An explicit flow-coordinate anchor (usually the edge midpoint) transformed by the viewport to a cell; ports directly.
evidence: No edge toolbar. (The router does compute out->label_anchor at the edge midpoint for labels, src/flow_route.h, but that is label placement, not a toolbar anchor.) grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: EdgeToolbar isVisible
description: isVisible (boolean, default false): when true the toolbar is shown even if the edge is not selected; by default it appears only when the edge is selected.
terminal_note: A force-show boolean over the selection-gated default; ports as a render flag.
evidence: No edge toolbar. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: EdgeToolbar alignX / alignY
description: alignX ('left' | 'center' | 'right', default 'center') aligns the toolbar horizontally relative to the passed x; alignY ('center' | 'top' | 'bottom', default 'center') aligns it vertically relative to the passed y.
terminal_note: Two independent 3-way alignment anchors around the (x,y) point map directly to cell anchoring (which corner/edge of the toolbar box sits at the anchor).
evidence: No edge toolbar. grep -i 'toolbar'/'alignX' src/ → no hits.

name: Built-in components are children of <ReactFlow>
description: All built-in plugin components (MiniMap, Controls, Background, Panel, and by extension NodeToolbar/NodeResizer/ViewportPortal/EdgeToolbar) are designed to be rendered as children of the <ReactFlow> component so they hook into its context/store.
terminal_note: Equivalent to registering these overlay/plugin widgets against the flow's shared state/context; an architectural convention that ports as a plugin registration model.
evidence: The registration-against-shared-state model exists: widgets are configured on the engine via flow_set_background (src/flow_model.h:728), flow_set_minimap (src/flow_model.h:725), and the overlay/Panel hook via flow_set_callbacks->on_overlay (src/flow_model.h:191, src/flow_render.h:267), all composed in flow_render's z-order (src/flow_render.h:167-272). Delta: 3 fixed built-in widgets, not a general/extensible plugin registry; Controls/NodeToolbar/NodeResizer/EdgeToolbar/ViewportPortal not built.

name: Example: NodeResizer usage patterns
description: The Node Resizer example shows three node types: (1) a basic node with <NodeResizer> always active, (2) a node whose resizer isVisible is bound to selection so handles appear only when selected, and (3) a custom resize UI built with <NodeResizeControl> wrapping a custom handle icon. Demonstrates minWidth/minHeight clamping and selection-tied visibility.
terminal_note: Selection-gated handles and min-size clamps port; the custom-icon handle becomes a custom glyph. autoScale/CSS handle styling do not port.
evidence: Depends on the NodeResizer subsystem which does not exist. grep -i 'resize' src/ → no hits; no resizer demo. Not in spec.

name: Example: NodeToolbar mutating node data via setNodes
description: The Node Toolbar example builds a contextual toolbar with cut/copy/paste buttons plus controls to change the toolbar's own position/align, an isVisible checkbox to force visibility, and shows the app-level pattern of toolbar buttons updating all nodes' data through setNodes (reactive re-render). Reiterates content is not scaled with zoom.
terminal_note: Toolbar buttons triggering state mutations on nodes (the controller pattern) ports directly; constant-size content is automatic in a TUI.
evidence: Depends on the NodeToolbar component which does not exist. The mutate-via-mutator pattern exists generally (direct mutators are the sole write path, spec §4) but no toolbar drives it. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: Example: EdgeToolbar at edge midpoint with delete action
description: The Edge Toolbar example renders toolbar content at the edge midpoint computed via getBezierPath (centerX/centerY), with isVisible tied to edge selection, content kept unscaled, and app-level handlers (e.g. a button that deletes the edge) wired into flow state. Shows two patterns: a single centered button and a floating multi-button toolbar above the edge.
terminal_note: Compute an edge's midpoint, anchor an action overlay there, and wire buttons to edge-mutation commands (delete) — all ports to a TUI with mouse or keyboard activation.
evidence: Depends on the EdgeToolbar component which does not exist. Edge midpoint (out->label_anchor, src/flow_route.h) and edge delete/select exist, but no toolbar overlay anchors there. grep -i 'toolbar' src/ → no hits.

name: nodes (controlled)
description: Array of Node objects rendered in a controlled flow; the parent owns state and must apply changes via onNodesChange. Default [].
terminal_note: Engine owns one node store mutated directly; rendering is a pure read of it (spec §1, §4). The controlled/change-descriptor contract is intentionally collapsed.
evidence: flow_node store + mutators flow_add_node/flow_move_node in src/flow_model.h:32-110; spec §1/§4 'we own one store and mutate it directly'. Engine-owned (not host-array) is the deliberate port.

name: edges (controlled)
description: Array of Edge objects rendered in a controlled flow; parent owns state, applies changes via onEdgesChange. Default [].
terminal_note: Edge array in the engine-owned store; same direct-mutation model as nodes.
evidence: flow_edge in src/flow_model.h:13-14; flow_add_edge/flow_remove_edge mutators src/flow_model.h:104-105; render edge loop src/flow_render.h:159.

name: defaultNodes (uncontrolled)
description: Initial nodes for an uncontrolled flow where React Flow keeps internal node state instead of the parent.
terminal_note: The library is inherently 'uncontrolled' — it owns the node array and the consumer seeds it via flow_add_node calls. No separate default-vs-controlled distinction exists.
evidence: Single engine-owned store seeded by flow_add_node (src/flow_model.h); spec §1 collapses controlled/uncontrolled into one owned store. The library-owned model IS the uncontrolled model.

name: defaultEdges (uncontrolled)
description: Initial edges for an uncontrolled flow; React Flow manages edge state internally.
terminal_note: Library-owned edge array seeded via flow_add_edge; the engine-owned store is the uncontrolled model.
evidence: flow_add_edge (src/flow_model.h:104) populates the internal edges array; spec §1/§4 single owned store.

name: width
description: Sets a fixed pixel width for the flow container.
terminal_note: Maps to the flow region's column count, passed to flow_new(cols,rows) / flow_resize.
evidence: flow_new(int cols,int rows) src/flow_model.h:32, flow_resize src/flow_model.h:34; the cols arg is the fixed cell-column width of the flow region.

name: height
description: Sets a fixed pixel height for the flow container.
terminal_note: Maps to the flow region's row count via flow_new(cols,rows) / flow_resize.
evidence: flow_new(int cols,int rows) src/flow_model.h:32, flow_resize src/flow_model.h:34; rows is the fixed cell-row height.

name: id
description: Identifier for the ReactFlow instance; useful when multiple flows coexist so internal store/DOM scoping is unique.
terminal_note: A flow-instance id; would only matter for multiple concurrent flow widgets, which the single-fullscreen-engine model does not target.
evidence: grep 'instance' / no id field on struct flow (src/flow_model.h:206-221). flow_t is a single engine handle; no instance-id concept exists. Spec never mentions multi-instance scoping.

name: className
description: CSS class applied to the flow container wrapper.
terminal_note: No CSS in a terminal; styling is per-cell fg/bg/attr structs (flow_cell), not class strings.
evidence: flow_cell {ch,fg,bg,attr} src/flow_cell.h; spec §6 'cell compositor' uses 256-color + attr bitflags. No class-string mechanism and none would apply to char cells.

name: style
description: Inline CSS style object applied to the flow container.
terminal_note: Inline CSS does not map to char-cell rendering; nearest analog is the per-cell fg/bg/attr struct.
evidence: flow_cell carries fg/bg/attr per cell (src/flow_cell.h); no CSS-style object exists or is meaningful for a terminal grid.

name: defaultViewport
description: Sets the initial viewport position {x,y} and zoom. Ignored when fitView is enabled.
terminal_note: Viewport is float ox/oy + zoom; can be set directly on f->view, but there is no dedicated initial-viewport constructor arg.
evidence: flow_viewport {float ox,oy,zoom} src/flow_geom.h:5; f->view settable (flow_view_get src/flow_model.h:643; flow_pan/flow_set_zoom in src/flow_view.h). flow_load restores ox/oy/zoom. Initial state is set via these, not a constructor option — minor delta.

name: viewport (controlled)
description: Controlled viewport {x,y,zoom}; when passed, the viewport is parent-controlled and requires onViewportChange to update.
terminal_note: Host can read f->view via flow_view_get and drive pan/zoom via flow_pan/flow_set_zoom, but there is no onViewportChange callback for a controlled mirror.
evidence: flow_view_get src/flow_model.h:643 (getter), flow_pan/flow_set_zoom src/flow_view.h (setters). Full read/write access exists; the React onViewportChange callback has no analog (no on_viewport_change in flow_callbacks src/flow_model.h:181-190) — delta.
status_corrected: candidate — corrected against source: flow_view reads + pan/set_zoom write exist, but no on-viewport-change callback fires (grep on_move/on_viewport src/ → no hits)

name: fitView
description: When true, zooms and pans on init so all initially-provided nodes fit in the viewport.
terminal_note: flow_fit_view computes the node bounding box and sets zoom+pan to fit the cell grid; also bound to the 'f' key.
evidence: flow_fit_view(f,margin) src/flow_model.h:120,864-874 (getViewportForBounds: clamps zoom to [zmin,zmax], centers bounds); built-in 'f' key (handoff #9 facts line 98).

name: minZoom
description: Minimum zoom level the viewport can reach. Default 0.5.
terminal_note: zmin floor on the zoom scalar; configurable via flow_set_zoom_limits (default 0.25).
evidence: f->zmin (default FLOW_ZOOM_MIN 0.25f, src/flow_model.h:5,399); clamp in flow_set_zoom src/flow_view.h:24; configurable via flow_set_zoom_limits src/flow_view.h:11.

name: maxZoom
description: Maximum zoom level the viewport can reach. Default 2.
terminal_note: zmax ceiling on the zoom scalar; configurable via flow_set_zoom_limits (default 4.0).
evidence: f->zmax (default FLOW_ZOOM_MAX 4.0f, src/flow_model.h:6,399); clamp in flow_set_zoom src/flow_view.h:25; flow_set_zoom_limits src/flow_view.h:11.

name: snapToGrid
description: When enabled, node positions snap to a grid while being dragged.
terminal_note: Always-on with a grid step of 1 cell: integer world positions mean snap-to-grid is automatic, never toggled.
evidence: Spec §3: stored positions are integer cells so 'snap-to-grid is automatic'. flow_node.pos is flow_pt{int x,y} (src/flow_model.h); flow_to_world rounds (src/flow_geom.h:20). Equivalent to snapToGrid=true, grid=[1,1].

name: snapGrid
description: Grid dimensions [width, height] (a tuple of two numbers) that nodes snap to when snapToGrid is on.
terminal_note: A configurable [gx,gy] step >1 cell has no implementation; the implicit grid step is fixed at 1 cell.
evidence: grep 'snap'/'grid_step' src/ → only background-grid (bg.gap) and undo hits; no configurable node-snap step. Implicit step is 1 (integer cells); a settable multi-cell snapGrid is absent and unmentioned by spec.

name: onlyRenderVisibleElements
description: When true, only renders nodes/edges currently visible in the viewport (perf optimization). Default false.
