terminal_note: Viewport culling of off-screen nodes/edges; flow_put already physically clips out-of-bounds writes, but there is no early-out skip of off-screen nodes before draw.
evidence: grep 'cull'/'visible'/'offscreen' src/flow_render.h → only handle-visibility (line 218); the node/edge draw loops iterate all and rely on flow_put's per-cell cb-bounds clip. No bounds-based skip; not in spec.

name: translateExtent
description: Restricts viewport pan boundaries to a coordinate rectangle [[minX,minY],[maxX,maxY]] (CoordinateExtent).
terminal_note: Clamp pan offset within a world rect; pure camera arithmetic, but unimplemented and unbounded today.
evidence: grep 'extent'/'translate' src/ → only autopan-overlap comment + fit_view; flow_pan (src/flow_view.h) applies no pan-bound clamp. Spec §3 says origin (0,0) 'unbounded'. Not implemented, not specd.

name: nodeExtent
description: Restricts where nodes may be placed/dragged to a coordinate rectangle (CoordinateExtent).
terminal_note: Clamp node positions to a world rect on move; groups add a per-parent clip frame but there is no global node-extent clamp.
evidence: grep 'extent' src/ → no node-position clamp; flow_move_node (src/flow_model.h:192) stores pos with no extent gate. Groups clip rendering (src/flow_cell.h clip rect) but do not constrain positions. Not specd.

name: panOnDrag
description: Pan the viewport by click-dragging the pane. boolean | number[] (button filter). Default true.
terminal_note: Left-drag on empty pane pans (always on); per-button filtering / disable toggle is not implemented.
evidence: flow_handle_mouse drag-on-empty-pane sets dragging_pan=1 (src/flow_input.h:179-181); decoded via SGR mouse (src/flow_input.h:24-26). No boolean disable or button-array filter — delta vs the configurable prop.

name: panOnScroll
description: When true, scrolling pans the viewport instead of zooming. Default false.
terminal_note: Wheel pans by default (up/down/left/right); this is the engine's chosen default, with Ctrl+wheel reserved for zoom.
evidence: flow_handle_mouse WHEEL branch: bare wheel calls flow_pan in all 4 directions (src/flow_input.h:72-78). Pan-on-scroll is the always-on default; the React prop is a toggle, here it is fixed behavior — delta.

name: panOnScrollMode
description: Restricts scroll-pan direction. PanOnScrollMode enum: 'free','vertical','horizontal'.
terminal_note: Axis lock on scroll-pan; wheel pans freely on both axes with no mode gate.
evidence: flow_input.h:73-78 handles vertical (button 0/1) and horizontal (button 2/3) wheel unconditionally; no axis-lock enum exists. Not in spec.

name: zoomOnScroll
description: When true (default), the mouse wheel zooms the viewport.
terminal_note: Wheel-to-zoom is implemented but gated behind Ctrl (Ctrl+wheel), not the default unmodified wheel (which pans); zoom is discrete per zoom-step.
evidence: flow_input.h:66-71 Ctrl+wheel calls flow_zoom_in/out pointer-centered. Differs from RF default (unmodified wheel zooms) — here unmodified wheel pans, Ctrl+wheel zooms; behavior present, binding differs.

name: zoomOnPinch
description: When true (default), a touch pinch gesture zooms the viewport.
terminal_note: No multi-touch input channel exists in a terminal; the +/- keys and Ctrl+wheel are the zoom analogs.
evidence: No touch input in terminal mouse protocol (src/flow_term.h SGR-1006 reports button/scroll only). Pinch gestures cannot be received.

name: zoomOnDoubleClick
description: When true (default), double-clicking zooms the viewport in.
terminal_note: Double-click is detected (consecutive-click pairing) and fires on_node_dblclick, but no zoom action is wired to it.
evidence: Double-click detection src/flow_input.h:259-261 fires on_node_dblclick only; grep shows no flow_zoom call on dblclick. Zoom-on-dblclick behavior not implemented; not in spec.

name: nodesDraggable
description: Global toggle for whether all nodes can be dragged (overridable per-node). Default true.
terminal_note: Dragging is always enabled; there is no global or per-node draggable gate.
evidence: grep 'draggable' src/ → no hits (only a 'connectable' comment in flow_types.h:43). Node drag in flow_handle_mouse (src/flow_input.h:171-178) is unconditional. Toggle absent, not specd.

name: nodesConnectable
description: Global toggle for whether all nodes can start/accept connections (overridable per-node). Default true.
terminal_note: Connections are governed only by whether a node type declares handles (group has none); there is no runtime connectable toggle.
evidence: Connectability is structural: group type has handle_count==0 (src/flow_types.h:43,46); default has 2 handles. No global/per-node 'connectable' boolean gate exists. Not specd.

name: nodesFocusable
description: Allows Tab/keyboard focus cycling between nodes (focus-then-Enter/Space to select). Default true.
terminal_note: A Tab focus ring over nodes would port, but no keyboard focus traversal exists; selection is mouse-driven only.
evidence: grep 'focus'/'Tab' src/ → no focus-ring; arrows call flow_pan (src/flow_run.h:26-29) not node focus. No Tab handling. Spec §8 keymap lists no focus traversal. Not implemented, not specd.

name: edgesFocusable
description: Allows Tab/keyboard focus cycling between edges. Default true.
terminal_note: Same as nodesFocusable — no keyboard focus ring; edges are selected by mouse click only.
evidence: No Tab/focus handling anywhere; edge selection is via flow_hit_edge + click (src/flow_input.h). No focusable-edge traversal. Not specd.

name: elementsSelectable
description: Global toggle for whether elements (nodes/edges) can be selected by clicking. Default true.
terminal_note: Click-select is always on; no global disable toggle.
evidence: Selection via flow_select_node/flow_select_edge (src/flow_model.h:93-97) is unconditional on click (src/flow_input.h). No 'selectable' gate; not specd.

name: selectNodesOnDrag
description: When true (default), a node becomes selected as soon as it is dragged.
terminal_note: On drag-start an unselected node is selected (replacing selection); an already-selected node keeps the set for group drag. Always-on, not a toggle.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:177-178: on threshold cross, '!(nd->flags & FLOW_SELECTED) → flow_select_node(f,down_node,0)' with explicit 'selectNodesOnDrag' comment.

name: selectionOnDrag
description: When true, dragging on the pane draws a selection box without holding the modifier key. Default false.
terminal_note: Marquee is implemented but only via Shift-drag; there is no mode where a bare pane-drag marquees instead of panning.
evidence: Marquee armed only under FLOW_MOD_SHIFT (src/flow_input.h:145-146); bare pane-drag pans (src/flow_input.h:179-181). The 'no-modifier marquee' variant is absent; not specd.

name: selectionMode
description: How nodes are captured by the selection box. SelectionMode enum: 'full' (fully inside) vs 'partial' (touching).
terminal_note: Both full (rect-contains) and partial (rect-intersects) modes exist; settable via flow_set_marquee_mode (default partial).
evidence: flow_select_mode {FLOW_SELECT_PARTIAL, FLOW_SELECT_FULL} src/flow_model.h:91; flow_select_in_rect honors mode (src/flow_model.h:100); flow_set_marquee_mode src/flow_model.h:101; marquee uses it src/flow_input.h:189.

name: autoPanOnNodeDrag
description: When true (default), the viewport auto-pans while dragging a node near the pane edge.
terminal_note: Implemented in this repo (commit 051cc62 auto-pan); viewport follows a node drag toward the edge.
evidence: flow__autopan in the node-drag branch src/flow_input.h:193 ('pan FIRST, then place'); test_autopan.c present in TESTS. Commit 051cc62.

name: autoPanOnSelection
description: When true (default), the viewport auto-pans while drawing a selection box near the pane edge.
terminal_note: Same auto-pan loop gated to marquee would port, but the marquee branch deliberately does NOT auto-pan today.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:55 comment: 'pane-pan and marquee drags never auto-pan'; the marquee motion branch (src/flow_input.h:183-189) has no flow__autopan call, unlike the connect/reconnect/node-drag branches (156/162/193). Not implemented; spec §8 phrases auto-pan generically but code excludes marquee.

name: autoPanSpeed
description: Speed at which the viewport auto-pans during drag/connect/selection near edges. Default 15.
terminal_note: autopan_speed = cells-per-motion-event step (default 2); field exists but the public flow_set_autopan setter to tune it is deferred to the integration pass.
evidence: f->autopan_speed default 2 set in flow_new src/flow_model.h:403; used in flow__autopan src/flow_input.h:59. No public setter yet (handoff integration list item #4: flow_set_autopan deferred) — value works, tuning knob pending.

name: connectOnClick
description: When true (default), connections can be made by clicking a source handle then a target handle (no drag needed).
terminal_note: Click-handle-then-click-handle is implemented as a first-class path alongside drag-connect; spec §8 calls it the better TUI UX. Always-on, not a toggle.
evidence: flow__resolve_connection_at + connectOnClick press path src/flow_input.h:37-47,89-91; release stays armed for the second click src/flow_input.h:232. Spec §8.

name: connectionMode
description: Connection validity rule. ConnectionMode enum: 'strict' (source->target only) vs 'loose' (any handle).
terminal_note: A two-mode handle-typing predicate would port, but no strict/loose mode exists; validation is structural (self-edge + duplicate) only.
evidence: grep 'connectionMode'/'strict'/'loose' src/ → no hits; flow_add_edge validates only src==dst and duplicate (source,target,handles) (src/flow_model.h:544,747-748). Handle source/target kind is not enforced as a mode. Not specd.

name: connectionRadius
description: Pixel radius around a handle within which a dragged connection will snap/complete. Default 20.
terminal_note: Connection completes on a direct hit-test of the target handle/node cell; there is no configurable snap radius (effectively 0 cells of slop).
evidence: flow__resolve_connection_at hit-tests the exact cell (flow_hit_handle/flow_hit_node, src/flow_input.h:40-45); no radius parameter. Not implemented, not specd.

name: connectionDragThreshold
description: Pixels the mouse must move from a handle before a connection line begins dragging. Default 1.
terminal_note: A connection arms on handle press and tracks immediately; the moved flag is set on the first cell of motion (effectively a 1-cell threshold), not configurable.
evidence: conn_active set on handle press (src/flow_input.h:105); motion sets f->moved when scr differs from down_pos (src/flow_input.h:151). Implicit 1-cell threshold; no configurable parameter — delta.

name: connectionLineType
description: Path style of the in-progress connection preview line. Enum: default/Bezier, straight, step, smoothstep.
terminal_note: The live preview is a fixed dashed rubber-band; bezier/smoothstep are explicitly cut (§1 'collapse to box-drawing'). Configurable preview path style is not offered.
evidence: Preview is a fixed dashed line src/flow_render.h:229-231; no preview-type selector. Bezier/smoothstep cut by spec §1/§16 and workpackages 'Deferred' (line 151). The step/straight value would map but no preview-style prop exists.

name: connectionLineStyle
description: Inline CSS styles applied to the connection preview line.
terminal_note: CSS styling of an SVG line has no analog; the preview glyph/attr is fixed in the compositor.
evidence: Preview drawn with fixed glyph/attr in src/flow_render.h:229-231; no style object. CSS does not apply to char cells.

name: connectionLineComponent
description: Custom React component rendering the connection preview line for full visual control.
terminal_note: A custom preview-draw callback could port (like the node-type render vtable), but no such hook exists; the preview is hardcoded.
evidence: No preview-render callback in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-190) or vtable; preview is hardcoded (src/flow_render.h:229-231). Not implemented, not specd.

name: connectionLineContainerStyle
description: Inline CSS styles applied to the SVG container of the connection line.
terminal_note: Styling an SVG container element has no terminal equivalent.
evidence: No SVG container concept; the compositor is a flat cell grid (src/flow_cell.h). CSS container styling cannot map.

name: isValidConnection
description: Callback that validates whether a proposed new connection is allowed; returns boolean.
terminal_note: A consumer predicate function pointer would port cleanly, but none is offered; validation is the fixed built-in rule set (self-edge + duplicate).
evidence: grep 'isValid'/'valid' src/ → only addEdge's internal self/duplicate rejection (src/flow_model.h:544,747-748); no user-supplied validity callback in flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:181-190). Not specd.

name: defaultEdgeOptions
description: Defaults merged into every new edge (type, animated, hidden, deletable, selectable, focusable, reconnectable, style, markers, zIndex, etc.).
terminal_note: No edge-defaults struct; new edges get type="" (orthogonal) and no label by construction. Per-edge type/label are set after creation, not via a defaults merge.
evidence: flow_add_edge (src/flow_model.h:104) takes only src/dst/handles; edge fields default to zero (type[16]="", label NULL). No defaults-merge mechanism; animated/hidden/zIndex fields don't exist on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13-14). Not specd.

name: defaultMarkerColor
description: Default color for edge arrow/markers. Default '#b1b1b7'.
terminal_note: Arrowhead glyphs are drawn by the router with no configurable color; the marker color is whatever the edge draw uses.
evidence: Arrowheads hardcoded as glyphs in flow_route_orthogonal/straight (src/flow_route.h:60,103) with no color parameter; no marker-color setting. Not in spec.

name: edgesReconnectable
description: Global toggle for whether edge endpoints can be dragged to new handles after creation. Default true.
terminal_note: Endpoint reconnection is implemented and always enabled; no global disable toggle.
evidence: flow_reconnect_edge src/flow_model.h:106; reconnect-drag arm/track/finish in src/flow_input.h:115-130,235-240. Behavior present; the global on/off toggle is absent — delta.

name: reconnectRadius
description: Pixel radius around an edge endpoint within which a reconnection drag is triggered. Default 10.
terminal_note: Reconnect arms when the press lands on an edge endpoint cell; the grab is an exact hit-test, not a configurable radius.
evidence: Reconnect arms on hitting an endpoint cell of the topmost edge (src/flow_input.h:115-127); no radius parameter (exact-cell grab). Behavior present, radius not configurable — delta.

name: nodeTypes
description: Map of custom node type name -> renderer component, augmenting built-in types.
terminal_note: A registry of type-name -> measure/render/save/load function pointers (vtable); the topo demo registers a custom 'device' type.
evidence: flow_node_type vtable + flow_register_node_type (src/flow_types.h:48-49; spec §5); custom 'device' type in demos/topo.c. Function-pointer table ports the registry; React component contract intentionally does not.

name: edgeTypes
description: Map of custom edge type name -> renderer component, augmenting built-in types.
terminal_note: Registry of edge-type -> route() function pointer; default (orthogonal) and straight ship registered.
evidence: flow_edge_type {type, route} + flow_register_edge_type (src/flow_types.h:50-51); flow_edge_type_for resolves by e->type in the render edge loop (src/flow_render.h:159). Spec §5.

name: nodeOrigin
description: Origin used when placing a node at its position. [x,y] in 0..1; [0,0]=top-left (default), [0.5,0.5]=centered.
terminal_note: A fractional anchor offset subtracted from position would port, but node positions are always treated as top-left; no origin field.
evidence: grep 'origin'/'nodeOrigin' src/ → only undo-snapshot/insertion-order hits; flow_node.pos is interpreted as top-left throughout (flow_node_rect_abs src/flow_model.h:710). flow_add_node_center centers a placement but is not a general origin. Not specd.

name: elevateNodesOnSelect
description: When true (default), selected nodes get a raised z-index so they render above others.
terminal_note: Selected nodes draw last within their sibling depth layer (the cell analog of a raised z-index). Always-on, not a toggle.
evidence: Render visits in (depth asc, unselected-before-selected, array index) order so selected siblings draw on top (src/flow_render.h:191-192; spec §6 'Selected items draw last within their layer'). Commit 438ea0d groups depth ordering.

name: elevateEdgesOnSelect
description: When true, selected edges get a raised z-index. Default false.
terminal_note: Edges all draw in one layer beneath nodes; there is no selected-edge draw-order elevation.
evidence: Edge draw loop (src/flow_render.h:159) iterates edges in array order with no selected-last reorder (the selected-last two-pass applies to nodes, src/flow_render.h:191-192). No edge elevation; not specd.

name: zIndexMode
description: Controls how z-index is calculated for nodes/edges (ZIndexMode). 12.x prop governing layering relative to selection/grouping.
terminal_note: The draw order is a fixed depth-then-selected sort (groups, commit 438ea0d); there is no configurable z-index policy enum.
evidence: flow__node_order is a fixed depth-aware ordering (src/flow_render.h:191-192); no policy enum to switch layering computation. Not in spec; layering is hardcoded.

name: deleteKeyCode
description: Key(s) that delete selected nodes/edges. KeyCode | null; default 'Backspace'. null disables.
terminal_note: Delete is bound to the 'x' key and the Delete escape sequence (\x1b[3~); the binding is fixed (overridable via flow_bind_key) rather than a configurable deleteKeyCode prop.
evidence: Built-in 'x' and Delete (\x1b[3~) keys delete selection (handoff #9 facts line 98-99; flow_delete_selection src/flow_model.h:372). Configurable via flow_bind_key (src/flow_model.h:116) but no dedicated deleteKeyCode/null-disable prop — delta.

name: multiSelectionKeyCode
description: Modifier key held to add/remove individual elements from a multi-selection by clicking. Default 'Meta'/'Control'.
terminal_note: Ctrl-click toggles a node in/out of the set and Shift-click adds; modifiers come from the SGR bits. Fixed keys, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:137-142: Ctrl→flow_toggle_node, Shift→additive add; mods from SGR decode (src/flow_input.h:25). Hardcoded Ctrl/Shift; no configurable prop — delta.

name: zoomActivationKeyCode
description: Modifier key that, while held, enables zooming (e.g. zoom-on-scroll gated behind it). Default 'Meta'/'Control'.
terminal_note: Ctrl+wheel is exactly this — wheel-zoom gated behind the Ctrl modifier; fixed to Ctrl, not configurable.
evidence: src/flow_input.h:66-71: 'if (ev->mods & FLOW_MOD_CTRL)' gates wheel-zoom. Behavior matches the prop's default (Ctrl); the activation key is hardcoded — delta.

name: panActivationKeyCode
description: Key that, while held, forces drag-to-pan even over nodes. KeyCode | null; default 'Space'.
terminal_note: Implemented as space-pan (commit c8a518c): Space forces drag-to-pan over a node or pane. A TTY has no key-up, so it is a sticky toggle rather than hold.
evidence: Space toggles f->space_held (src/flow_model.h:906); space-pan forces dragging_pan over node/pane (src/flow_input.h:92,249-250). Commit c8a518c; test_space_pan.c present. Sticky-toggle (no TTY key-up) is the honest adaptation.

name: KeyCode value forms
description: Underlying type for all *KeyCode props: string | string[] (single key or alternatives); combo syntax underspecified.
terminal_note: flow_bind_key takes a single byte-sequence string (up to 7 bytes); there is no string-array of alternatives, and combos rely on the SGR modifier bits.
evidence: flow_bind_key(f, const char *seq, ...) src/flow_model.h:116 — single seq <=7 bytes, longest-first match; no array-of-alternatives form. Modifier combos only via SGR mouse bits (src/flow_input.h:25), not key-combo strings. Partial vs the string|string[] shape.

name: disableKeyboardA11y
description: When true, disables keyboard accessibility (notably arrow-key node movement); Tab/Enter/Space still work. Default false.
terminal_note: There is no arrow-key node movement to disable — arrows pan the viewport — so this toggle has nothing to gate.
evidence: Arrows call flow_pan, not node movement (src/flow_run.h:26-29); no keyboard-a11y node-move feature exists, hence no toggle. Not implemented, not specd.

name: ariaLabelConfig
description: Partial<AriaLabelConfig> customizing all built-in screen-reader text (node/edge descriptions, controls, minimap, handle labels).
terminal_note: A11y label strings serve OS screen-reader APIs a terminal lacks; a label/string table could drive a status line, but no such config exists.
evidence: grep 'aria'/'a11y'/'ariaLabel' src/ → no hits. No label-string table; the statusbar help text is hardcoded (handoff integration item #2). No screen-reader channel; not implemented and not specd as a label table.

name: Keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter/Space/Escape/arrows)
description: Tab cycles focus; Enter/Space selects focused element; Escape clears selection; arrows move selected node (Shift = bigger step).
terminal_note: The keys exist but do different things: arrows PAN the viewport, Space toggles space-pan, lone Esc only cancels an in-flight connection. No Tab focus ring, Enter/Space-select, arrow-move, or Esc-clears-selection.
evidence: arrows→flow_pan (src/flow_run.h:26-29); Space→space_held toggle (src/flow_model.h:906); lone Esc→flow_cancel_connection (src/flow_run.h:39). flow_clear_selection exists (src/flow_model.h:94) but is NOT bound to Esc. The xyflow keyboard-nav model is unbuilt; Esc-clears/exit is a deferred integration item (handoff line 119).

name: ARIA roles/attributes & live region
description: Nodes/edges get role='group' and aria-label/roledescription; an aria-live region announces node movements.
terminal_note: ARIA roles and aria-live regions hook the browser/OS accessibility tree; a terminal has no equivalent assistive-tech surface.
evidence: grep 'aria'/'role' src/ → no hits. No accessibility tree exists for a TTY; spec never targets ARIA. The closest analog (a status-line announce region) is not built.

name: nodeDragThreshold
description: Pixels a node must be dragged before drag events fire (distinguishes click vs drag). Default 1.
terminal_note: A 1-cell move threshold distinguishes click from drag (the moved flag), matching the default 1; not configurable.
evidence: Drag begins only after f->moved is set on first cell of motion (src/flow_input.h:166-167); a no-move release is a click. Implicit 1-cell threshold; no configurable parameter — delta.

name: nodeClickDistance
description: Max mouse movement between mousedown and mouseup that still counts as a node click. Default 0.
terminal_note: A node click fires only on a no-motion release on the node; the tolerance is effectively 0 cells, not configurable.
evidence: on_node_click / dblclick logic keys on the no-move release path (src/flow_input.h:255-263); any motion converts to a drag (moved==1). Fixed 0-tolerance; no configurable parameter — delta.

name: noDragClassName
description: CSS class marking sub-elements inside a node that should not initiate node dragging. Default 'nodrag'.
terminal_note: Nodes are opaque drag targets; there is no per-region no-drag flag on a node's content.
evidence: grep 'nodrag'/'no_drag' src/ → no hits; a press anywhere on a node body arms a drag (src/flow_input.h:171-178). No interactive-subregion escape hatch; not specd.

name: noWheelClassName
description: CSS class marking sub-elements where wheel/zoom-on-scroll should be suppressed. Default 'nowheel'.
terminal_note: Wheel events always go to the viewport; no per-region 'consume-wheel' flag exists for scrollable node content.
evidence: grep 'nowheel'/'no_wheel' src/ → no hits; WHEEL events are handled globally by flow_handle_mouse (src/flow_input.h:65-79). No region-level wheel capture; not specd.

name: noPanClassName
description: CSS class marking sub-elements where click-drag should not pan the viewport. Default 'nopan'.
terminal_note: A per-region 'no-pan' flag on node content has no analog; pan engages only on empty-pane drag anyway.
evidence: grep 'nopan'/'no_pan' src/ → no hits; pan arms only when down_node==-1 (src/flow_input.h:179-181). No content-region pan-suppression flag; not specd.

name: colorMode
description: Theme of the flow. ColorMode enum: 'light' (default), 'dark', 'system'.
terminal_note: Light/dark could map to two terminal palette sets, but no theme/palette switch exists; colors are baked into render code and respect the terminal's own scheme.
evidence: grep 'colorMode'/'theme'/'dark'/'palette' src/ → only a background-grid 'light' comment (src/flow_render.h:5). No theme enum or palette struct. Not specd.

name: proOptions / hideAttribution
description: ProOptions: hideAttribution (removes corner watermark) and account (string).
terminal_note: There is no attribution watermark to hide and no pro-account concept; the whole notion is moot in this library.
evidence: grep 'attribution'/'proOptions'/'account' src/ → no hits. No watermark is rendered, so nothing to suppress; not specd.

name: attributionPosition
description: Corner where the attribution link is shown. PanelPosition enum: top/bottom × left/center/right.
terminal_note: 6-anchor overlay placement could map to corner positioning, but there is no attribution panel and no general Panel-position component.
evidence: grep 'attribution'/'PanelPosition' src/ → no hits. Overlay is a single on_overlay callback (src/flow_model.h:181) with no anchor enum; no attribution. Not specd.

name: debug
description: When true, logs internal debug information to the console. Default false.
terminal_note: A debug-logging toggle routed to stderr/a file would port, but no such flag exists.
evidence: grep 'debug' src/ → no hits; no debug flag on struct flow (src/flow_model.h:206-221). Not implemented, not specd.

name: Required CSS stylesheet import
description: React Flow requires importing dist/style.css; without it the flow is unstyled and nonfunctional.
terminal_note: No external stylesheet; all visual defaults are baked into the compositor's render code (single-header, no deps).
evidence: Spec overview goal 'no external dependencies, no ncurses — pure ANSI escapes'; rendering is self-contained in src/flow_render.h. There is no CSS import step to require.

name: Container sizing requirement
description: The <ReactFlow> parent must have explicit width and height; a zero-size container renders nothing.
terminal_note: The flow region must be allocated a cell rect (cols x rows) via flow_new / flow_resize; a zero-size buffer renders nothing.
evidence: flow_new(int cols,int rows) allocates the cell buffer (src/flow_model.h:397-405); flow_resize src/flow_model.h:34. The cols/rows rect is the required sizing — equivalent to the container width/height.

name: Controlled vs uncontrolled flow model
description: Two state models: controlled (apply onNodesChange/onEdgesChange yourself) vs uncontrolled (library owns state).
terminal_note: The library deliberately collapses both into one engine-owned store mutated through mutators; rendering is a pure read. The controlled change-descriptor returns only as the undo command layer.
evidence: Spec §1: 'In C we own one store and mutate it directly... the change vocabulary returns as the undo/redo command layer (§11)'. Single store in struct flow (src/flow_model.h); undo journal commit src/flow_undo.h. The uncontrolled model is the implemented one.

name: Viewport concept (x,y,zoom)
description: The viewport is the camera: x/y pan offset, zoom scale; node coords are in world space. screen = (world - pan) * zoom.
terminal_note: Core camera model fully ported: screen = round(world*zoom + offset), units are cells.
evidence: flow_viewport {float ox,oy,zoom} src/flow_geom.h:5; flow_project/flow_unproject src/flow_geom.h:14-20; flow_to_screen/flow_to_world. Spec §3/§7. The genuine xyflow transform.

name: Pane concept
description: The pane is the draggable/zoomable surface holding nodes/edges; dragging the pane pans the viewport.
terminal_note: The background drawing surface is the pane; empty-pane drag pans the viewport.
evidence: Empty-pane drag (down_node==-1) sets dragging_pan=1 (src/flow_input.h:179-181); flow_pan moves the camera (src/flow_view.h). Hit order handle→node→edge→pane (spec §8).

name: Built-in node types (default/input/output/group)
description: Four built-in node types: default, input (source only), output (target only), group (container).
terminal_note: default (N/E/S/W handles) and group (container) are implemented; spec §5 deliberately defines only default+group, so input/output are intentionally not ported as distinct types.
evidence: flow_default_node_type + flow_group_node_type registered (src/flow_types.h:27,46,48-49); group via commit 438ea0d. Spec §5 'Built-in types: a default node... and group'. input/output absent by design — partial vs the 4-type set, but the spec's set is complete.

name: Built-in edge types (default/straight/step/smoothstep)
description: Four built-in edge path types: default (bezier), straight, step, smoothstep.
terminal_note: default (orthogonal/step-like) and straight are implemented; bezier/smoothstep are explicitly cut (§1 'collapse to box-drawing corners').
evidence: flow_default_edge_type (orthogonal) + flow_straight_edge_type registered (src/flow_types.h:50-51); straight router src/flow_route.h (commit 498d071). bezier/smoothstep cut by spec §1 and workpackages 'Deferred' (line 151). Two of the meaningful types ship; the box-drawing default fills the step role.

name: Handle concept
description: Handles are connection points on node edges where edges attach; source vs target typing drives connection validity.
terminal_note: Handles are anchor cells on node borders declared by the node type; side/position computed analytically each frame. source/target kind exists on the struct.
evidence: flow_handle {id,kind,pos,along} src/flow_model.h (FLOW_HANDLE_SOURCE/TARGET/BOTH); flow_handle_anchor / flow_hit_handle drive connect (src/flow_input.h:101-105); analytic anchors per spec §5. Default type has 2 handles (src/flow_types.h:27).

name: Interaction Props example (toggle demo)
description: Example app with a panel of checkboxes/dropdown to live-toggle nodesDraggable, nodesConnectable, elementsSelectable, zoomOnScroll, panOnScroll, panOnScrollMode, zoomOnDoubleClick, panOnDrag; logs pane/node clicks.
terminal_note: An options panel wired to interaction toggles is an app-level pattern; the underlying toggles (draggable/connectable/selectable/scroll modes) are mostly not implemented, so the demo could not be built today.
evidence: The toggle props it exercises (nodesDraggable, nodesConnectable, elementsSelectable, panOnScrollMode, zoomOnDoubleClick) are all candidate per this sweep; no such demo exists (demos/ has hello_flow.c, topo.c only). The event-logging callbacks exist (on_node_click/on_pane_click, src/flow_model.h:183-184) but the toggle panel does not. Not in spec.

name: Example gallery categories
description: Examples organized into Nodes, Edges, Interaction, Subflows & Grouping, Layout, Styling, Whiteboard, Misc (save/restore, prevent cycles, validation, undo/redo, dark mode, etc.).
terminal_note: A catalogue of app-level patterns; several underlying features (save/restore, undo/redo, grouping) are implemented, but the demos showcasing them (notably flowchart.c) are pending, and many gallery patterns are web-bound.
evidence: Underlying features exist: serialize (commit f3f87a0), undo-redo (commit 74b6045), groups (commit 438ea0d). But the showcase demo set is incomplete — flowchart.c (groups+layout) is NOT built (handoff line 28, #9 pending) and is in the pending workpackages. Tailwind/Turbo/collaborative are web-bound (na). Mixed; the demo deliverable is specd-but-unlanded.

name: <Handle> component
description: Core connection-point component rendered inside custom nodes to define where edges attach. A built-in node has one source + one target handle; custom nodes may contain arbitrarily many. Rendered as a div (styleable). Wrapping arbitrary children (e.g. an icon) turns any component into a handle by setting handle border/background to none, sizing it, and giving the child pointer-events:none.
terminal_note: A handle is a fixed cell-region on a node edge (glyph + analytic attach-point); the DOM-div / child-wrapping styling model collapses to the spec §5 declared anchor.
evidence: flow_handle struct {id,kind,pos,along} src/flow_model.h:10; declared on flow_node_type.handles/handle_count src/flow_model.h:20; rendered as glyph 0x25C9 ◉ at flow__handle_screen src/flow_render.h:225; default node has 2 handles src/flow_types.h:23-27

name: Handle.id prop
description: `id?: string | null`. Unique identifier for a handle; required when a node has multiple handles of the same type so React Flow (and edge sourceHandle/targetHandle) can disambiguate which handle an edge attaches to.
terminal_note: Plain string key stored on the handle struct; identical in a TUI.
evidence: flow_handle.id[16] src/flow_model.h:10; matched by id in flow__handle_named src/flow_model.h:978-983 and stored on edges as source_handle/target_handle[16] src/flow_model.h:13

name: Handle.type prop (source/target)
description: `type: 'source' | 'target'`. Designates whether connections originate from (source) or terminate at (target) this handle. Drives strict-mode connection rules and default rendering side.
terminal_note: Enum flag (flow_handle_kind) on the handle struct; trivially ported.
evidence: flow_handle_kind {SOURCE,TARGET,BOTH} src/flow_model.h:9; flow_begin_connection requires SOURCE/BOTH src/flow_model.h:987; flow_end_connection picks TARGET/BOTH src/flow_model.h:1012; default handles in=TARGET out=SOURCE src/flow_types.h:23-25

name: Handle.position prop
description: `position: Position` (Left|Top|Right|Bottom). Placement of the handle relative to its node; also influences edge path entry/exit direction. Defaults differ by flow orientation (Right for source in horizontal, Top in vertical). By default a handle sits centered on its side; multiple handles per side need manual offset.
terminal_note: Four edge-sides map to cell coordinates on a box; centering and per-side offsetting are integer column/row math (the `along` field) instead of CSS.
evidence: flow_pos {TOP,RIGHT,BOTTOM,LEFT} src/flow_model.h (flow_geom); flow_handle.pos drives flow_handle_anchor side math + center + `along` offset src/flow_model.h:913-923; default in=LEFT out=RIGHT src/flow_types.h:23-25

name: Handle.isConnectable prop
description: `isConnectable?: boolean` (core prop type is strictly boolean). When false the handle cannot start or end connections. Inherited from node.connectable / ReactFlow nodesConnectable unless overridden.
terminal_note: Boolean gate checked during connect interaction; no rendering dependency.
evidence: No boolean connectable gate exists: grep 'connectable' src/ → only the flow_types.h:43 'unconnectable group' comment. Directional eligibility comes solely from flow_handle_kind (the Handle.type feature), not a separate boolean.

name: Handle.isConnectableStart prop
description: `isConnectableStart?: boolean` (default true). Controls whether a new connection can BEGIN from this handle. Setting false on a target handle (e.g. easy-connect) makes it accept-only.
terminal_note: Boolean checked at drag-start; pure logic.
evidence: No isConnectableStart boolean: grep 'connectable'/'isConnectable' src/ → no hits. Begin is gated only on kind==SOURCE||BOTH (src/flow_model.h:987), which is the Handle.type feature, not a separate start-flag.

name: Handle.isConnectableEnd prop
description: `isConnectableEnd?: boolean` (default true). Controls whether a connection can END (be dropped) on this handle. Setting false makes a handle start-only.
terminal_note: Boolean checked when hovering a drop target; pure logic.
evidence: No isConnectableEnd boolean: grep 'connectable'/'isConnectable' src/ → no hits. End is gated only on kind==TARGET||BOTH (src/flow_model.h:1012), the Handle.type feature, not a separate end-flag.

name: Handle.isValidConnection prop (per-handle)
description: `isValidConnection?: IsValidConnection` = `(edge: Edge | Connection) => boolean`. Called when a connection is dragged TO this handle for custom validation. Docs recommend moving this to the ReactFlow-level prop for performance.
terminal_note: Callback receiving a candidate connection, returns bool to allow/deny. Pure logic, ports directly as a function-pointer.
evidence: grep 'isValid'/'is_valid'/'valid_connection' src/ → no hits. Only structural validation exists in flow_add_edge (self/missing/dup, src/flow_model.h:543-549); no custom-predicate hook on the handle or engine.

name: Handle.onConnect prop
description: `onConnect?: OnConnect` = `(connection: Connection) => void`. Fires when a connection is successfully made involving this handle (handle-scoped variant of the global onConnect).
terminal_note: Event callback; identical in a TUI event loop. Distinct from the global on_connect, which IS implemented.
evidence: Only a GLOBAL on_connect(f,src,dst,user) callback exists (src/flow_model.h:185, fired src/flow_model.h:1016). No per-handle onConnect variant; grep finds no handle-scoped callback field.

name: Handle pass-through HTML/div attributes
description: `...props: Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, 'id'>`. Arbitrary div attributes (style, className, onClick, children, etc.) are spread onto the handle element, enabling custom styling/sizing and custom-component handles.
terminal_note: DOM/CSS escape hatch with no grid analog; nearest equivalent is the fixed glyph + cell attributes the renderer already stamps for handles. Handle geometry/glyph is type-declared, not consumer-spreadable.
evidence: Handles render as a single fixed glyph 0x25C9 with engine-chosen fg/bg/attr (src/flow_render.h:225); no consumer-supplied attribute spread. The HTMLAttributes spread model is a browser artifact.

name: Multiple handles per node
description: A custom node can render many source/target handles, each with a unique id; edges target a specific one via sourceHandle/targetHandle. Default centering means multiple handles on one side require manual offset.
terminal_note: Each handle occupies a distinct cell slot along a node side; offset is integer column/row spacing via the `along` field.
evidence: flow_node_type.handles is an array of arbitrary count (handle_count) src/flow_model.h:20; iterated in flow_hit_handle/render src/flow_model.h:959-963; flow_handle.along offsets multiple handles along one side src/flow_model.h:917-920; test asserts 'along' shifts anchor tests/test_connect.c:28-34

name: Typeless / loose handles
description: Setting ReactFlow connectionMode to Loose lets a handle act for both incoming and outgoing connections (no strict source/target enforcement).
terminal_note: A handle can be declared FLOW_HANDLE_BOTH, dropping the source-vs-target gate for that handle. Per-handle rather than a global mode flag.
evidence: FLOW_HANDLE_BOTH kind src/flow_model.h:9; begin accepts SOURCE||BOTH src/flow_model.h:987; end accepts TARGET||BOTH src/flow_model.h:1012; flow__find_handle accepts kind||BOTH src/flow_render.h:93. A BOTH handle is usable as either end.

name: Dynamic handles requiring internals update
description: When handles are added/removed/repositioned programmatically, you must call updateNodeInternals (from useUpdateNodeInternals) so React Flow recomputes node dimensions and handle bounds. Example: a node that renders a random count of handles, or a rotatable node updating handle angles.
terminal_note: No analog needed: spec §5 states handle anchors are computed analytically every frame, so there is no cached handle-bounds to invalidate. The DOM re-measure step does not exist.
evidence: Spec §5 lines 264-266: 'Handle anchors are computed analytically — no DOM measure ... recomputed every frame, so useUpdateNodeInternals has no analog'; flow_handle_anchor recomputes from flow_node_rect_abs each call src/flow_model.h:913-923

name: Hiding handles correctly
description: To hide a handle use visibility:hidden or opacity:0, NOT display:none — React Flow must still measure the handle's width/height; display:none reports 0x0 and breaks layout/connect math.
terminal_note: The 'must still measure dimensions' concern is purely a DOM artifact; flow handles are analytic (zero-measure). The nearest analog — visibility gating of handle glyphs — already exists via the hover/select/connect render gate, with the anchor always computable.
evidence: Handles only render/hit on hovered/selected/connecting nodes (flow__node_handles_visible src/flow_model.h:951-953; same gate in render src/flow_render.h:218) yet anchors stay analytically computable regardless of visibility — no 0x0-measurement hazard exists.

name: Handle styling state classes (connectingfrom/connectingto/valid)
description: During a drag, a handle gains `.connectingfrom` (connection started here) / `.connectingto` (line currently over it) and `.valid` when the line is over it AND the connection passes validation. Base classes: `.react-flow__handle`, `.react-flow__handle-{top,right,bottom,left}`, `.connectionindicator` (hover/focus styling).
terminal_note: The CSS-class mechanism does not port; state flags map to cell render attributes. The 'connectingfrom' state is realized (source handle bolded mid-connect); 'connectingto'/'valid' validity feedback are absent.
evidence: Source handle is bolded while connecting: bold set iff n->id==conn_node && handle id matches conn_handle, src/flow_render.h:223-224 — the .connectingfrom analog. No .connectingto/.valid styling (no validity predicate exists); base directional classes have no analog.

name: ConnectionMode (Strict / Loose)
description: enum ConnectionMode { Strict='strict', Loose='loose' }. Strict: connections only source→target. Loose: any handle to any handle, including source→source (target→target is NOT supported). ReactFlow-level prop connectionMode.
terminal_note: A single global mode flag toggling the type-compatibility check; pure logic. Distinct from per-handle FLOW_HANDLE_BOTH, which loosens one handle at a time.
evidence: No global connectionMode flag: grep 'ConnectionMode'/'loose'/'strict' src/ → no hits. Looseness is per-handle only (FLOW_HANDLE_BOTH, src/flow_model.h:9), not an engine-wide Strict/Loose toggle.

name: connectOnClick (click/tap-to-connect / easy connect)
description: `connectOnClick?: boolean` (ReactFlow). When true, click/tap a source handle to start a connection then click a target handle to finish (instead of dragging). Drives the touch tap-two-handles workflow. Has companion onClickConnectStart / onClickConnectEnd handlers.
source: ReactFlow prop `connectOnClick`, onClickConnectStart/End — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/react-flow (interaction props, via llms-full.txt)
terminal_note: Two-step state machine (arm on first click, commit on second); natural for a click/keyboard TUI. The onClickConnectStart/End companion callbacks are absent.
evidence: Press while connecting completes via flow__resolve_connection_at src/flow_input.h:89-91; spec §8 line 346 calls it out. Tests: 'connectOnClick armed after first click' / 'completed -> one edge A->B' tests/test_connect.c:114-135

name: connectionRadius (drop snapping)
description: `connectionRadius?: number` (ReactFlow). The radius around a handle within which dropping a connection line snaps to / creates an edge on that handle. Also governs the ConnectionState.isValid 'inside connection radius' check.
source: ReactFlow prop `connectionRadius` — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/react-flow (connection line props, via llms-full.txt)
terminal_note: A cell-distance snap threshold. Today the drop must land exactly on a handle cell or falls back to the node body; no tunable radius exists.
evidence: flow_hit_handle requires exact cell match (s.x==screen.x && s.y==screen.y) src/flow_model.h:963; drop falls back to flow_hit_node body src/flow_input.h:42. grep 'radius' src/ → no hits; no configurable snap distance.

name: nodesConnectable (global connectable toggle)
description: `nodesConnectable?: boolean` (ReactFlow). Controls whether all nodes are connectable; individual nodes override via node.connectable. Feeds the Handle isConnectable default.
terminal_note: Global boolean default with per-node override; pure logic.
evidence: grep 'connectable'/'nodesConnectable' src/ → only the group comment src/flow_types.h:43. No global or per-node connectable flag; connectability is implicit in whether a node type declares handles.

name: isValidConnection (global validation)
description: `isValidConnection?: IsValidConnection` (ReactFlow) = `(edge: Edge | Connection) => boolean`. Validate every prospective connection; return false to reject. Preferred over per-handle isValidConnection for performance. Drives the valid/invalid feedback + connectionStatus.
terminal_note: A single engine-level predicate on the candidate connection; pure logic, ideal for a TUI. Only structural validation (self/missing/dup) exists; no custom-predicate hook.
evidence: grep 'isValid'/'is_valid' src/ → no hits. flow_add_edge enforces only self-edge/missing-endpoint/duplicate rejection (src/flow_model.h:543-549); no consumer-supplied validation callback.

name: Connection type
description: Minimal edge description: `{ source: string; target: string; sourceHandle: string|null; targetHandle: string|null }`. addEdge upgrades a Connection to a full Edge; onConnect receives one.
terminal_note: The four fields exist as parameters/edge-fields, but no standalone Connection record is passed to onConnect (which receives only src,dst ints).
evidence: source/target ids + source_handle[16]/target_handle[16] are flow_edge fields src/flow_model.h:13 and the exact params of flow_add_edge(f,src,dst,sh,th) src/flow_model.h:543. Delta: on_connect passes only (src,dst), not the handle pair (src/flow_model.h:185,1016).

name: ConnectionState (useConnection return)
description: Union of NoConnection (inProgress:false, all fields null) and ConnectionInProgress. Fields: inProgress, isValid (true/false when over a handle or in connectionRadius else null), from/to (XYPosition), fromHandle/toHandle (Handle|null), fromPosition/toPosition (Position), fromNode/toNode (Node|null), pointer (XYPosition). Returned by useConnection.
terminal_note: A transient in-progress-connection record; coords map to cells. Internal in-flight state exists but is not exposed as the rich xyflow record.
evidence: struct flow holds conn_active/conn_node/conn_handle[16]/conn_end (src/flow_model.h:218) tracked across begin/update/end (src/flow_model.h:985-1023). Delta: only flow_connecting() is public (a bool, src/flow_model.h:1023); no isValid/fromHandle/toNode/pointer accessor; not a unified ConnectionState struct.

name: In-progress connection line rendering
description: While dragging out from a handle, React Flow draws a connection line from fromX/fromY to the current pointer (toX/toY), snapping to nearby valid handles. Element class .react-flow__connection / .react-flow__connection-path.
terminal_note: A live preview line between two cell points, drawn with box-drawing chars (no curves). Implemented as a dashed orthogonal rubber-band.
evidence: Dashed orthogonal preview from source anchor to conn_end (free cursor cell), arrowhead-less, every-other-cell dashed: src/flow_render.h:229-246 (gated on f->conn_active)

name: ConnectionLineType enum
description: enum { Bezier='default', Straight='straight', Step='step', SmoothStep='smoothstep', SimpleBezier='simplebezier' }. ReactFlow connectionLineType picks the path shape used for the in-progress line (edge can later be any type).
terminal_note: Straight + step/orthogonal port to box-drawing; bezier/smoothstep/simplebezier curves cannot render in cells (spec §1 non-goal). The preview line shape is hard-wired orthogonal, not a selectable enum.
evidence: Preview always uses flow_route_orthogonal src/flow_render.h:239; no connectionLineType selection (grep 'ConnectionLineType'/'connectionLineType' src/ → no hits). Edge types straight+orthogonal exist (flow_register_defaults src/flow_types.h:50-51) but the in-progress PREVIEW line type is not configurable.

name: connectionLineStyle prop
description: `connectionLineStyle?: CSSProperties` (ReactFlow). Styles applied to the in-progress connection line (e.g. stroke color/width). connectionLineContainerStyle styles its container.
terminal_note: Line color/emphasis could map to cell color attrs; stroke-width and container CSS have no analog. The preview color/dash is hard-coded.
evidence: Preview cells stamped with fixed FLOW_FG/FLOW_BG, attr 0 src/flow_render.h:244; no consumer style hook. grep 'connectionLineStyle' src/ → no hits.

name: Custom ConnectionLineComponent
description: `connectionLineComponent?: ConnectionLineComponent` (ReactFlow) = `ComponentType<ConnectionLineComponentProps>`. Replace the default line with a custom renderer; receives fromNode/fromHandle/fromX/fromY/toX/toY/fromPosition/toPosition/connectionLineType/connectionLineStyle/connectionStatus('valid'|'invalid'|null)/toNode/toHandle/pointer.
terminal_note: Could port as a callback emitting cells given endpoints/status; SVG-path APIs become cell-line generators. No such custom-preview hook exists.
evidence: Preview rendering is hard-coded in flow_render src/flow_render.h:229-246 with no override callback. grep 'ConnectionLineComponent' src/ → no hits; flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:180-190) has no connection-line renderer field.

name: useConnection hook
description: Returns current ConnectionState during an active connection (null-ish fields otherwise). Accepts an optional connectionSelector to subscribe to a slice and avoid re-renders. Used to colorize handles by validity, swap handle rendering mid-drag (easy-connect), or draw multi connection lines.
terminal_note: Read-access to the live connection record; the React selector is a perf optimization with no TUI need. Only a bool (flow_connecting) is exposed today.
evidence: flow_connecting(f) returns 1 while a connection is in flight src/flow_model.h:1023; tested tests/test_connect.c:125-129. Delta: only the boolean is public — no rich ConnectionState accessor (from/to/handles/isValid).

name: useNodeConnections hook
description: Returns NodeConnection[] for a node, optionally filtered by handleType ('source'|'target') and/or handleId. Params (UseNodeConnectionsParams): { id?: string (node id, defaults to NodeIdContext); handleType?; handleId?; onConnect?: (connections)=>void; onDisconnect?: (connections)=>void }. Powers connection-limit gating and live connection counts.
terminal_note: Query the edge index for a node/handle plus connect/disconnect callbacks; pure data/derivation. The consumer can iterate flow_edges() manually but no first-class query helper exists.
evidence: No per-node connection query: grep 'node_connections'/'useNodeConnections' src/ → no hits. flow_edges()/flow_edge_count() expose the raw array (src/flow_model.h:48,46) but there is no filtered-by-handle accessor or connect/disconnect subscription.

name: useHandleConnections hook (deprecated)
description: Deprecated in favor of useNodeConnections. Returns HandleConnection[] for a specific handle. Params: { type:'source'|'target'; id?; nodeId?; onConnect?:(Connection[])=>void; onDisconnect?:(Connection[])=>void }.
terminal_note: Same query scoped to one handle; would port only the successor (useNodeConnections) unless back-compat is wanted. Neither exists.
evidence: grep 'handle_connections'/'useHandleConnections' src/ → no hits. No per-handle connection query helper; only the raw flow_edges() array (src/flow_model.h:48).

name: useUpdateNodeInternals hook
description: Returns updateNodeInternals(id | id[]) which forces React Flow to re-measure a node and reposition its handles after handles are added/removed/moved programmatically. Must be used under ReactFlowProvider/ReactFlow.
terminal_note: No analog: spec §5 states handle anchors are recomputed analytically every frame, so there is no cached handle-bounds to invalidate. Duplicate of the Dynamic-handles concern.
evidence: Spec §5 lines 264-266 explicitly: 'useUpdateNodeInternals has no analog'; anchors recomputed each frame from flow_node_rect_abs (src/flow_model.h:913-923). Node sizing via flow_measure_node is on-demand (src/flow_model.h:448-452), not DOM-deferred.

name: HandleConnection / NodeConnection type
description: Both extend Connection (source,target,sourceHandle,targetHandle) with `edgeId: string`. Returned by useHandleConnections / useNodeConnections so each connection can be traced to its edge.
terminal_note: A Connection plus its edge id; ports verbatim as a struct. The data already lives on flow_edge but there is no dedicated derived type/query returning it.
evidence: flow_edge already carries id+source+target+source_handle+target_handle (src/flow_model.h:13) — the full content of a NodeConnection — but no useNodeConnections-style query returns this shape (grep 'NodeConnection' src/ → no hits).

name: Handle type (geometry record)
description: `{ id?:string|null; nodeId:string; x:number; y:number; position:Position; type:'source'|'target'; width:number; height:number }`. The measured/positioned handle record React Flow tracks per handle (stored in node.internals.handleBounds).
terminal_note: x/y become cell coords; width/height collapse since a handle is one cell. The TUI declares geometry rather than measuring it.
evidence: flow_handle {id[16],kind,pos,along} src/flow_model.h:10 carries id/type(kind)/position(pos); nodeId is the owning node; absolute cell x/y derived analytically by flow_handle_anchor src/flow_model.h:913-923. width/height are 1 cell (single glyph render src/flow_render.h:225); no DOM-measured bounds (spec §5 line 264).

name: NodeHandle type (SSR handle dims)
description: `{ id?; x; y; position; type; width?; height? }`. Used to declare handle positions/dimensions when server-side rendering, since the DOM can't be measured on the server.
terminal_note: A TUI never auto-measures DOM, so ALL handle geometry is declared this way — the natural model for the port.
evidence: Spec §5 makes declared (not measured) handle geometry the only model; flow_handle is exactly that declared record on flow_node_type.handles src/flow_model.h:10,20; default node declares its two handles statically src/flow_types.h:23-26

name: IsValidConnection type
description: `(edge: Edge | Connection) => boolean`. Shared signature for both the global and per-handle validation callbacks; returning false prevents the edge being created.
terminal_note: A predicate function-pointer type; ports directly. Not present since neither global nor per-handle validation callback exists.
evidence: grep 'IsValidConnection'/'isValid'/'is_valid' src/ → no hits. No validation-predicate typedef or field on flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:180-190); only structural checks in flow_add_edge.

name: onConnect event (OnConnect)
description: `onConnect?: OnConnect` = `(connection: Connection) => void`. Fires when a connection line is completed between two handles. Typically wraps addEdge to commit the new edge.
terminal_note: Completion callback delivering the new connection; pure event. Here it delivers src/dst ids (the engine commits the edge itself via flow_add_edge).
evidence: flow_callbacks.on_connect(f,source,target,user) src/flow_model.h:185, fired after flow_add_edge succeeds in flow_end_connection src/flow_model.h:1015-1016; spec §8 line 355. Tested: 'on_connect fired once' src+dst tests/test_connect.c:108-110

name: onConnectStart event (OnConnect­Start)
description: `(event, params: OnConnectStartParams) => void`. Fires when the user starts dragging a connection line. params = `{ nodeId: string|null; handleId: string|null; handleType: HandleType|null }` (confirmed in system/src/types/general.ts). Companion onClickConnectStart for click-to-connect.
terminal_note: A drag/arm-start callback carrying origin node+handle ids; pure event. The connect-start MECHANIC (flow_begin_connection) exists but fires no callback.
evidence: flow_begin_connection runs but emits no event (src/flow_model.h:985-993); grep 'on_connect_start'/'onConnectStart' src/ → no hits. flow_callbacks has only on_connect (completion), not start (src/flow_model.h:180-190).

name: onConnectEnd event (OnConnect­End) + FinalConnectionState
description: `(event, connectionState: FinalConnectionState) => void`. Fires when a connection attempt finishes OR is cancelled — even with no valid target. FinalConnectionState = Omit<ConnectionState,'inProgress'> (confirmed in source), so it exposes isValid, fromNode, fromHandle, to/toNode/toHandle, pointer, etc. Used for add-node-on-edge-drop and temporary edges. Companion onClickConnectEnd.
terminal_note: End/cancel callback delivering the final connection record incl. isValid + origin; the basis for drop-to-empty patterns. The end/cancel MECHANIC exists but fires no callback and exposes no FinalConnectionState.
evidence: flow_end_connection / flow_cancel_connection (src/flow_model.h:1002-1022) emit no event on completion-or-cancel; on success only on_connect fires (no isValid/origin record). grep 'on_connect_end'/'FinalConnectionState' src/ → no hits.

name: onClickConnectStart / onClickConnectEnd events
description: `onClickConnectStart?: OnConnectStart` and `onClickConnectEnd?: OnConnectEnd`. Click/tap-to-connect analogs of onConnectStart/End, fired when connectOnClick drives the interaction.
terminal_note: Same callbacks routed through the click-to-connect state machine; pure events. The connectOnClick mechanic exists but fires no start/end callbacks.
evidence: connectOnClick is implemented (src/flow_input.h:89-91) but emits no click-connect start/end events; grep 'onClickConnect'/'click_connect' src/ → no hits. Only the global on_connect fires on success.

name: addEdge util
description: `addEdge(edgeParams: Edge|Connection, edges: Edge[], options?: { getEdgeId?; onError? }) => Edge[]`. Appends a new edge with validation: refuses invalid edges and duplicates (same source+target and matching sourceHandle/targetHandle), even if the id differs. Standard onConnect body.
terminal_note: A store mutator with dedup/validation rather than a pure array transform (the engine owns one store); getEdgeId is automatic (monotonic int).
evidence: flow_add_edge rejects self-edge (src==dst), missing endpoints, and duplicate (source,target,handles) src/flow_model.h:543-549; mints a monotonic id (nexteid). Spec §4 line 214 ports addEdge validation. Tested: 'duplicate A->B rejected' tests/test_connect.c:179

name: reconnectEdge util
description: `reconnectEdge(oldEdge, newConnection, edges, options?: { shouldReplaceId?; getEdgeId?; onError? }) => Edge[]`. Finds the edge by id and rewrites its source/target/handles to the new connection; shouldReplaceId controls whether the edge id is regenerated. Used in onReconnect.
terminal_note: A store mutator updating one edge's endpoint/handle, with re-validation; ports directly. Edge id is preserved (no shouldReplaceId knob).
evidence: flow_reconnect_edge(f,edge,endpoint_node,handle,which) repoints source(0)/target(1) endpoint + handle, revalidated like flow_add_edge (rejects self + duplicate), edge unchanged on rejection src/flow_model.h:739-765

name: onReconnect event (OnReconnect)
description: `onReconnect?: OnReconnect` = `(oldEdge: Edge, newConnection: Connection) => void`. Fires after the user drags an existing reconnectable edge's source/target onto a new handle; fires even if the endpoints don't change. Typically wraps reconnectEdge.
source: type OnReconnect + ReactFlow edge events — https://reactflow.dev/api-reference/types/on-connect (OnReconnect, via llms-full.txt)
terminal_note: Endpoint-drag commit callback; pure event. The reconnect drag/commit mechanic exists but fires no callback.
evidence: Reconnect drag commits via flow_reconnect_edge (src/flow_input.h:235-238) with no event; grep 'on_reconnect'/'onReconnect' src/ → no hits. flow_callbacks (src/flow_model.h:180-190) has no reconnect event.

name: onReconnectStart / onReconnectEnd events
description: onReconnectStart: `(event, edge, handleType) => void` fired when the user begins dragging an editable edge end. onReconnectEnd: `(event, edge, handleType, connectionState: FinalConnectionState) => void` fired on release even if no change occurred.
terminal_note: Begin/end callbacks for the edge-endpoint drag; pure events. The endpoint-drag begin/release mechanic exists but emits no callbacks.
evidence: Endpoint-drag arm (src/flow_input.h:126-131) and release (src/flow_input.h:235-242) run with no events; grep 'on_reconnect_start'/'onReconnectStart' src/ → no hits.

name: edgesReconnectable + Edge.reconnectable + reconnectRadius
description: `edgesReconnectable?: boolean` (ReactFlow) globally allows dragging an existing edge's source/target to a new handle when an onReconnect handler exists; individual edges override via `reconnectable?: boolean | HandleType` ('source'|'target' limits which end is draggable). `reconnectRadius?: number` is the pixel radius around an edge end that can trigger reconnection. Edge gains `.react-flow__edge.updating` class during reconnect.
terminal_note: Reconnection drag works for all edges unconditionally; there is no global/per-edge enable gate, no which-end limiter, no tunable radius (the endpoint cell must be hit exactly), and no .updating render-state.
evidence: Reconnect is always available — armed when the press lands exactly on an endpoint cell (src/flow_input.h:118-132), tol=1 (src/flow_input.h:118). grep 'reconnectable'/'reconnectRadius' src/ → no hits; no flag/limiter/radius config, and no edge.reconnectable field on flow_edge (src/flow_model.h:13).

name: Pattern: connection validation (isValidConnection)
description: Example wiring isValidConnection on ReactFlow (e.g. only allow edges whose target is node 'B'); rejected drops show invalid feedback via the connectingfrom/connectingto/valid handle classes and connectionStatus='invalid'.
terminal_note: Predicate + state-flag feedback; logic ports fully, feedback becomes cell-styling. Blocked on a custom-validation hook, which is absent.
evidence: Depends on isValidConnection (candidate) — grep 'isValid' src/ → no hits. Only structural validation in flow_add_edge (src/flow_model.h:543-549); no custom-predicate path and no .valid/.invalid feedback styling.

name: Pattern: prevent cycles
description: isValidConnection runs a DFS using getOutgoers over current nodes/edges: reject if target===source or if following outgoers from the target reaches the source (would form a cycle).
terminal_note: An edge-graph DFS over adjacency; pure algorithm, ideal for a C port. The existing ancestor check guards PARENT cycles only, not edge cycles.
evidence: flow_is_ancestor (src/flow_model.h:480-487) guards only parent-chain cycles in flow_set_parent (src/flow_model.h:494); flow_add_edge has NO edge-cycle/outgoers DFS (only self/missing/dup, src/flow_model.h:543-549). grep 'getOutgoers'/'cycle' src/ → only the parent-cycle comments.

name: Pattern: connection limit per handle
description: A custom handle wrapper calls useNodeConnections({handleType}) and sets isConnectable={connections.length < max}. The wrapper's own prop accepts a boolean, a number (max connections), or a callback returning boolean — note this flexibility is the wrapper's API, not the core Handle.isConnectable (which is strictly boolean).
terminal_note: Count current connections for a handle and gate connecting; pure logic. Blocked on both useNodeConnections and a connectable gate, neither of which exists.
evidence: Requires useNodeConnections (candidate) + an isConnectable gate (candidate) — grep 'connectable'/'useNodeConnections' src/ → no hits. No per-handle connection count or eligibility gate; connecting is allowed whenever the kind permits (src/flow_model.h:987).

name: Pattern: easy connect (whole node as handle)
description: A large handle covers the whole node so the entire node acts as a connection target; useConnection().inProgress conditionally renders the source handle (hidden during drag) and a target handle with isConnectableStart={false}; pairs with a floating edge + custom straight connection line. Requires separate drag-handle to still move the node.
terminal_note: Treating the node BODY as a drop target is natural and is realized; the oversized-handle visual trick, mid-drag handle hiding, and floating-edge variant are not.
evidence: Dropping on a node body (not just a handle) completes a connection: flow__resolve_connection_at falls back to flow_hit_node (src/flow_input.h:42), and flow_end_connection auto-picks the first TARGET/BOTH handle when none is named (src/flow_model.h:1008-1013). Delta: no whole-node-as-source, no isConnectableStart hiding, no floating edge.

name: Pattern: proximity connect
description: onNodeDrag scans nodeLookup for the nearest node within MIN_DISTANCE (euclidean over positionAbsolute), creates a temporary edge (className 'temp', source/target inferred by relative x) and shows it live; onNodeDragStop commits the temp edge as permanent (or discards it). Dedupes against existing edges.
terminal_note: Nearest-neighbor scan + temp-edge preview ports as logic; distance is cell-space and the preview uses ASCII line chars. Not built.
evidence: grep 'proximity'/'MIN_DISTANCE'/'temp' src/ → no hits. No nearest-node-during-drag scan or temporary-edge commit; the drag path only moves/reparents nodes (src/flow_input.h:190-301).

name: Pattern: add node on edge drop
description: onConnectStart records the origin; onConnectEnd checks connectionState.isValid===false (dropped on empty pane) and, using connectionState.fromNode.id + screenToFlowPosition(event coords), creates a new node at the drop point plus an edge from origin to it.
terminal_note: Drop-on-empty creates node+edge; screen→flow becomes screen-cell→world conversion (flow_to_world exists). Blocked on onConnectStart/onConnectEnd + FinalConnectionState, which are absent.
evidence: Requires onConnectStart/onConnectEnd with isValid+origin (both candidate) — grep 'on_connect_start'/'on_connect_end' src/ → no hits. On empty-pane drop flow__resolve_connection_at just cancels (src/flow_input.h:46-47); no node-creation hook. flow_to_world exists (src/flow_model.h:642) but is unused for this pattern.

name: Pattern: temporary / incomplete edges
description: Releasing a connection line on empty space creates a 'ghost' node at the release point and a temporary edge to it; because the edge is reconnectable, the user can later pick it up and complete it, removing the ghost node. Built on onConnectStart/onConnectEnd + reconnect handlers.
terminal_note: Half-connected edge anchored to a placeholder node, completable later; all state/logic. Blocked on the connect-lifecycle events; not built.
evidence: Requires onConnectStart/onConnectEnd + onReconnect (all candidate) — none exist (grep 'on_connect_start'/'on_reconnect' src/ → no hits). Empty-pane connection drop cancels (src/flow_input.h:47); no ghost-node/temp-edge logic.

name: Pattern: multi connection line (drag many at once)
description: A custom ConnectionLineComponent reads useNodes()/selected nodes and draws a line from every selected node's source handle to the pointer; onConnect must reduce over all selected source nodes (filter source||selected) and addEdge for each, else only one edge is created.
terminal_note: Fan-out preview + multi-edge commit is pure logic; rendering N preview lines uses ASCII chars. Not built — connection is single-source.
evidence: Requires a custom ConnectionLineComponent (candidate) + multi-source connect. Connection state is a single conn_node (src/flow_model.h:218); preview draws one line from one source (src/flow_render.h:231-246). No multi-source fan-out.

name: Pattern: connection event lifecycle ordering
description: Demonstrates the firing order of onConnectStart → onConnect → onConnectEnd for new connections, and onReconnectStart → onReconnect → onReconnectEnd for reconnects; onConnectEnd/onReconnectEnd fire regardless of success so you can branch on connectionState.isValid.
terminal_note: Defines the connect/reconnect event sequence to replicate; pure events. Only the single global on_connect (success) fires; start/end/reconnect events are absent so the ordering cannot be observed.
evidence: Only on_connect (completion) exists (src/flow_model.h:185,1016); onConnectStart/End/onReconnectStart/Reconnect/End are all candidate (grep 'on_connect_start'/'on_connect_end'/'on_reconnect' src/ → no hits). No lifecycle sequence to order.

name: UI: BaseHandle component
description: shadcn-style wrapper that just re-exports HandleProps and applies shared base styling (size, rounded, border, dark-mode) so all handles in an app look consistent; renders children. Foundation for ButtonHandle/LabeledHandle.
terminal_note: A theming convenience wrapper; the 'shared style' idea maps to a default handle glyph/color, but the CSS/shadcn machinery does not port.
evidence: Handles already share a single default glyph 0x25C9/color (src/flow_render.h:225) — the only 'consistent base style' a grid supports. The shadcn/HTMLProps wrapper machinery is a browser/React artifact with no port; grep 'BaseHandle' src/ → no hits.

name: UI: ButtonHandle component
description: BaseHandle plus a small button rendered alongside it (offset per Position via translate classes); uses useConnection(selector=>inProgress) to hide the button while a connection is in progress; button area uses nodrag/nopan + pointer-events-auto so clicks don't pan/drag. Common 'add node from handle' affordance.
terminal_note: A clickable affordance next to a handle could be an adjacent interactive cell/glyph; the per-Position CSS offsets become integer placement, pointer-events become hit-test exclusions. Not built (no clickable per-handle affordance).
evidence: grep 'ButtonHandle' src/ → no hits. No per-handle adjacent interactive affordance; handle interaction is limited to begin-connection on press (src/flow_input.h:103-105). A consumer custom node could draw one but the engine offers no helper.

name: UI: LabeledHandle component
description: Wraps BaseHandle in a flex row/col (direction chosen by Position) with a text <label> beside the handle (title prop), with handleClassName/labelClassName overrides; for ports/IO rows on a node.
terminal_note: A handle glyph plus an adjacent text label, side chosen by Position — straightforward in a TUI. Not built as a helper; a custom node renderer could draw the label itself, but handles carry no title.
evidence: grep 'LabeledHandle'/'title' src/ → no hits. flow_handle has no label/title field (src/flow_model.h:10) and handles render as a bare glyph (src/flow_render.h:225); no engine-provided handle-with-label. (Edge labels exist, but that is a separate feature.)
